library(admiral)

# Create outputs directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#---------------------------
# Helper functions
#---------------------------

read_single_column_tsv <- function(path, colname) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop(sprintf("Input file not found: %s", path))
  }
  df <- tryCatch(
    read.delim(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", quote = "", check.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop(sprintf("Error reading %s: %s", path, e$message))
  )
  if (!colname %in% names(df)) {
    stop(sprintf("Expected column '%s' in %s, found: %s",
                 colname, path, paste(names(df), collapse = ", ")))
  }
  df[[colname]]
}

validate_equal_length <- function(...) {
  lengths_vec <- vapply(list(...), length, integer(1))
  if (length(unique(lengths_vec)) != 1L) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Input vectors must have equal length. Lengths: %s",
      paste(lengths_vec, collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
}

#---------------------------
# Read inputs
#---------------------------

xxtpt_path <- file.path("inputs", "xxtpt.tsv")
result_path <- file.path("inputs", "result.tsv")
na_idx_path <- file.path("inputs", "na_idx.tsv")
treat_dur_path <- file.path("inputs", "treatment_duration.tsv")

xxtpt_raw <- read_single_column_tsv(xxtpt_path, "x")
result_raw <- read_single_column_tsv(result_path, "result")
na_idx_raw <- read_single_column_tsv(na_idx_path, "x")
treat_dur_raw <- read_single_column_tsv(treat_dur_path, "treatment_duration")

#---------------------------
# Basic cleaning / coercion
#---------------------------

xxtpt <- trimws(as.character(xxtpt_raw))

# Coerce result to numeric, warn on coercion issues
result <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(result_raw))
if (any(is.na(result) & !is.na(result_raw))) {
  warning("Some 'result' values could not be coerced to numeric and were set to NA.")
}

# na_idx: logical vector. Interpret non-empty, non-NA, non-"FALSE"/"0" as TRUE.
na_idx_char <- as.character(na_idx_raw)
na_idx <- !is.na(na_idx_char) &
  na_idx_char != "" &
  !tolower(na_idx_char) %in% c("false", "0", "no", "n")

# treatment_duration: numeric, non-negative
treatment_duration <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(treat_dur_raw))
if (any(is.na(treatment_duration) & !is.na(treat_dur_raw))) {
  warning("Some 'treatment_duration' values could not be coerced to numeric and were set to NA.")
}
if (any(treatment_duration < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  stop("treatment_duration must be non-negative.")
}

#---------------------------
# Validate vector lengths
#---------------------------

validate_equal_length(xxtpt, result, na_idx, treatment_duration)

#---------------------------
# Apply conversion
#---------------------------

result_conv <- tryCatch(
  convert_min_pre_eot(
    xxtpt = xxtpt,
    result = result,
    na_idx = na_idx,
    treatment_duration = treatment_duration
  ),
  error = function(e) {
    stop(sprintf("Error in convert_min_pre_eot: %s", e$message))
  }
)

#---------------------------
# Basic post-checks
#---------------------------

if (length(result_conv) != length(result)) {
  stop("Output length from convert_min_pre_eot does not match input length.")
}

# Identify unexpected patterns that remain NA where xxtpt is not NA
remaining_na <- is.na(result_conv) & !is.na(xxtpt) & !na_idx
if (any(remaining_na)) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "There are %d positions where xxtpt is not NA and result_conv is NA. ",
    sum(remaining_na)
  ))
}

#---------------------------
# Save outputs
#---------------------------

out_df <- data.frame(
  xxtpt = xxtpt,
  result_before = result,
  result_after = result_conv,
  na_idx = na_idx,
  treatment_duration = treatment_duration,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

csv_out_path <- file.path("outputs", "converted_min_pre_eot.csv")
rds_out_path <- file.path("outputs", "converted_min_pre_eot.rds")

write.csv(out_df, csv_out_path, row.names = FALSE)
saveRDS(result_conv, rds_out_path)

# Confirm files written
if (!file.exists(csv_out_path)) {
  stop(sprintf("CSV output file was not written: %s", csv_out_path))
}
if (!file.exists(rds_out_path)) {
  stop(sprintf("RDS output file was not written: %s", rds_out_path))
)

# Optional: simple structural check of RDS
test_obj <- tryCatch(readRDS(rds_out_path), error = function(e) e)
if (inherits(test_obj, "error")) {
  stop(sprintf("Error reading back RDS output: %s", test_obj$message))
}
if (!is.numeric(test_obj)) {
  stop("RDS output is not numeric as expected.")
}

The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/xxtpt.tsv, inputs/result.tsv, inputs/na_idx.tsv, inputs/treatment_duration.tsv, inputs/range_method.tsv). Converts time range patterns relative to end of infusion/treatment to numeric hours using specified range method, adding the treatment duration. Handles both short form (EOI/EOT) and long form (END OF INFUSION/TREATMENT), as well as POST INFUSION patterns (with or without hyphens). Additional details: Recognizes and converts the following range patterns: * "0-4H AFTER EOI" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "0-4H POST EOI" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "0-4H EOI" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "0-4H AFTER EOT" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "0-4H EOT" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "4-8H AFTER END OF INFUSION" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "4-8H AFTER END OF TREATMENT" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "4-8H POST INFUSION" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "4-8H POST INF" -> treatment_duration + range_value * "4-8H POST-INF" -> treatment_duration + range_value With midpoint method, "0-4H AFTER EOI" with treatment_duration=1 gives 1 + 2 = 3. With midpoint method, "4-8H AFTER END OF INFUSION" with treatment_duration=1 gives 1 + 6 = 7. Note: Consistent with non-range "24 HR POST INF" behavior which adds treatment_duration. Only updates result for positions where result is currently NA and xxtpt is not NA. library(admiral)

```r
# Create outputs directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# Helper: safe file check
#-----------------------------
check_input_file <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop(sprintf("Input file not found: %s", path))
  }
}

#-----------------------------
# Read and validate inputs
#-----------------------------
xxtpt_path  <- file.path("inputs", "xxtpt.tsv")
result_path <- file.path("inputs", "result.tsv")
naidx_path  <- file.path("inputs", "na_idx.tsv")

check_input_file(xxtpt_path)
check_input_file(result_path)
check_input_file(naidx_path)

xxtpt_df  <- tryCatch(
  read.delim(xxtpt_path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to read xxtpt.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

result_df <- tryCatch(
  read.delim(result_path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to read result.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

naidx_df  <- tryCatch(
  read.delim(naidx_path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to read na_idx.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# Basic column presence checks
if (!"x" %in% names(xxtpt_df)) {
  stop("Column 'x' not found in xxtpt.tsv")
}
if (!"result" %in% names(result_df)) {
  stop("Column 'result' not found in result.tsv")
}
if (!"x" %in% names(naidx_df)) {
  stop("Column 'x' not found in na_idx.tsv")
}

# Ensure same length
n_xxtpt  <- nrow(xxtpt_df)
n_result <- nrow(result_df)
n_naidx  <- nrow(naidx_df)

if (length(unique(c(n_xxtpt, n_result, n_naidx))) != 1) {
  stop(
    "Input lengths mismatch: ",
    "xxtpt=", n_xxtpt, ", ",
    "result=", n_result, ", ",
    "na_idx=", n_naidx
  )
}

#-----------------------------
# Prepare vectors for convert_simple_units
#-----------------------------
# Character vector of timepoint descriptions (trimmed)
xxtpt_chr <- trimws(as.character(xxtpt_df[["x"]]))

# Numeric result vector (may contain NA)
result_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(result_df[["result"]]))
if (any(is.na(result_df[["result"]]) & !is.na(result_num))) {
  warning("Some 'result' values were coerced to NA during numeric conversion.")
}

# Logical NA index based on provided na_idx file:
# interpret non-missing 'x' as not-NA in xxtpt (FALSE), missing as TRUE
na_idx_log <- is.na(naidx_df[["x"]])

if (!is.logical(na_idx_log) || length(na_idx_log) != length(xxtpt_chr)) {
  stop("na_idx could not be correctly interpreted as a logical vector.")
}

# Sanity check: required length equality already ensured above
stopifnot(
  length(xxtpt_chr) == length(result_num),
  length(xxtpt_chr) == length(na_idx_log)
)

#-----------------------------
# Convert simple time unit patterns
#-----------------------------
# This will:
# - convert hours-only and minutes-only patterns to hours
# - only update positions where result is NA and xxtpt is not NA
# - process hours first, then minutes (per admiral implementation)
# - handle POST/AFTER terminology
converted_result <- tryCatch(
  convert_simple_units(
    xxtpt  = xxtpt_chr,
    result = result_num,
    na_idx = na_idx_log
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("convert_simple_units failed: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

#-----------------------------
# Validate conversion output
#-----------------------------
if (!is.numeric(converted_result) ||
    length(converted_result) != length(result_num)) {
  stop("convert_simple_units returned an invalid result vector.")
}

# Check for unexpected patterns where xxtpt contains obvious H/M patterns
# but result is still NA (simple heuristic)
hour_like <- grepl("\\b[0-9]+\\s*(H|HR|HOUR)", xxtpt_chr, ignore.case = TRUE) |
             grepl("POST[- ]?DOSE|AFTER[- ]?DOSE", xxtpt_chr, ignore.case = TRUE)
min_like  <- grepl("\\b[0-9]+\\s*(M|MIN)\\b", xxtpt_chr, ignore.case = TRUE)

flag_idx <- (hour_like | min_like) & is.na(converted_result) & !na_idx_log

if (any(flag_idx)) {
  warning(
    "Potentially unconverted simple unit patterns detected at indices: ",
    paste(which(flag_idx), collapse = ", ")
  )
}

#-----------------------------
# Write outputs
#-----------------------------
out_df <- data.frame(
  xxtpt  = xxtpt_chr,
  result = converted_result,
  na_idx = na_idx_log,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

out_csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "converted_simple_units.csv")
out_rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "converted_simple_units.rds")

write_ok_csv <- tryCatch(
  {
    write.csv(out_df, out_csv_path, row.names = FALSE)
    TRUE
  },
  error = function(e) {
    warning("Failed to write CSV output: ", conditionMessage(e))
    FALSE
  }
)

write_ok_rds <- tryCatch(
  {
    saveRDS(converted_result, out_rds_path)
    TRUE
  },
  error = function(e) {
    warning("Failed to write RDS output: ", conditionMessage(e))
    FALSE
  }
)

# Confirm files were written
if (!write_ok_csv || !file.exists(out_csv_path)) {
  stop("CSV output file was not written correctly: ", out_csv_path)
}
if (!write_ok_rds || !file.exists(out_rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not written correctly: ", out_rds_path)
)
```

```r
library(admiral)

# Create output directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#------------------------------
# Helper functions
#------------------------------

read_single_column_tsv <- function(path, colname) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop(sprintf("File not found: %s", path))
  }
  df <- tryCatch(
    read.delim(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop(sprintf("Failed to read %s: %s", path, e$message))
  )
  if (!colname %in% names(df)) {
    stop(sprintf("Expected column '%s' not found in %s", colname, path))
  }
  vec <- df[[colname]]
  if (length(vec) == 0L) {
    stop(sprintf("Column '%s' in %s is empty", colname, path))
  }
  vec
}

validate_lengths <- function(...) {
  args <- list(...)
  lens <- vapply(args, length, integer(1))
  if (length(unique(lens)) != 1L) {
    stop(sprintf(
      "Length mismatch among inputs: %s",
      paste(sprintf("%s=%d", names(args), lens), collapse = ", ")
    ))
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

#------------------------------
# Read inputs
#------------------------------

xxtpt <- read_single_column_tsv("inputs/xxtpt.tsv", "x")
result_raw <- read_single_column_tsv("inputs/result.tsv", "result")
na_idx_raw <- read_single_column_tsv("inputs/na_idx.tsv", "x")
treatment_duration_raw <- read_single_column_tsv("inputs/treatment_duration.tsv", "treatment_duration")

#------------------------------
# Preprocess / coerce types
#------------------------------

# Trim xxtpt, keep as character, allow NA
xxtpt <- trimws(as.character(xxtpt))

# Result: numeric, may contain NA; coerce non-numeric to NA with warning
suppressWarnings({
  result <- as.numeric(result_raw)
})
if (any(is.na(result) & !is.na(result_raw))) {
  warning("Some values in 'result' could not be coerced to numeric and were set to NA.")
}

# na_idx: logical vector indicating which positions in xxtpt are NA
# Input preview suggests it is not pre-logical; coerce safely
na_idx_chr <- as.character(na_idx_raw)
na_idx <- tolower(na_idx_chr) %in% c("true", "t", "1", "yes", "y")

# If all FALSE and xxtpt actually has NA, reconstruct from xxtpt
if (!any(na_idx) && any(is.na(xxtpt))) {
  warning("Recomputing na_idx from xxtpt because no TRUE values were found.")
  na_idx <- is.na(xxtpt)
}

# treatment_duration: numeric, non-negative
suppressWarnings({
  treatment_duration <- as.numeric(treatment_duration_raw)
})
if (any(is.na(treatment_duration) & !is.na(treatment_duration_raw))) {
  warning("Some values in 'treatment_duration' could not be coerced to numeric and were set to NA.")
}
if (any(treatment_duration < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  stop("Negative values found in treatment_duration; expected non-negative hours.")
}

#------------------------------
# Validate alignment
#------------------------------

validate_lengths(
  xxtpt = xxtpt,
  result = result,
  na_idx = na_idx,
  treatment_duration = treatment_duration
)

# Sanity check: positions marked as NA in na_idx should correspond to NA in xxtpt
mismatch_na <- which(na_idx & !is.na(xxtpt))
if (length(mismatch_na) > 0L) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "na_idx indicates NA at positions %s, but xxtpt is not NA there.",
    paste(head(mismatch_na, 20), collapse = ", ")
  ))
}

#------------------------------
# Convert special case patterns
#------------------------------

# admiral::convert_special_cases expects:
# - xxtpt (character)
# - result (numeric)
# - na_idx (logical)
# - treatment_duration (numeric)

result_converted <- convert_special_cases(
  xxtpt = xxtpt,
  result = result,
  na_idx = na_idx,
  treatment_duration = treatment_duration
)

#------------------------------
# Post-conversion checks
#------------------------------

# Check for unexpected patterns: positions where result is still NA while xxtpt is non-NA
still_na_idx <- which(is.na(result_converted) & !is.na(xxtpt))
if (length(still_na_idx) > 0L) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "There are %d positions where result remains NA although xxtpt is not NA. Examples indices: %s",
    length(still_na_idx),
    paste(head(still_na_idx, 20), collapse = ", ")
  ))
}

# Simple consistency check: length and type
if (length(result_converted) != length(result)) {
  stop("Length of converted result does not match original result.")
}
if (!is.numeric(result_converted)) {
  stop("Converted result is not numeric.")
}

#------------------------------
# Save outputs
#------------------------------

# Combine into a data frame for CSV output
out_df <- data.frame(
  xxtpt = xxtpt,
  result_original = result,
  result_converted = result_converted,
  na_idx = na_idx,
  treatment_duration = treatment_duration,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "converted_timepoints.csv")
rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "converted_timepoints.rds")

write.csv(out_df, file = csv_path, row.names = FALSE)
saveRDS(result_converted, file = rds_path)

#------------------------------
# Verify that files were written
#------------------------------

if (!file.exists(csv_path)) {
  stop(sprintf("CSV output file was not written: %s", csv_path))
}
if (!file.exists(rds_path)) {
  stop(sprintf("RDS output file was not written: %s", rds_path))
}

# Basic read-back check
check_df <- tryCatch(
  read.csv(csv_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop(sprintf("Failed to read back CSV output: %s", e$message))
)

check_rds <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop(sprintf("Failed to read back RDS output: %s", e$message))
)

if (!is.numeric(check_rds) || length(check_rds) != length(result_converted)) {
  stop("RDS read-back validation failed: content does not match expectations.")
}
```

```r
library(admiral)

# Create outputs directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#---------------------------
# Helper: robust TSV reader
#---------------------------
read_single_col_tsv <- function(path, expected_name = NULL) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop("File not found: ", path)
  }
  df <- tryCatch(
    read.delim(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop("Error reading ", path, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
  if (ncol(df) != 1) {
    stop("File ", path, " must contain exactly one column, found ", ncol(df))
  }
  if (!is.null(expected_name) && !identical(names(df)[1], expected_name)) {
    stop(
      "File ", path, " must have column '", expected_name,
      "', found '", names(df)[1], "'"
    )
  }
  df[[1]]
}

#---------------------------
# Read inputs
#---------------------------
xxtpt_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/xxtpt.tsv", "x")
result_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/result.tsv", "result")
na_idx_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/na_idx.tsv", "x")
range_method_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/range_method.tsv", "method")

#---------------------------
# Basic validation
#---------------------------
if (length(xxtpt_raw) != length(result_raw)) {
  stop("Length mismatch: xxtpt (", length(xxtpt_raw),
       ") vs result (", length(result_raw), ")")
}

# Convert na_idx to logical, aligned with xxtpt
if (length(na_idx_raw) != length(xxtpt_raw)) {
  warning(
    "Length mismatch: na_idx (", length(na_idx_raw),
    ") vs xxtpt (", length(xxtpt_raw),
    "); recycling will be used."
  )
}
na_idx <- as.logical(na_idx_raw)
if (any(is.na(na_idx))) {
  stop("na_idx contains NAs after logical coercion; check inputs/na_idx.tsv")
}

# Derive range_method from input
range_method <- tolower(trimws(range_method_raw[1]))
if (!range_method %in% c("start", "midpoint", "end")) {
  stop(
    "range_method must be one of 'start', 'midpoint', or 'end'; got '",
    range_method_raw[1], "'"
  )
}

#---------------------------
# Prepare vectors
#---------------------------
xxtpt <- trimws(xxtpt_raw)
result <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(result_raw))

if (all(is.na(result)) && any(!is.na(result_raw))) {
  warning("Non-numeric values found in result.tsv; all coerced to NA")
}

# Positions eligible for update: result is NA and xxtpt not NA and !na_idx
update_idx <- is.na(result) & !is.na(xxtpt) & !na_idx

#---------------------------
# Range conversion using admiral
#---------------------------

# 1) Ranges with direction (PRIOR/BEFORE vs POST/AFTER), processed first
#    Accept both 'INFUSION' and 'TREATMENT' and generic START OF ...
#    Pattern examples:
#    "0-4H PRIOR START OF INFUSION"
#    "0-4H BEFORE START OF TREATMENT"
#    "0-6H AFTER START OF TREATMENT"
#
# We'll use convert_ranges twice: once for directional phrases, then for
# simple ranges without explicit direction.

xxtpt_dir <- xxtpt
result_dir <- result

# Masks: only at update_idx
dir_idx <- update_idx

if (any(dir_idx)) {
  # Normalize case for matching; convert_ranges is case-sensitive for pattern,
  # so we just rely on regex matching via its pattern argument.
  # Time unit pattern: hours (H or h), optional spaces.
  # Directional keywords handled via suffix.

  # PRIOR / BEFORE  -> negative sign via direction = "prior"
  # POST / AFTER    -> positive sign via direction = "post"
  #
  # First handle PRIOR/BEFORE
  res_tmp <- admiral::convert_ranges(
    x = xxtpt_dir[dir_idx],
    result = result_dir[dir_idx],
    pattern = "(?i)^(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*[Hh]\\s*(PRIOR|BEFORE)\\b.*$",
    unit = "H",
    method = range_method,
    sign = -1,
    extract = TRUE
  )

  # Update only those newly converted (non-NA where previously NA)
  new_non_na <- is.na(result_dir[dir_idx]) & !is.na(res_tmp)
  result_dir[dir_idx][new_non_na] <- res_tmp[new_non_na]

  # Then handle POST/AFTER
  res_tmp2 <- admiral::convert_ranges(
    x = xxtpt_dir[dir_idx],
    result = result_dir[dir_idx],
    pattern = "(?i)^(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*[Hh]\\s*(POST|AFTER)\\b.*$",
    unit = "H",
    method = range_method,
    sign = 1,
    extract = TRUE
  )

  new_non_na2 <- is.na(result_dir[dir_idx]) & !is.na(res_tmp2)
  result_dir[dir_idx][new_non_na2] <- res_tmp2[new_non_na2]
}

# 2) Simple ranges without explicit direction, assume positive
#    Example: "0-6h Post-dose" -> 3 (midpoint)
xxtpt_simple <- xxtpt
result_simple <- result_dir

simple_idx <- update_idx & is.na(result_simple)

if (any(simple_idx)) {
  res_simple <- admiral::convert_ranges(
    x = xxtpt_simple[simple_idx],
    result = result_simple[simple_idx],
    pattern = "(?i)^(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*[Hh]\\b.*$",
    unit = "H",
    method = range_method,
    sign = 1,
    extract = TRUE
  )

  new_non_na_simple <- is.na(result_simple[simple_idx]) & !is.na(res_simple)
  result_simple[simple_idx][new_non_na_simple] <- res_simple[new_non_na_simple]
}

updated_result <- result_simple

#---------------------------
# Final validation
#---------------------------
if (length(updated_result) != length(result_raw)) {
  stop("Length of updated_result differs from original result; possible corruption.")
}

# Check for unexpected patterns: entries still NA where xxtpt is non-NA and !na_idx
remaining_na <- which(is.na(updated_result) & !is.na(xxtpt) & !na_idx)
if (length(remaining_na) > 0) {
  warning(
    "There are ", length(remaining_na),
    " positions with non-NA xxtpt and !na_idx but NA updated_result. ",
    "These may be non-range patterns or unparsed formats."
  )
}

#---------------------------
# Save outputs
#---------------------------

# Save updated_result as CSV
out_df <- data.frame(
  result = updated_result,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

out_csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "updated_result.csv")
out_rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "updated_result.rds")

write.csv(out_df, out_csv_path, row.names = FALSE)
saveRDS(updated_result, out_rds_path)

#---------------------------
# Confirm outputs written
#---------------------------
if (!file.exists(out_csv_path)) {
  stop("CSV output file was not written: ", out_csv_path)
}
if (!file.exists(out_rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not written: ", out_rds_path)
}

# Basic sanity check: re-read and compare lengths
chk_df <- tryCatch(
  read.csv(out_csv_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Error re-reading CSV output: ", conditionMessage(e))
)
chk_vec <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(out_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Error re-reading RDS output: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

if (nrow(chk_df) != length(updated_result)) {
  stop("Row count mismatch in CSV output vs updated_result")
}
if (length(chk_vec) != length(updated_result)) {
  stop("Length mismatch in RDS output vs updated_result")
}

# Optional: quick check for obviously malformed numeric values
if (any(!is.finite(updated_result[!is.na(updated_result)]))) {
  warning("Non-finite values detected in updated_result (Inf, -Inf, or NaN).")
}
```

library(admiral)

# Create outputs directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#---------------------------
# Helper: robust TSV reader
#---------------------------
read_single_col_tsv <- function(path, expected_name = NULL) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop("File not found: ", path)
  }
  df <- tryCatch(
    read.delim(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop("Error reading ", path, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
  if (ncol(df) != 1) {
    stop("File ", path, " must contain exactly one column, found ", ncol(df))
  }
  if (!is.null(expected_name) && !identical(names(df)[1], expected_name)) {
    stop(
      "File ", path, " must have column '", expected_name,
      "', found '", names(df)[1], "'"
    )
  }
  df[[1]]
}

#---------------------------
# Read inputs
#---------------------------
xxtpt_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/xxtpt.tsv", "x")
result_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/result.tsv", "result")
na_idx_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/na_idx.tsv", "x")
range_method_raw <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/range_method.tsv", "method")

#---------------------------
# Basic validation
#---------------------------
if (length(xxtpt_raw) != length(result_raw)) {
  stop("Length mismatch: xxtpt (", length(xxtpt_raw),
       ") vs result (", length(result_raw), ")")
}

# Convert na_idx to logical, aligned with xxtpt
if (length(na_idx_raw) != length(xxtpt_raw)) {
  warning(
    "Length mismatch: na_idx (", length(na_idx_raw),
    ") vs xxtpt (", length(xxtpt_raw),
    "); recycling will be used."
  )
}
na_idx <- as.logical(na_idx_raw)
if (any(is.na(na_idx))) {
  stop("na_idx contains NAs after logical coercion; check inputs/na_idx.tsv")
}

# Derive range_method from input
range_method <- tolower(trimws(range_method_raw[1]))
if (!range_method %in% c("start", "midpoint", "end")) {
  stop(
    "range_method must be one of 'start', 'midpoint', or 'end'; got '",
    range_method_raw[1], "'"
  )
}

#---------------------------
# Prepare vectors
#---------------------------
xxtpt <- trimws(xxtpt_raw)
result <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(result_raw))

if (all(is.na(result)) && any(!is.na(result_raw))) {
  warning("Non-numeric values found in result.tsv; all coerced to NA")
}

# Positions eligible for update: result is NA and xxtpt not NA and !na_idx
update_idx <- is.na(result) & !is.na(xxtpt) & !na_idx

#---------------------------
# Range conversion using admiral
#---------------------------

# 1) Ranges with direction (PRIOR/BEFORE vs POST/AFTER), processed first
#    Accept both 'INFUSION' and 'TREATMENT' and generic START OF ...
#    Pattern examples:
#    "0-4H PRIOR START OF INFUSION"
#    "0-4H BEFORE START OF TREATMENT"
#    "0-6H AFTER START OF TREATMENT"
#
# We'll use convert_ranges twice: once for directional phrases, then for
# simple ranges without explicit direction.

xxtpt_dir <- xxtpt
result_dir <- result

# Masks: only at update_idx
dir_idx <- update_idx

if (any(dir_idx)) {
  # Normalize case for matching; convert_ranges is case-sensitive for pattern,
  # so we just rely on regex matching via its pattern argument.
  # Time unit pattern: hours (H or h), optional spaces.
  # Directional keywords handled via suffix.

  # PRIOR / BEFORE  -> negative sign via direction = "prior"
  # POST / AFTER    -> positive sign via direction = "post"
  #
  # First handle PRIOR/BEFORE
  res_tmp <- admiral::convert_ranges(
    x = xxtpt_dir[dir_idx],
    result = result_dir[dir_idx],
    pattern = "(?i)^(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*[Hh]\\s*(PRIOR|BEFORE)\\b.*$",
    unit = "H",
    method = range_method,
    sign = -1,
    extract = TRUE
  )

  # Update only those newly converted (non-NA where previously NA)
  new_non_na <- is.na(result_dir[dir_idx]) & !is.na(res_tmp)
  result_dir[dir_idx][new_non_na] <- res_tmp[new_non_na]

  # Then handle POST/AFTER
  res_tmp2 <- admiral::convert_ranges(
    x = xxtpt_dir[dir_idx],
    result = result_dir[dir_idx],
    pattern = "(?i)^(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*[Hh]\\s*(POST|AFTER)\\b.*$",
    unit = "H",
    method = range_method,
    sign = 1,
    extract = TRUE
  )

  new_non_na2 <- is.na(result_dir[dir_idx]) & !is.na(res_tmp2)
  result_dir[dir_idx][new_non_na2] <- res_tmp2[new_non_na2]
}

# 2) Simple ranges without explicit direction, assume positive
#    Example: "0-6h Post-dose" -> 3 (midpoint)
xxtpt_simple <- xxtpt
result_simple <- result_dir

simple_idx <- update_idx & is.na(result_simple)

if (any(simple_idx)) {
  res_simple <- admiral::convert_ranges(
    x = xxtpt_simple[simple_idx],
    result = result_simple[simple_idx],
    pattern = "(?i)^(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+\\.?\\d*)\\s*[Hh]\\b.*$",
    unit = "H",
    method = range_method,
    sign = 1,
    extract = TRUE
  )

  new_non_na_simple <- is.na(result_simple[simple_idx]) & !is.na(res_simple)
  result_simple[simple_idx][new_non_na_simple] <- res_simple[new_non_na_simple]
}

updated_result <- result_simple

#---------------------------
# Final validation
#---------------------------
if (length(updated_result) != length(result_raw)) {
  stop("Length of updated_result differs from original result; possible corruption.")
}

# Check for unexpected patterns: entries still NA where xxtpt is non-NA and !na_idx
remaining_na <- which(is.na(updated_result) & !is.na(xxtpt) & !na_idx)
if (length(remaining_na) > 0) {
  warning(
    "There are ", length(remaining_na),
    " positions with non-NA xxtpt and !na_idx but NA updated_result. ",
    "These may be non-range patterns or unparsed formats."
  )
}

#---------------------------
# Save outputs
#---------------------------

# Save updated_result as CSV
out_df <- data.frame(
  result = updated_result,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

out_csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "updated_result.csv")
out_rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "updated_result.rds")

write.csv(out_df, out_csv_path, row.names = FALSE)
saveRDS(updated_result, out_rds_path)

#---------------------------
# Confirm outputs written
#---------------------------
if (!file.exists(out_csv_path)) {
  stop("CSV output file was not written: ", out_csv_path)
}
if (!file.exists(out_rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not written: ", out_rds_path)
}

# Basic sanity check: re-read and compare lengths
chk_df <- tryCatch(
  read.csv(out_csv_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Error re-reading CSV output: ", conditionMessage(e))
)
chk_vec <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(out_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Error re-reading RDS output: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

if (nrow(chk_df) != length(updated_result)) {
  stop("Row count mismatch in CSV output vs updated_result")
}
if (length(chk_vec) != length(updated_result)) {
  stop("Length mismatch in RDS output vs updated_result")
}

# Optional: quick check for obviously malformed numeric values
if (any(!is.finite(updated_result[!is.na(updated_result)]))) {
  warning("Non-finite values detected in updated_result (Inf, -Inf, or NaN).")
}

prompt:
Write R code to convert time unit patterns using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/xxtpt.tsv, inputs/result.tsv, inputs/na_idx.tsv). Converts days and hours+minutes combination patterns to numeric hours. Additional details: Recognizes and converts the following patterns: * Days: "Day 1" -> 24, "2D" -> 48, "2 days" -> 48, "30 DAYS AFTER LAST" -> 720 Requires at least one unit indicator (day prefix, d/day/days suffix, or contextual suffix). Bare numbers without units (e.g., "2") are not interpreted as days to avoid ambiguity. * Hours+minutes: "1H30M" -> 1.5, "2HR15MIN" -> 2.25 Only updates result for positions where result is currently NA and xxtpt is not NA. Use admiral's convert_time_units function with the following parameters: xxtpt (Character vector of timepoint descriptions (trimmed, no leading/ trailing whitespace)), result (Numeric vector of results (partially filled, may contain NA)), na_idx (Logical vector indicating which positions in xxtpt are NA). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: Updated numeric vector with time unit patterns converted to hours. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

## Inputs preview (no reference answers)

### may.tsv (25 bytes)
may
AVAL
AVISITN
USUBJID

### na_idx.tsv (15 bytes)
time_unit
days

### no.tsv (24 bytes)
no
AVAL
AVISITN
USUBJID

### result.tsv (28 bytes)
result
AVAL
AVISITN
USUBJID

### xxtpt.tsv (16 bytes)
time_unit
weeks
raw_response:
```r
library(admiral)

# ---- Setup ----
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE)

# ---- Read inputs ----
xxtpt_df <- tryCatch(
  read.delim("inputs/xxtpt.tsv", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading inputs/xxtpt.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

result_df <- tryCatch(
  read.delim("inputs/result.tsv", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading inputs/result.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

na_idx_df <- tryCatch(
  read.delim("inputs/na_idx.tsv", header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading inputs/na_idx.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# ---- Basic validation ----
required_cols_xxtpt  <- c("time_unit")
required_cols_result <- c("result")
required_cols_na     <- c("time_unit")

if (!all(required_cols_xxtpt %in% names(xxtpt_df))) {
  stop("xxtpt.tsv must contain column: ", paste(required_cols_xxtpt, collapse = ", "))
}
if (!all(required_cols_result %in% names(result_df))) {
  stop("result.tsv must contain column: ", paste(required_cols_result, collapse = ", "))
}
if (!all(required_cols_na %in% names(na_idx_df))) {
  stop("na_idx.tsv must contain column: ", paste(required_cols_na, collapse = ", "))
}

n_xxtpt  <- nrow(xxtpt_df)
n_result <- nrow(result_df)
n_na     <- nrow(na_idx_df)

if (length(unique(c(n_xxtpt, n_result, n_na))) != 1L) {
  stop(
    "Row counts of input files must match. ",
    "xxtpt: ", n_xxtpt, ", result: ", n_result, ", na_idx: ", n_na
  )
}

# ---- Prepare vectors for convert_time_units ----
xxtpt_vec <- trimws(as.character(xxtpt_df$time_unit))

# result may be numeric or character; coerce safely to numeric
result_vec_raw <- result_df$result
suppressWarnings({
  result_vec <- as.numeric(result_vec_raw)
})
# If a value failed to convert (NA where raw was non-NA and non-empty), raise an error
bad_conv <- !is.na(result_vec_raw) & nzchar(as.character(result_vec_raw)) & is.na(result_vec)
if (any(bad_conv)) {
  stop(
    "Non-numeric values in result column that cannot be converted to numeric: ",
    paste(unique(result_vec_raw[bad_conv]), collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# Construct na_idx logical: TRUE where xxtpt is NA
na_idx_vec <- is.na(xxtpt_vec)

# ---- Convert time units using admiral ----
updated_result <- tryCatch(
  convert_time_units(
    xxtpt  = xxtpt_vec,
    result = result_vec,
    na_idx = na_idx_vec
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in convert_time_units: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# ---- Post-conversion validation ----
if (!is.numeric(updated_result)) {
  stop("convert_time_units did not return a numeric vector.")
}
if (length(updated_result) != n_xxtpt) {
  stop(
    "Length of updated_result (", length(updated_result),
    ") does not match input length (", n_xxtpt, ")."
  )
}

# Check that result was only updated where original result was NA and xxtpt not NA
changed_idx <- which(!is.na(result_vec) & (result_vec != updated_result))
if (length(changed_idx) > 0L) {
  stop(
    "Unexpected changes detected in non-NA original results at indices: ",
    paste(changed_idx, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# Check for any suspicious non-converted patterns that look like time units
suspect_idx <- which(
  is.na(updated_result) &
    !is.na(xxtpt_vec) &
    grepl("(?i)\\b(day|days|d\\b|hr|h\\b|hour|min|after)\\b", xxtpt_vec, perl = TRUE)
)
if (length(suspect_idx) > 0L) {
  warning(
    "Some time-like patterns were not converted (indices): ",
    paste(suspect_idx, collapse = ", "),
    ". Examples: ",
    paste(utils::head(xxtpt_vec[suspect_idx], 5), collapse = " | ")
  )
}

# ---- Prepare output objects ----
# 1) Simple numeric vector as RDS
out_rds_path <- "outputs/converted_time_hours.rds"
saveRDS(updated_result, file = out_rds_path)

# 2) Combined data frame with original and converted values as CSV
out_df <- data.frame(
  xxtpt       = xxtpt_vec,
  result_orig = result_vec,
  result_conv = updated_result,
  na_idx      = na_idx_vec,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

out_csv_path <- "outputs/converted_time_hours.csv"
write.csv(out_df, file = out_csv_path, row.names = FALSE)

# ---- Confirm outputs written correctly ----
rds_check <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(out_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading back RDS output: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

csv_check <- tryCatch(
  read.csv(out_csv_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading back CSV output: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

if (!is.numeric(rds_check) || length(rds_check) != length(updated_result)) {
  stop("RDS output integrity check failed: content mismatch.")
}

if (!all(c("xxtpt", "result_orig", "result_conv", "na_idx") %in% names(csv_check))) {
  stop("CSV output integrity check failed: expected columns not found.")
}

invisible(NULL)
```

Write R code to convert `xxtpt` strings to hours using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/xxtpt.tsv, inputs/treatment_duration.tsv, inputs/range_method.tsv). `r lifecycle::badge("experimental")` Converts CDISC timepoint strings (e.g., `PCTPT`, `VSTPT`, `EGTPT`, `ISTPT`, `LBTPT`) into numeric hours for analysis. The function handles common dose-centric formats including pre-dose, post-dose (hours/minutes), days, time ranges, and treatment-related time markers. convert_xxtpt_to_hours(c( "Screening", "Pre-dose", "Pre-treatment", "Before", "30M", "1H", "2H POSTDOSE", "Day 1" )) convert_xxtpt_to_hours(c("5 MIN PREDOSE", "5 MIN PRE-DOSE", "1 HOUR BEFORE")) convert_xxtpt_to_hours(c( "EOT", "1 HOUR POST EOT", "1 HOUR AFTER EOT", "After End of Treatment" )) convert_xxtpt_to_hours( c( "EOI", "1 HOUR POST EOI", "24 HR POST INF", "24 HR POST-INF", "30MIN AFTER END OF INFUSION", "8H PRIOR START OF INFUSION", "10MIN PRE EOI" ), treatment_duration = 1 ) convert_xxtpt_to_hours( c("EOI", "1 HOUR POST EOI", "EOI", "1 HOUR POST EOI"), treatment_duration = c(1, 1, 2, 2) ) convert_xxtpt_to_hours(c( "0-6h Post-dose", "0-4H PRIOR START OF INFUSION", "8-16H POST START OF INFUSION" )) convert_xxtpt_to_hours("0-6h Post-dose", range_method = "end") convert_xxtpt_to_hours("0-6h Post-dose", range_method = "start") convert_xxtpt_to_hours( c( "0-4H AFTER EOI", "0-4H POST EOI", "4-8H AFTER END OF INFUSION", "4-8H AFTER EOT", "4-8H POST INFUSION", "4-8H POST-INF" ), treatment_duration = 1 ) convert_xxtpt_to_hours( c("Pre-dose", "1H POST", "2H POST", "4H POST"), treatment_duration = 2 ) convert_xxtpt_to_hours( c("Pre-dose", "EOI", "1H POST EOI", "2H POST EOI"), treatment_duration = 2 ) convert_xxtpt_to_hours( c("1H POST", "1H POST EOI", "1H POST INFUSION"), treatment_duration = 2 ) Additional details: The function recognizes the following patterns (all case-insensitive): **Special Cases:** * `"Screening"` -> 0 * `"Pre-dose"`, `"Predose"`, `"Pre-treatment"`, `"Pre-infusion"`, `"Pre-inf"`, `"Before"`, `"Infusion"`, `"0H"` -> 0 * `"EOI"`, `"EOT"`, `"End of Infusion"`, `"End of Treatment"`, `"After End of Infusion"`, `"After End of Treatment"` -> `treatment_duration` (default: 0) * `"Morning"`, `"Evening"` -> `NA_real_` * Unrecognized values -> `NA_real_` **Time Ranges:** Time ranges are converted based on the `range_method` parameter: * `"0-6h Post-dose"` with `range_method = "midpoint"` (default) -> 3 * `"0-6h Post-dose"` with `range_method = "start"` -> 0 * `"0-6h Post-dose"` with `range_method = "end"` -> 6 * `"0-4H PRIOR START OF INFUSION"` with midpoint -> -2 (negative for prior) * `"8-16H POST START OF INFUSION"` with midpoint -> 12 * `"0-4H AFTER EOI"` with midpoint and treatment_duration=1 -> 3 (1 + 2) * `"0-4H EOT"` with midpoint and treatment_duration=0 -> 2 * `"4-8H AFTER END OF INFUSION"` with midpoint and treatment_duration=1 -> 7 (1 + 6) * `"4-8H POST INFUSION"` with midpoint and treatment_duration=1 -> 7 (1 + 6) * `"4-8H POST-INF"` with midpoint and treatment_duration=1 -> 7 (1 + 6) **Time-based Conversions:** * **Days**: `"Day 1"` -> 24, `"2D"` -> 48, `"30 DAYS AFTER LAST"` -> 720 (requires unit indicator; bare numbers like `"2"` return `NA`) * **Hours + Minutes**: `"1H30M"` -> 1.5 * **Hours**: `"2 hours"` -> 2, `"1 HOUR POST"` -> 1 * **Minutes**: `"30M"` -> 0.5, `"30 MIN POST"` -> 0.5 * **Predose**: `"5 MIN PREDOSE"` -> -0.0833, `"5 MIN PRE-DOSE"` -> -0.0833 * **Before treatment**: `"5 MIN BEFORE"` -> -0.0833 * **Post EOI/EOT**: `"1 HOUR POST EOI"` -> treatment_duration + 1, `"24 HR POST INF"` -> treatment_duration + 24, `"24 HR POST-INF"` -> treatment_duration + 24, `"1 HOUR AFTER EOT"` -> treatment_duration + 1 * **After end**: `"30MIN AFTER END OF INFUSION"` -> treatment_duration + 0.5 * **Start of infusion/treatment**: `"8H PRIOR START OF INFUSION"` -> -8, `"8H BEFORE START OF TREATMENT"` -> -8 * **Pre EOI/EOT**: `"10MIN PRE EOI"` -> treatment_duration - 1/6, `"10MIN BEFORE EOT"` -> treatment_duration - 1/6 **Supported Unit Formats:** * Hours: H, h, HR, hr, HOUR, hour (with optional plurals) * Minutes: M, m, MIN, min, MINUTE, minute (with optional plurals) * Days: D, d, DAY, day (with optional plurals) * Flexible whitespace and optional "Post-dose", "POST", "After last" suffixes * Hyphens in compound terms: "PRE-DOSE", "POST-INF", "POST-INFUSION" **Understanding POST/AFTER Patterns:** It's important to distinguish between patterns relative to treatment **start** versus treatment **end**: * **Relative to START** (treatment_duration NOT added): - `"1H POST"`, `"1H AFTER"`, `"30M POST"` -> Time from dose/treatment start - These patterns assume treatment starts at time 0 - Example: `"1H POST"` -> 1 hour (regardless of treatment_duration) * **Relative to END** (treatment_duration IS added): - `"1H POST EOI"`, `"1H AFTER EOT"`, `"1H POST INFUSION"` -> Time from treatment end - These patterns account for when treatment ends (start + duration) - Example: `"1H POST EOI"` with treatment_duration=2 -> 3 hours (2 + 1) This distinction follows standard pharmacokinetic conventions where "post-dose" refers to time from treatment initiation, while "post end of infusion" refers to time from treatment completion. **Vectorized Treatment Duration:** When `treatment_duration` is a vector, each timepoint uses its corresponding treatment duration value. This is useful when different records have different treatment durations (e.g., different infusion lengths). Options are "midpoint" (default), "start", or "end".). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: A numeric vector of timepoints in hours.
```r
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

#---------------------------
# Helper: safe convert wrapper
#---------------------------
safe_convert_xxtpt_to_hours <- safely(convert_xxtpt_to_hours, otherwise = NA_real_)

#---------------------------
# Read inputs
#---------------------------
xxtpt_df <- read_tsv("inputs/xxtpt.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
tdur_df <- read_tsv("inputs/treatment_duration.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
range_df <- read_tsv("inputs/range_method.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

#---------------------------
# Derive parameters
#---------------------------

# range_method: validate and fall back to "midpoint"
range_method <- tolower(range_df$method[1] %||% "midpoint")
if (!range_method %in% c("midpoint", "start", "end")) {
  warning("Unsupported range_method in inputs/range_method.tsv. Using 'midpoint'.")
  range_method <- "midpoint"
}

# treatment_duration:
# Expect a numeric column named 'treatment_duration'; otherwise default to 0
if ("treatment_duration" %in% names(tdur_df)) {
  tdur_vec <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(tdur_df$treatment_duration))
  if (all(is.na(tdur_vec))) {
    warning("Column 'treatment_duration' in treatment_duration.tsv is not numeric. Using 0.")
    tdur_vec <- 0
  }
} else {
  warning("No 'treatment_duration' column in treatment_duration.tsv. Using 0.")
  tdur_vec <- 0
}

#---------------------------
# Prepare xxtpt vector
#---------------------------
# Expect a column named 'x' with timepoint strings; if not present, use first character column
if (!"x" %in% names(xxtpt_df)) {
  char_cols <- names(xxtpt_df)[vapply(xxtpt_df, is.character, logical(1))]
  if (length(char_cols) == 0) {
    stop("No character columns found in xxtpt.tsv to use as xxtpt.")
  }
  warning("No 'x' column in xxtpt.tsv. Using first character column: ", char_cols[1])
  x_col <- char_cols[1]
} else {
  x_col <- "x"
}

xxtpt_vec <- xxtpt_df[[x_col]]

# Align treatment_duration length with xxtpt length (vectorized support)
if (length(tdur_vec) == 1L) {
  tdur_vec <- rep(tdur_vec, length(xxtpt_vec))
} else if (length(tdur_vec) != length(xxtpt_vec)) {
  warning(
    "Length of treatment_duration (", length(tdur_vec),
    ") does not match length of xxtpt (", length(xxtpt_vec),
    "). Recycling shorter vector."
  )
  tdur_vec <- rep(tdur_vec, length.out = length(xxtpt_vec))
}

#---------------------------
# Convert xxtpt to hours
#---------------------------
conv_res <- map2(
  xxtpt_vec,
  tdur_vec,
  ~ safe_convert_xxtpt_to_hours(
    xxtpt = .x,
    treatment_duration = .y,
    range_method = range_method
  )
)

# Extract results and possible errors
hours_vec <- map_dbl(conv_res, "result")
err_list <- map_chr(conv_res, ~ {
  err <- .x$error
  if (is.null(err)) "" else conditionMessage(err)
})

result_df <- xxtpt_df %>%
  mutate(
    xxtpt_original = .data[[x_col]],
    treatment_duration = tdur_vec,
    xxtpt_hours = hours_vec,
    parse_error = na_if(err_list, "")
  )

#---------------------------
# Basic validation checks
#---------------------------

# Check for all NA conversions (suspicious)
if (all(is.na(result_df$xxtpt_hours))) {
  warning("All converted timepoints are NA. Check input formats and mapping logic.")
}

# Check for unexpected non-time descriptors that still produced non-NA
non_time_patterns <- c("Morning", "Evening")
suspicious <- result_df %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(xxtpt_original, regex(paste(non_time_patterns, collapse = "|"), ignore_case = TRUE)) &
      !is.na(xxtpt_hours)
  )

if (nrow(suspicious) > 0) {
  warning("Some non-time descriptors produced non-NA results. Inspect 'suspicious_results.csv'.")
  write_csv(suspicious, "outputs/suspicious_results.csv", na = "")
}

#---------------------------
# Write outputs
#---------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

write_csv(result_df, "outputs/xxtpt_converted_hours.csv", na = "")

saveRDS(result_df, "outputs/xxtpt_converted_hours.rds")

#---------------------------
# Confirm outputs
#---------------------------
stopifnot(
  file.exists("outputs/xxtpt_converted_hours.csv"),
  file.exists("outputs/xxtpt_converted_hours.rds")
)

# Quick sanity summary to console for manual inspection
summary_df <- result_df %>%
  summarize(
    n = n(),
    n_na = sum(is.na(xxtpt_hours)),
    n_non_na = sum(!is.na(xxtpt_hours)),
    min_time = suppressWarnings(min(xxtpt_hours, na.rm = TRUE)),
    max_time = suppressWarnings(max(xxtpt_hours, na.rm = TRUE))
  )

print(summary_df)
```

```r
library(admiral)

# Create directories if needed
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#---------------------------
# Helper functions
#---------------------------

safe_read_tsv_single_col <- function(path, col_name) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) stop("File not found: ", path)
  df <- tryCatch(
    read.delim(path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop("Error reading ", path, ": ", e$message)
  )
  if (!col_name %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Column '", col_name, "' not found in ", path)
  }
  val <- df[[col_name]]
  if (length(val) < 1 || all(is.na(val))) {
    stop("No non-NA values found in ", path)
  }
  val[[1]]
}

#---------------------------
# Read parameter inputs
#---------------------------

dataset_name <- safe_read_tsv_single_col("inputs/dataset_name.tsv", "dataset_name")
date_imputation <- safe_read_tsv_single_col("inputs/date.tsv", "date_imputation")
filter_expr_chr <- safe_read_tsv_single_col("inputs/filter.tsv", "filter")
set_values_to_chr <- safe_read_tsv_single_col("inputs/set_values_to.tsv", "set_values_to")

# Validate date_imputation (basic check)
if (!date_imputation %in% c("first", "last", "none")) {
  stop("Unexpected date_imputation value: ", date_imputation,
       ". Expected one of 'first', 'last', 'none'.")
}

#---------------------------
# Load dataset referenced in dataset_name
# (assume TSV at inputs/{dataset_name}.tsv)
#---------------------------

dataset_path <- file.path("inputs", paste0(dataset_name, ".tsv"))
if (!file.exists(dataset_path)) {
  stop("Dataset file not found for dataset_name='", dataset_name, "': ", dataset_path)
}

dataset <- tryCatch(
  read.delim(dataset_path, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading dataset file ", dataset_path, ": ", e$message)
)

if (!is.data.frame(dataset) || nrow(dataset) == 0L) {
  stop("Dataset loaded from ", dataset_path, " is empty or invalid.")
}

#---------------------------
# Build expressions safely
#---------------------------

# Build filter expression (unquoted condition)
filter_call <- tryCatch(
  rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr_chr),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to parse filter expression '", filter_expr_chr, "': ", e$message)
)
if (!rlang::is_expression(filter_call)) {
  stop("Parsed filter expression is not a valid R expression.")
}

# Build set_values_to expression
set_values_call <- tryCatch(
  rlang::parse_expr(set_values_to_chr),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to parse set_values_to expression '", set_values_to_chr, "': ", e$message)
)

# Evaluate set_values_call in a minimal environment where exprs() is available
set_values_env <- rlang::env(parent = rlang::global_env(), exprs = rlang::exprs)
set_values_to <- tryCatch(
  eval(set_values_call, envir = set_values_env),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to evaluate set_values_to: ", e$message)
)

# Basic validation of set_values_to
if (!is.list(set_values_to) || length(set_values_to) == 0L) {
  stop("set_values_to must evaluate to a non-empty list-like object.")
}

#---------------------------
# Define date variable/expression
#---------------------------

# Here we assume a standard date variable name in the dataset, e.g., ADT
# Adjust if your real dataset uses a different date variable.
if (!"ADT" %in% names(dataset)) {
  stop("Expected date variable 'ADT' not found in dataset '", dataset_name,
       "'. Please adjust the code to the correct date variable.")
}

date_var <- ADT

#---------------------------
# Construct date_source object
#---------------------------

date_src <- tryCatch(
  date_source(
    dataset_name = dataset_name,
    filter       = !!filter_call,
    date         = date_var,
    set_values_to = set_values_to
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error creating date_source object: ", e$message)
)

#---------------------------
# Basic validation of date_source object
#---------------------------

if (!inherits(date_src, "date_source")) {
  stop("Object created is not of class 'date_source'.")
}

# Optionally inspect structure for unexpected patterns
date_src_str <- capture.output(str(date_src))

# Check for obvious issues (very naive checks)
if (any(grepl("NULL", date_src_str))) {
  warning("Possible NULL components detected in date_source structure. Inspect manually.")
}

#---------------------------
# Save outputs
#---------------------------

# date_source is a complex object -> save as RDS
date_src_rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "date_source.rds")
tryCatch(
  saveRDS(date_src, date_src_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to write RDS file: ", e$message)
)

# For convenience, also derive a simple data frame summary (if possible) and save as CSV
# Attempt to coerce to data.frame if it has a known slot/element `dataset_name`
date_src_df <- NULL
if (!is.null(date_src$dataset_name)) {
  date_src_df <- data.frame(
    dataset_name = date_src$dataset_name,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

if (!is.null(date_src_df)) {
  date_src_csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "date_source_summary.csv")
  tryCatch(
    write.csv(date_src_df, date_src_csv_path, row.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) warning("Failed to write CSV summary: ", e$message)
  )
}

#---------------------------
# Confirm files were written
#---------------------------

if (!file.exists(date_src_rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not created: ", date_src_rds_path)
}

# Basic integrity check: read back the RDS
date_src_check <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(date_src_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to read back RDS file: ", e$message)
)

if (!inherits(date_src_check, "date_source")) {
  stop("Read-back object from RDS is not of class 'date_source'.")
}

# Optional: check for simple misalignment patterns in structure text
date_src_check_str <- capture.output(str(date_src_check))
if (length(date_src_check_str) == 0L) {
  warning("Empty structure output for date_source after round-trip read.")
}
```

filter_call <- tryCatch(
  rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr_chr),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to parse filter expression '", filter_expr_chr, "': ", e$message)
)
if (!rlang::is_expression(filter_call)) {
  stop("Parsed filter expression is not a valid R expression.")
}

# Build set_values_to expression
set_values_call <- tryCatch(
  rlang::parse_expr(set_values_to_chr),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to parse set_values_to expression '", set_values_to_chr, "': ", e$message)
)

# Evaluate set_values_call in a minimal environment where exprs() is available
set_values_env <- rlang::env(parent = rlang::global_env(), exprs = rlang::exprs)
set_values_to <- tryCatch(
  eval(set_values_call, envir = set_values_env),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to evaluate set_values_to: ", e$message)
)

# Basic validation of set_values_to
if (!is.list(set_values_to) || length(set_values_to) == 0L) {
  stop("set_values_to must evaluate to a non-empty list-like object.")
}

#---------------------------
# Define date variable/expression
#---------------------------

# Here we assume a standard date variable name in the dataset, e.g., ADT
# Adjust if your real dataset uses a different date variable.
if (!"ADT" %in% names(dataset)) {
  stop("Expected date variable 'ADT' not found in dataset '", dataset_name,
       "'. Please adjust the code to the correct date variable.")
}

date_var <- ADT

#---------------------------
# Construct date_source object
#---------------------------

date_src <- tryCatch(
  date_source(
    dataset_name = dataset_name,
    filter       = !!filter_call,
    date         = date_var,
    set_values_to = set_values_to
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error creating date_source object: ", e$message)
)

#---------------------------
# Basic validation of date_source object
#---------------------------

if (!inherits(date_src, "date_source")) {
  stop("Object created is not of class 'date_source'.")
}

# Optionally inspect structure for unexpected patterns
date_src_str <- capture.output(str(date_src))

# Check for obvious issues (very naive checks)
if (any(grepl("NULL", date_src_str))) {
  warning("Possible NULL components detected in date_source structure. Inspect manually.")
}

#---------------------------
# Save outputs
#---------------------------

# date_source is a complex object -> save as RDS
date_src_rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "date_source.rds")
tryCatch(
  saveRDS(date_src, date_src_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to write RDS file: ", e$message)
)

# For convenience, also derive a simple data frame summary (if possible) and save as CSV
# Attempt to coerce to data.frame if it has a known slot/element `dataset_name`
date_src_df <- NULL
if (!is.null(date_src$dataset_name)) {
  date_src_df <- data.frame(
    dataset_name = date_src$dataset_name,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

if (!is.null(date_src_df)) {
  date_src_csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "date_source_summary.csv")
  tryCatch(
    write.csv(date_src_df, date_src_csv_path, row.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) warning("Failed to write CSV summary: ", e$message)
  )
}

#---------------------------
# Confirm files were written
#---------------------------

if (!file.exists(date_src_rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not created: ", date_src_rds_path)
}

# Basic integrity check: read back the RDS
date_src_check <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(date_src_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to read back RDS file: ", e$message)
)

if (!inherits(date_src_check, "date_source")) {
  stop("Read-back object from RDS is not of class 'date_source'.")
}

# Optional: check for simple misalignment patterns in structure text
date_src_check_str <- capture.output(str(date_src_check))
if (length(date_src_check_str) == 0L) {
  warning("Empty structure output for date_source after round-trip read.")
}

Write R code to derive basetype variable using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/basetypes.tsv). Baseline Type `BASETYPE` is needed when there is more than one definition of baseline for a given Analysis Parameter `PARAM` in the same dataset. For a given parameter, if Baseline Value `BASE` or `BASEC` are derived and there is more than one definition of baseline, then `BASETYPE` must be non-null on all records of any type for that parameter where either `BASE` or `BASEC` are also non-null. Each value of `BASETYPE` refers to a definition of baseline that characterizes the value of `BASE` on that row. Please see section 4.2.1.6 of the ADaM Implementation Guide, version 1.3 for further background. Adds the `BASETYPE` variable to a dataset and duplicates records based upon the provided conditions. Additional details: For each element of `basetypes` the input dataset is subset based upon the provided expression and the `BASETYPE` variable is set to the name of the expression. Then, all subsets are stacked. Records which do not match any condition are kept and `BASETYPE` is set to `NA`. Use admiral's derive_basetype_records function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("basetypes"))`), basetypes (A *named* list of expressions created using the `rlang::exprs()` function The names corresponds to the values of the new.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with variable `BASETYPE` added. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/dataset_ref.tsv, inputs/by_vars.tsv, inputs/id_vars_ref.tsv, inputs/analysis_var.tsv, inputs/imputation.tsv, inputs/order.tsv, inputs/keep_vars.tsv). - The reference dataset for the imputed records is specified by the `dataset_add` argument. It should contain all expected combinations of variables. In this case, `advs_expected_obsv` is created by `crossing()` datasets `paramcd` and `avisit`, which includes all combinations of PARAMCD, AVISITN, and AVISIT. - The groups for which new records are added are specified by the `by_vars` argument. Here, one record should be added for each *subject* and *parameter*. Therefore, `by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD)` is specified. - The imputation method is specified using the `imputation` argument. In this case, records with missing analysis values *add* records from `dataset_ref` after the data are sorted by the variables in `by_vars` and by visit (`AVISITN` and `AVISIT`), as specified in the `order` argument. - Variables other than `analysis_var` and `by_vars` that require LOCF (Last-Observation- Carried-Forward handling (in this case, `PARAMN`) are specified in the `keep_vars` argument. library(dplyr) library(tibble) library(tidyr) advs <- tribble( ~STUDYID, ~USUBJID, ~VSSEQ, ~PARAMCD, ~PARAMN, ~AVAL, ~AVISITN, ~AVISIT, "CDISC01", "01-701-1015", 1, "PULSE", 1, 65, 0, "BASELINE", "CDISC01", "01-701-1015", 2, "DIABP", 2, 79, 0, "BASELINE", "CDISC01", "01-701-1015", 3, "DIABP", 2, 80, 2, "WEEK 2", "CDISC01", "01-701-1015", 4, "DIABP", 2, NA, 4, "WEEK 4", "CDISC01", "01-701-1015", 5, "DIABP", 2, NA, 6, "WEEK 6", "CDISC01", "01-701-1015", 6, "SYSBP", 3, 130, 0, "BASELINE", "CDISC01", "01-701-1015", 7, "SYSBP", 3, 132, 2, "WEEK 2" ) paramcd <- tribble( ~PARAMCD, "PULSE", "DIABP", "SYSBP" ) avisit <- tribble( ~AVISITN, ~AVISIT, 0, "BASELINE", 2, "WEEK 2", 4, "WEEK 4", 6, "WEEK 6" ) advs_expected_obsv <- paramcd %>% crossing(avisit) derive_locf_records( dataset = advs, dataset_ref = advs_expected_obsv, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID, PARAMCD), imputation = "add", order = exprs(AVISITN, AVISIT), keep_vars = exprs(PARAMN) ) |> arrange(USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVISIT) are updated using values from the last record after the dataset is sorted by `by_vars` and `order`. For the new observations, `analysis_var` is set to the non-missing `analysis_var` of the previous observation in the input dataset (when sorted by `order`) and `DTYPE` is set to "LOCF". The `imputation` argument decides whether to update the existing observation when `analysis_var` is `NA` (`"update"` and `"update_add"`), or to add a new observation from `dataset_ref` instead (`"add"`).
```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(rlang)
library(purrr)

#--- Helper to read single-column TSVs as character vectors --------------------
read_single_col_tsv <- function(path) {
  df <- readr::read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
  if (ncol(df) != 1L) {
    stop("File ", path, " must have exactly one column.")
  }
  df[[1]]
}

#--- Create outputs directory if needed ----------------------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#--- Read input datasets -------------------------------------------------------
dataset      <- readr::read_tsv("inputs/dataset_ref.tsv",
                                show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
dataset_ref  <- readr::read_tsv("inputs/dataset_ref.tsv",
                                show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)

analysis_var_vec <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/analysis_var.tsv")
by_vars_vec      <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv")
id_vars_ref_vec  <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/id_vars_ref.tsv")
keep_vars_vec    <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/keep_vars.tsv")
order_vec        <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/order.tsv")

#--- Basic validation ----------------------------------------------------------
if (!length(analysis_var_vec) %in% c(1L)) {
  stop("analysis_var.tsv must contain exactly one analysis variable.")
}
analysis_var <- analysis_var_vec[[1]]

missing_vars_dataset <- setdiff(
  unique(c(analysis_var, by_vars_vec, order_vec, keep_vars_vec)),
  names(dataset)
)
if (length(missing_vars_dataset) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset: ", paste(missing_vars_dataset, collapse = ", "))
}

missing_vars_ref <- setdiff(
  unique(c(id_vars_ref_vec, by_vars_vec, order_vec, keep_vars_vec)),
  names(dataset_ref)
)
if (length(missing_vars_ref) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset_ref: ", paste(missing_vars_ref, collapse = ", "))
}

#--- Build quosures for admiral arguments --------------------------------------
by_vars_expr   <- rlang::syms(by_vars_vec)
id_vars_ref_expr <- rlang::syms(id_vars_ref_vec)
order_expr     <- rlang::syms(order_vec)
keep_vars_expr <- rlang::syms(keep_vars_vec)

#--- Derive LOCF records: imputation = "add" -----------------------------------
locf_add <- derive_locf_records(
  dataset      = dataset,
  dataset_ref  = dataset_ref,
  by_vars      = by_vars_expr,
  id_vars_ref  = id_vars_ref_expr,
  analysis_var = analysis_var,
  imputation   = "add",
  order        = order_expr,
  keep_vars    = keep_vars_expr
)

#--- Derive LOCF records: imputation = "update" --------------------------------
locf_update <- derive_locf_records(
  dataset      = dataset,
  dataset_ref  = dataset_ref,
  by_vars      = by_vars_expr,
  id_vars_ref  = id_vars_ref_expr,
  analysis_var = analysis_var,
  imputation   = "update",
  order        = order_expr,
  keep_vars    = keep_vars_expr
)

#--- Derive LOCF records: imputation = "update_add" ----------------------------
locf_update_add <- derive_locf_records(
  dataset      = dataset,
  dataset_ref  = dataset_ref,
  by_vars      = by_vars_expr,
  id_vars_ref  = id_vars_ref_expr,
  analysis_var = analysis_var,
  imputation   = "update_add",
  order        = order_expr,
  keep_vars    = keep_vars_expr
)

#--- Save outputs --------------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(locf_add,        "outputs/locf_add.csv")
write_csv(locf_update,     "outputs/locf_update.csv")
write_csv(locf_update_add, "outputs/locf_update_add.csv")

saveRDS(locf_add,        "outputs/locf_add.rds")
saveRDS(locf_update,     "outputs/locf_update.rds")
saveRDS(locf_update_add, "outputs/locf_update_add.rds")

#--- Basic post-write checks ---------------------------------------------------
check_written <- function(obj, csv_path, rds_path) {
  if (!file.exists(csv_path)) stop("CSV not written: ", csv_path)
  if (!file.exists(rds_path)) stop("RDS not written: ", rds_path)

  df_csv <- readr::read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
  obj2   <- readRDS(rds_path)

  if (!all(names(df_csv) == names(obj))) {
    warning("Column names differ between in-memory object and CSV: ", csv_path)
  }
  if (nrow(df_csv) != nrow(obj)) {
    warning("Row count mismatch for CSV: ", csv_path,
            " (in-memory: ", nrow(obj), ", CSV: ", nrow(df_csv), ")")
  }
  if (!all(names(obj2) == names(obj))) {
    warning("Column names differ between in-memory object and RDS: ", rds_path)
  }
  if (nrow(obj2) != nrow(obj)) {
    warning("Row count mismatch for RDS: ", rds_path,
            " (in-memory: ", nrow(obj), ", RDS: ", nrow(obj2), ")")
  }
}

check_written(locf_add,        "outputs/locf_add.csv",        "outputs/locf_add.rds")
check_written(locf_update,     "outputs/locf_update.csv",     "outputs/locf_update.rds")
check_written(locf_update_add, "outputs/locf_update_add.csv", "outputs/locf_update_add.rds")
```

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(rlang)
library(purrr)

#--- Helper to read single-column TSVs as character vectors --------------------
read_single_col_tsv <- function(path) {
  df <- readr::read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
  if (ncol(df) != 1L) {
    stop("File ", path, " must have exactly one column.")
  }
  df[[1]]
}

#--- Create outputs directory if needed ----------------------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#--- Read input datasets -------------------------------------------------------
dataset      <- readr::read_tsv("inputs/dataset_ref.tsv",
                                show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
dataset_ref  <- readr::read_tsv("inputs/dataset_ref.tsv",
                                show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)

analysis_var_vec <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/analysis_var.tsv")
by_vars_vec      <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv")
id_vars_ref_vec  <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/id_vars_ref.tsv")
keep_vars_vec    <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/keep_vars.tsv")
order_vec        <- read_single_col_tsv("inputs/order.tsv")

#--- Basic validation ----------------------------------------------------------
if (!length(analysis_var_vec) %in% c(1L)) {
  stop("analysis_var.tsv must contain exactly one analysis variable.")
}
analysis_var <- analysis_var_vec[[1]]

missing_vars_dataset <- setdiff(
  unique(c(analysis_var, by_vars_vec, order_vec, keep_vars_vec)),
  names(dataset)
)
if (length(missing_vars_dataset) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset: ", paste(missing_vars_dataset, collapse = ", "))
}

missing_vars_ref <- setdiff(
  unique(c(id_vars_ref_vec, by_vars_vec, order_vec, keep_vars_vec)),
  names(dataset_ref)
)
if (length(missing_vars_ref) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset_ref: ", paste(missing_vars_ref, collapse = ", "))
}

#--- Build quosures for admiral arguments --------------------------------------
by_vars_expr   <- rlang::syms(by_vars_vec)
id_vars_ref_expr <- rlang::syms(id_vars_ref_vec)
order_expr     <- rlang::syms(order_vec)
keep_vars_expr <- rlang::syms(keep_vars_vec)

#--- Derive LOCF records: imputation = "add" -----------------------------------
locf_add <- derive_locf_records(
  dataset      = dataset,
  dataset_ref  = dataset_ref,
  by_vars      = by_vars_expr,
  id_vars_ref  = id_vars_ref_expr,
  analysis_var = analysis_var,
  imputation   = "add",
  order        = order_expr,
  keep_vars    = keep_vars_expr
)

#--- Derive LOCF records: imputation = "update" --------------------------------
locf_update <- derive_locf_records(
  dataset      = dataset,
  dataset_ref  = dataset_ref,
  by_vars      = by_vars_expr,
  id_vars_ref  = id_vars_ref_expr,
  analysis_var = analysis_var,
  imputation   = "update",
  order        = order_expr,
  keep_vars    = keep_vars_expr
)

#--- Derive LOCF records: imputation = "update_add" ----------------------------
locf_update_add <- derive_locf_records(
  dataset      = dataset,
  dataset_ref  = dataset_ref,
  by_vars      = by_vars_expr,
  id_vars_ref  = id_vars_ref_expr,
  analysis_var = analysis_var,
  imputation   = "update_add",
  order        = order_expr,
  keep_vars    = keep_vars_expr
)

#--- Save outputs --------------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(locf_add,        "outputs/locf_add.csv")
write_csv(locf_update,     "outputs/locf_update.csv")
write_csv(locf_update_add, "outputs/locf_update_add.csv")

saveRDS(locf_add,        "outputs/locf_add.rds")
saveRDS(locf_update,     "outputs/locf_update.rds")
saveRDS(locf_update_add, "outputs/locf_update_add.rds")

#--- Basic post-write checks ---------------------------------------------------
check_written <- function(obj, csv_path, rds_path) {
  if (!file.exists(csv_path)) stop("CSV not written: ", csv_path)
  if (!file.exists(rds_path)) stop("RDS not written: ", rds_path)

  df_csv <- readr::read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
  obj2   <- readRDS(rds_path)

  if (!all(names(df_csv) == names(obj))) {
    warning("Column names differ between in-memory object and CSV: ", csv_path)
  }
  if (nrow(df_csv) != nrow(obj)) {
    warning("Row count mismatch for CSV: ", csv_path,
            " (in-memory: ", nrow(obj), ", CSV: ", nrow(df_csv), ")")
  }
  if (!all(names(obj2) == names(obj))) {
    warning("Column names differ between in-memory object and RDS: ", rds_path)
  }
  if (nrow(obj2) != nrow(obj)) {
    warning("Row count mismatch for RDS: ", rds_path,
            " (in-memory: ", nrow(obj), ", RDS: ", nrow(obj2), ")")
  }
}

check_written(locf_add,        "outputs/locf_add.csv",        "outputs/locf_add.rds")
check_written(locf_update,     "outputs/locf_update.csv",     "outputs/locf_update.rds")
check_written(locf_update_add, "outputs/locf_update_add.csv", "outputs/locf_update_add.rds")

prompt:
Write R code to adds a parameter for corrected qt (an ecg measurement) using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/by_vars.tsv, inputs/method.tsv, inputs/qt_code.tsv, inputs/rr_code.tsv, inputs/get_unit_expr.tsv). formula for each by group (e.g., subject and visit) where the source parameters are available. **Note:** This is a wrapper function for the more generic `derive_param_computed()`. and results in a character value Use admiral's derive_param_qtc function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("by_vars", "get_unit_expr"))` `PARAMCD`, and `AVAL` are expected as well.), by_vars (Grouping variables Only variables specified in `by_vars` will be populated in the newly created records.), method (Method used to QT correction See [compute_qtc()] for details.), qt_code (QT parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as the QT interval assessme.), rr_code (RR parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as the RR interval assessme.), get_unit_expr (An expression providing the unit of the parameter The result is used to check the units of the input parameters.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the new parameter added. Note, a variable will only be populated in the new parameter rows if it is specified in `by_vars`.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/weight_code.tsv, inputs/height_code.tsv, inputs/constant_by_vars.tsv). (e.g., subject and visit) where the source parameters are available. **Note:** This is a wrapper function for the more generic `derive_param_computed()`. Additional details: The analysis value of the new parameter is derived as \deqn{BMI = \frac{WEIGHT}{HEIGHT^2}} Use admiral's derive_param_bmi function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("by_vars"))` `PARAMCD`, and `AVAL` are expected as well.), weight_code (WEIGHT parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as the WEIGHT.), height_code (HEIGHT parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as the HEIGHT.), constant_by_vars (By variables for when HEIGHT is constant When HEIGHT is constant, the HEIGHT parameters (measured only once) are merged .). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the new parameter added. Note, a variable will only be populated in the new parameter rows if it is specified in `by_vars`.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Write R code to adds a parameter for mean arterial pressure using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/sysbp_code.tsv, inputs/diabp_code.tsv, inputs/hr_code.tsv, inputs/set_values_to.tsv). (e.g., subject and visit) where the source parameters are available. **Note:** This is a wrapper function for the more generic `derive_param_computed()`. Additional details: The analysis value of the new parameter is derived as \deqn{\frac{2DIABP + SYSBP}{3}}{(2DIABP + SYSBP) / 3} if it is based on diastolic and systolic blood pressure and \deqn{DIABP + 0.01 e^{4.14 - \frac{40.74}{HR}} (SYSBP - DIABP)}{ DIABP + 0.01 exp(4.14 - 40.74 / HR) (SYSBP - DIABP)} if it is based on diastolic, systolic blood pressure, and heart rate. Use admiral's derive_param_map function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("by_vars"))` `PARAMCD`, and `AVAL` are expected as well.), sysbp_code (Systolic blood pressure parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as the.), diabp_code (Diastolic blood pressure parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as th.), hr_code (Heart rate parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as the heart rate a.), set_values_to (Variables to be set The specified variables are set to the specified values for the new observations.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the new parameter added. Note, a variable will only be populated in the new parameter rows if it is specified in `by_vars`.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/hr_code.tsv). subject and visit) where the source parameters are available. **Note:** This is a wrapper function for the more generic `derive_param_computed()`. The analysis value of the new parameter is derived as \deqn{\frac{60000}{HR}}{60000 / HR} Use admiral's derive_param_rr function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("by_vars"))` `PARAMCD`, and `AVAL` are expected as well.), hr_code (HR parameter code The observations where `PARAMCD` equals the specified value are considered as the heart rate assessmen.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the new parameter added. Note, a variable will only be populated in the new parameter rows if it is specified in `by_vars`.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects).
