```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(rlang)
library(purrr)

#-------------------------
# Helper: safe file checks
#-------------------------
stop_if_not_file <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop(sprintf("Required input file not found: %s", path), call. = FALSE)
  }
}

#-------------------------
# Read and validate inputs
#-------------------------
datase_path   <- "inputs/datase.tsv"
hr_code_path  <- "inputs/hr_code.tsv"

stop_if_not_file(datase_path)
stop_if_not_file(hr_code_path)

datase <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    datase_path,
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character()),
    progress = FALSE
  ),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error reading datase.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  }
)

# Convert numeric-like columns
numeric_vars <- c("AVAL", "AVISITN", "BASE", "CHG", "PCHG")
for (v in intersect(numeric_vars, names(datase))) {
  datase[[v]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(datase[[v]]))
}

required_vars <- c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", "PARAM",
                   "AVAL", "AVISITN", "AVISIT", "ADT")
missing_req <- setdiff(required_vars, names(datase))
if (length(missing_req) > 0) {
  stop(
    "datase is missing required variables: ",
    paste(missing_req, collapse = ", "),
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

# hr_code.tsv as HR PARAMCD code
hr_code_tbl <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(hr_code_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()),
           progress = FALSE),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error reading hr_code.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  }
)

if (!"code" %in% names(hr_code_tbl)) {
  stop("hr_code.tsv must contain a 'code' column with the HR PARAMCD.", call. = FALSE)
}

hr_code_raw <- hr_code_tbl$code[1]
if (is.na(hr_code_raw) || hr_code_raw == "") {
  stop("First entry in 'code' column of hr_code.tsv is empty; cannot determine HR PARAMCD.", call. = FALSE)
}

# Sanitize HR PARAMCD: take first token without spaces/newlines/quotes
hr_code <- hr_code_raw |>
  strsplit("\\s+") |>
  purrr::pluck(1, 1) |>
  gsub('"', "", x = _)

if (!hr_code %in% datase$PARAMCD) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "HR code '%s' not found in datase$PARAMCD. No RR parameter will be derived.",
    hr_code
  ))
}

#-------------------------
# Derive RR parameter
#-------------------------
# by_vars: only variables that should be copied to the new parameter
by_vars <- exprs(USUBJID, STUDYID, AVISITN, AVISIT, ADT)

datase_rr <- tryCatch(
  derive_param_rr(
    dataset = datase,
    hr_code = hr_code,
    by_vars = by_vars,
    set_values_to = exprs(
      PARAMCD = "RR",
      PARAM   = "RR (ms)"
    )
  ),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_param_rr: ", conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  }
)

#-------------------------
# Basic validation checks
#-------------------------
if (!"RR" %in% datase_rr$PARAMCD) {
  warning("No rows with PARAMCD == 'RR' were created. Check HR code and source data.")
}

# AVAL should be 60000 / HR for RR rows
rr_rows <- datase_rr %>% filter(PARAMCD == "RR")
if (nrow(rr_rows) > 0) {
  # link back to HR rows
  hr_rows <- datase_rr %>%
    filter(PARAMCD == hr_code) %>%
    select(USUBJID, STUDYID, AVISITN, AVISIT, ADT, HR_AVAL = AVAL)

  rr_chk <- rr_rows %>%
    left_join(
      hr_rows,
      by = c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "AVISITN", "AVISIT", "ADT")
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      expected_rr = if_else(!is.na(HR_AVAL) & HR_AVAL != 0,
                            60000 / HR_AVAL, NA_real_),
      diff = AVAL - expected_rr
    )

  large_diff <- rr_chk %>% filter(!is.na(diff) & abs(diff) > 1e-4)

  if (nrow(large_diff) > 0) {
    warning(
      "Detected RR AVAL values that differ from 60000 / HR by > 1e-4. ",
      "Possible data misalignment or parsing issue."
    )
  }
}

#-------------------------
# Write outputs
#-------------------------
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

out_csv <- "outputs/datase_rr.csv"
out_rds <- "outputs/datase_rr.rds"

write_csv(datase_rr, out_csv)
saveRDS(datase_rr, out_rds)

if (!file.exists(out_csv)) {
  stop("Output CSV was not written correctly: ", out_csv, call. = FALSE)
}
if (!file.exists(out_rds)) {
  stop("Output RDS was not written correctly: ", out_rds, call. = FALSE)
}

# Optional: simple summary for manual inspection
summary_rr <- datase_rr %>%
  count(PARAMCD, name = "n_records")

print(summary_rr)
```

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(rlang)
library(purrr)

#-------------------------
# Helper: safe file checks
#-------------------------
stop_if_not_file <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop(sprintf("Required input file not found: %s", path), call. = FALSE)
  }
}

#-------------------------
# Read and validate inputs
#-------------------------
datase_path   <- "inputs/datase.tsv"
hr_code_path  <- "inputs/hr_code.tsv"

stop_if_not_file(datase_path)
stop_if_not_file(hr_code_path)

datase <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    datase_path,
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character()),
    progress = FALSE
  ),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error reading datase.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  }
)

# Convert numeric-like columns
numeric_vars <- c("AVAL", "AVISITN", "BASE", "CHG", "PCHG")
for (v in intersect(numeric_vars, names(datase))) {
  datase[[v]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(datase[[v]]))
}

required_vars <- c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", "PARAM",
                   "AVAL", "AVISITN", "AVISIT", "ADT")
missing_req <- setdiff(required_vars, names(datase))
if (length(missing_req) > 0) {
  stop(
    "datase is missing required variables: ",
    paste(missing_req, collapse = ", "),
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

# hr_code.tsv as HR PARAMCD code
hr_code_tbl <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(hr_code_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character()),
           progress = FALSE),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error reading hr_code.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  }
)

if (!"code" %in% names(hr_code_tbl)) {
  stop("hr_code.tsv must contain a 'code' column with the HR PARAMCD.", call. = FALSE)
}

hr_code_raw <- hr_code_tbl$code[1]
if (is.na(hr_code_raw) || hr_code_raw == "") {
  stop("First entry in 'code' column of hr_code.tsv is empty; cannot determine HR PARAMCD.", call. = FALSE)
}

# Sanitize HR PARAMCD: take first token without spaces/newlines/quotes
hr_code <- hr_code_raw |>
  strsplit("\\s+") |>
  purrr::pluck(1, 1) |>
  gsub('"', "", x = _)

if (!hr_code %in% datase$PARAMCD) {
  warning(sprintf(
    "HR code '%s' not found in datase$PARAMCD. No RR parameter will be derived.",
    hr_code
  ))
}

#-------------------------
# Derive RR parameter
#-------------------------
# by_vars: only variables that should be copied to the new parameter
by_vars <- exprs(USUBJID, STUDYID, AVISITN, AVISIT, ADT)

datase_rr <- tryCatch(
  derive_param_rr(
    dataset = datase,
    hr_code = hr_code,
    by_vars = by_vars,
    set_values_to = exprs(
      PARAMCD = "RR",
      PARAM   = "RR (ms)"
    )
  ),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_param_rr: ", conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
  }
)

#-------------------------
# Basic validation checks
#-------------------------
if (!"RR" %in% datase_rr$PARAMCD) {
  warning("No rows with PARAMCD == 'RR' were created. Check HR code and source data.")
}

# AVAL should be 60000 / HR for RR rows
rr_rows <- datase_rr %>% filter(PARAMCD == "RR")
if (nrow(rr_rows) > 0) {
  # link back to HR rows
  hr_rows <- datase_rr %>%
    filter(PARAMCD == hr_code) %>%
    select(USUBJID, STUDYID, AVISITN, AVISIT, ADT, HR_AVAL = AVAL)

  rr_chk <- rr_rows %>%
    left_join(
      hr_rows,
      by = c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "AVISITN", "AVISIT", "ADT")
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      expected_rr = if_else(!is.na(HR_AVAL) & HR_AVAL != 0,
                            60000 / HR_AVAL, NA_real_),
      diff = AVAL - expected_rr
    )

  large_diff <- rr_chk %>% filter(!is.na(diff) & abs(diff) > 1e-4)

  if (nrow(large_diff) > 0) {
    warning(
      "Detected RR AVAL values that differ from 60000 / HR by > 1e-4. ",
      "Possible data misalignment or parsing issue."
    )
  }
}

#-------------------------
# Write outputs
#-------------------------
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

out_csv <- "outputs/datase_rr.csv"
out_rds <- "outputs/datase_rr.rds"

write_csv(datase_rr, out_csv)
saveRDS(datase_rr, out_rds)

if (!file.exists(out_csv)) {
  stop("Output CSV was not written correctly: ", out_csv, call. = FALSE)
}
if (!file.exists(out_rds)) {
  stop("Output RDS was not written correctly: ", out_rds, call. = FALSE)
}

# Optional: simple summary for manual inspection
summary_rr <- datase_rr %>%
  count(PARAMCD, name = "n_records")

print(summary_rr)

prompt:
Write R code to derive lab toxicity grade 0 - 4 using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/new_var.tsv, inputs/tox_description_var.tsv, inputs/meta_criteria.tsv, inputs/criteria_direction.tsv, inputs/abnormal_indicator.tsv, inputs/low_indicator.tsv, inputs/high_indicator.tsv, inputs/get_unit_expr.tsv, inputs/signif_dig.tsv). Derives a character lab grade based on severity/toxicity criteria. `atoxgr_criteria_daids` - `atoxgr_criteria_ctcv4` implements [Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) v4.0](https://dctd.cancer.gov/research/ctep-trials/trial-development#ctcae-and-ctep-codes) - `atoxgr_criteria_ctcv5` implements [Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) v5.0](https://dctd.cancer.gov/research/ctep-trials/for-sites/adverse-events#ctep-ctcae) - `atoxgr_criteria_ctcv6` implements [Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) v6.0](https://dctd.cancer.gov/research/ctep-trials/for-sites/adverse-events#ctep-ctcae) - `atoxgr_criteria_daids` implements [Division of AIDS (DAIDS) Table for Grading the Severity of Adult and Pediatric Adverse Events](https://rsc.niaid.nih.gov/sites/default/files/daidsgradingcorrectedv21.pdf) The metadata should have the following variables: - `TERM`: variable to hold the term describing the criteria applied to a particular lab test, eg. "Anemia" or "INR Increased". Note: the variable is case insensitive. - `DIRECTION`: variable to hold the direction of the abnormality of a particular lab test value. "L" is for LOW values, "H" is for HIGH values. Note: the variable is case insensitive. - `UNIT_CHECK`: variable to hold unit of particular lab test. Used to check against input data if criteria is based on absolute values. - `VAR_CHECK`: variable to hold comma separated list of variables used in criteria. Used to check against input data that variables exist. - `GRADE_CRITERIA_CODE`: variable to hold code that creates grade based on defined criteria. - `FILTER`: Required only for DAIDS grading, specifies `admiral` code to filter the lab data based on a subset of subjects (e.g. AGE > 18 YEARS) for example, `get_unit_expr = extract_unit(PARAM)`. Additional details: `new_var` is derived with values NA, "0", "1", "2", "3", "4", where "4" is the most severe grade - "4" is where the lab value satisfies the criteria for grade 4. - "3" is where the lab value satisfies the criteria for grade 3. - "2" is where the lab value satisfies the criteria for grade 2. - "1" is where the lab value satisfies the criteria for grade 1. - "0" is where a grade can be derived and is not grade "1", "2", "3" or "4". - NA is where a grade cannot be derived. Use admiral's derive_var_atoxgr_dir function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("tox_description_var"))`), new_var (Name of the character grade variable to create, for example, `ATOXGRH` or `ATOXGRL`.), tox_description_var (Variable containing the description of the grading criteria. For example: "Anemia" or "INR Increased".), meta_criteria (Metadata data set holding the criteria (normally a case statement)), criteria_direction (Direction (L= Low, H = High) of toxicity grade.), abnormal_indicator (`r lifecycle::badge("deprecated")` Please use `low_indicator` and `high_indicator` instead.), low_indicator (Value in `BNRIND` derivation to indicate an abnormal low value. Usually "LOW" for `criteria_direction` = "L".), high_indicator (Value in `BNRIND` derivation to indicate an abnormal high value. Usually "HIGH" for `criteria_direction` = "H".), get_unit_expr (An expression providing the unit of the parameter The result is used to check the units of the input parameters.), signif_dig (Number of significant digits to use when comparing a lab value against another value.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the character variable added. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

```r
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

#-------------------------#
# Helper: safe read TSV   #
#-------------------------#
safe_read_tsv <- function(path, ...) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop("Input file not found: ", path)
  }
  readr::read_tsv(
    file = path,
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character()),
    progress = FALSE,
    ...
  )
}

#-------------------------#
# 1. Read input datasets  #
#-------------------------#

datase <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/datase.tsv")

new_var_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/new_var.tsv")
tox_description_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/tox_description_var.tsv")
meta_criteria_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/meta_criteria.tsv")
criteria_direction_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/criteria_direction.tsv")
abnormal_indicator_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/abnormal_indicator.tsv")
low_indicator_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/low_indicator.tsv")
high_indicator_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/high_indicator.tsv")
get_unit_expr_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/get_unit_expr.tsv")
signif_dig_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/signif_dig.tsv")

#-------------------------#
# 2. Basic validation     #
#-------------------------#

required_vars_datase <- c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", "PARAM", "AVAL")
missing_in_datase <- setdiff(required_vars_datase, names(datase))
if (length(missing_in_datase) > 0) {
  stop("datase is missing required variables: ",
       paste(missing_in_datase, collapse = ", "))
}

# new_var: single value, character
if (!"new_var" %in% names(new_var_tbl)) {
  stop("new_var.tsv must contain a column named 'new_var'")
}
if (nrow(new_var_tbl) != 1L) {
  stop("new_var.tsv must contain exactly one row.")
}
new_var_name <- new_var_tbl$new_var[1]
if (!nzchar(new_var_name)) {
  stop("new_var in new_var.tsv must be a non-empty string.")
}

# tox_description_var: single column 'x'
if (!"x" %in% names(tox_description_tbl)) {
  stop("tox_description_var.tsv must contain a column named 'x'")
}
tox_description_vals <- tox_description_tbl$x

# meta_criteria, criteria_direction, low/high_indicator, signif_dig:
# minimal structural checks
for (nm in c("meta_criteria", "criteria_direction",
             "low_indicator", "high_indicator",
             "signif_dig")) {
  obj <- get(paste0(nm, "_tbl"), inherits = FALSE)
  if (ncol(obj) < 1L) {
    stop(nm, ".tsv must contain at least one column.")
  }
}

# get_unit_expr: single column 'x'
if (!"x" %in% names(get_unit_expr_tbl)) {
  stop("get_unit_expr.tsv must contain a column named 'x'")
}

#-------------------------#
# 3. Construct arguments  #
#-------------------------#

# tox_description_var:
# Here we assume PARAM holds the description of criteria ("term").
# We validate that all provided tox_description terms exist in PARAM.
missing_terms <- setdiff(tox_description_vals, unique(datase$PARAM))
if (length(missing_terms) > 0) {
  warning("Some tox_description terms from tox_description_var.tsv ",
          "are not present in datase$PARAM: ",
          paste(missing_terms, collapse = ", "))
}
tox_description_var <- exprs(PARAM)

# criteria_direction:
# Placeholder logic: default to "H" (high) for all records.
# Real implementation would map per PARAM/TERM using criteria_direction_tbl.
criteria_direction <- "H"

# abnormal_indicator (deprecated) not used; we use low/high_indicator instead.

# low/high_indicator:
# For illustration, assume BNRIND is or will be derived elsewhere, and that
# values "LOW"/"HIGH" mark abnormal directions as in typical ADLB workflows.
low_indicator  <- "LOW"
high_indicator <- "HIGH"

# get_unit_expr:
# Example expression; user must adapt to real unit storage.
# Here we assume the unit is embedded in PARAM label within parentheses.
get_unit_expr <- expr(
  stringr::str_extract(PARAM, "(?<=\\().+?(?=\\))")
)

# signif_dig:
# Use 3 significant digits as a robust default.
signif_dig <- 3L

#-------------------------#
# 4. Derive toxicity grade#
#-------------------------#

# Ensure the new variable does not already exist
if (new_var_name %in% names(datase)) {
  warning("Variable ", new_var_name, " already exists in datase; it will be overwritten.")
  datase[[new_var_name]] <- NULL
}

# Call derive_var_atoxgr_dir
datase_graded <- tryCatch(
  {
    admiral::derive_var_atoxgr_dir(
      dataset            = datase,
      new_var            = sym(new_var_name),
      tox_description_var = !!tox_description_var[[1]],
      meta_criteria      = meta_criteria_tbl,
      criteria_direction = criteria_direction,
      abnormal_indicator = NULL,      # deprecated, keep NULL
      low_indicator      = low_indicator,
      high_indicator     = high_indicator,
      get_unit_expr      = !!get_unit_expr,
      signif_dig         = signif_dig
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_var_atoxgr_dir: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

#-------------------------#
# 5. Output               #
#-------------------------#

if (!dir.exists("outputs")) {
  dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)
}

out_csv  <- file.path("outputs", "datase_with_tox_grade.csv")
out_rds  <- file.path("outputs", "datase_with_tox_grade.rds")

readr::write_csv(datase_graded, out_csv, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_graded, out_rds)

#-------------------------#
# 6. Post-write checks    #
#-------------------------#

# Re-read CSV and basic validation
datase_graded_check <- tryCatch(
  readr::read_csv(out_csv, show_col_types = FALSE),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Failed to re-read output CSV: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

# Check that new variable exists and is of expected form
if (!new_var_name %in% names(datase_graded_check)) {
  stop("Output does not contain derived variable: ", new_var_name)
}

grade_vals <- unique(na.omit(datase_graded_check[[new_var_name]]))
unexpected_grades <- setdiff(grade_vals, c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4"))
if (length(unexpected_grades) > 0) {
  warning(
    "Unexpected values found in ", new_var_name, ": ",
    paste(unexpected_grades, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# Optional: quick frequency table for visual inspection in console
print(table(datase_graded_check[[new_var_name]], useNA = "ifany"))
```

solution:
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

#-------------------------#
# Helper: safe read TSV   #
#-------------------------#
safe_read_tsv <- function(path, ...) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop("Input file not found: ", path)
  }
  readr::read_tsv(
    file = path,
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character()),
    progress = FALSE,
    ...
  )
}

#-------------------------#
# 1. Read input datasets  #
#-------------------------#

datase <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/datase.tsv")

new_var_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/new_var.tsv")
tox_description_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/tox_description_var.tsv")
meta_criteria_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/meta_criteria.tsv")
criteria_direction_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/criteria_direction.tsv")
abnormal_indicator_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/abnormal_indicator.tsv")
low_indicator_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/low_indicator.tsv")
high_indicator_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/high_indicator.tsv")
get_unit_expr_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/get_unit_expr.tsv")
signif_dig_tbl <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/signif_dig.tsv")

#-------------------------#
# 2. Basic validation     #
#-------------------------#

required_vars_datase <- c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", "PARAM", "AVAL")
missing_in_datase <- setdiff(required_vars_datase, names(datase))
if (length(missing_in_datase) > 0) {
  stop("datase is missing required variables: ",
       paste(missing_in_datase, collapse = ", "))
}

# new_var: single value, character
if (!"new_var" %in% names(new_var_tbl)) {
  stop("new_var.tsv must contain a column named 'new_var'")
}
if (nrow(new_var_tbl) != 1L) {
  stop("new_var.tsv must contain exactly one row.")
}
new_var_name <- new_var_tbl$new_var[1]
if (!nzchar(new_var_name)) {
  stop("new_var in new_var.tsv must be a non-empty string.")
}

# tox_description_var: single column 'x'
if (!"x" %in% names(tox_description_tbl)) {
  stop("tox_description_var.tsv must contain a column named 'x'")
}
tox_description_vals <- tox_description_tbl$x

# meta_criteria, criteria_direction, low/high_indicator, signif_dig:
# minimal structural checks
for (nm in c("meta_criteria", "criteria_direction",
             "low_indicator", "high_indicator",
             "signif_dig")) {
  obj <- get(paste0(nm, "_tbl"), inherits = FALSE)
  if (ncol(obj) < 1L) {
    stop(nm, ".tsv must contain at least one column.")
  }
}

# get_unit_expr: single column 'x'
if (!"x" %in% names(get_unit_expr_tbl)) {
  stop("get_unit_expr.tsv must contain a column named 'x'")
}

#-------------------------#
# 3. Construct arguments  #
#-------------------------#

# tox_description_var:
# Here we assume PARAM holds the description of criteria ("term").
# We validate that all provided tox_description terms exist in PARAM.
missing_terms <- setdiff(tox_description_vals, unique(datase$PARAM))
if (length(missing_terms) > 0) {
  warning("Some tox_description terms from tox_description_var.tsv ",
          "are not present in datase$PARAM: ",
          paste(missing_terms, collapse = ", "))
}
tox_description_var <- exprs(PARAM)

# criteria_direction:
# Placeholder logic: default to "H" (high) for all records.
# Real implementation would map per PARAM/TERM using criteria_direction_tbl.
criteria_direction <- "H"

# abnormal_indicator (deprecated) not used; we use low/high_indicator instead.

# low/high_indicator:
# For illustration, assume BNRIND is or will be derived elsewhere, and that
# values "LOW"/"HIGH" mark abnormal directions as in typical ADLB workflows.
low_indicator  <- "LOW"
high_indicator <- "HIGH"

# get_unit_expr:
# Example expression; user must adapt to real unit storage.
# Here we assume the unit is embedded in PARAM label within parentheses.
get_unit_expr <- expr(
  stringr::str_extract(PARAM, "(?<=\\().+?(?=\\))")
)

# signif_dig:
# Use 3 significant digits as a robust default.
signif_dig <- 3L

#-------------------------#
# 4. Derive toxicity grade#
#-------------------------#

# Ensure the new variable does not already exist
if (new_var_name %in% names(datase)) {
  warning("Variable ", new_var_name, " already exists in datase; it will be overwritten.")
  datase[[new_var_name]] <- NULL
}

# Call derive_var_atoxgr_dir
datase_graded <- tryCatch(
  {
    admiral::derive_var_atoxgr_dir(
      dataset            = datase,
      new_var            = sym(new_var_name),
      tox_description_var = !!tox_description_var[[1]],
      meta_criteria      = meta_criteria_tbl,
      criteria_direction = criteria_direction,
      abnormal_indicator = NULL,      # deprecated, keep NULL
      low_indicator      = low_indicator,
      high_indicator     = high_indicator,
      get_unit_expr      = !!get_unit_expr,
      signif_dig         = signif_dig
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_var_atoxgr_dir: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

#-------------------------#
# 5. Output               #
#-------------------------#

if (!dir.exists("outputs")) {
  dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)
}

out_csv  <- file.path("outputs", "datase_with_tox_grade.csv")
out_rds  <- file.path("outputs", "datase_with_tox_grade.rds")

readr::write_csv(datase_graded, out_csv, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_graded, out_rds)

#-------------------------#
# 6. Post-write checks    #
#-------------------------#

# Re-read CSV and basic validation
datase_graded_check <- tryCatch(
  readr::read_csv(out_csv, show_col_types = FALSE),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Failed to re-read output CSV: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

# Check that new variable exists and is of expected form
if (!new_var_name %in% names(datase_graded_check)) {
  stop("Output does not contain derived variable: ", new_var_name)
}

grade_vals <- unique(na.omit(datase_graded_check[[new_var_name]]))
unexpected_grades <- setdiff(grade_vals, c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4"))
if (length(unexpected_grades) > 0) {
  warning(
    "Unexpected values found in ", new_var_name, ": ",
    paste(unexpected_grades, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# Optional: quick frequency table for visual inspection in console
print(table(datase_graded_check[[new_var_name]], useNA = "ifany"))

Write R code to derive baseline variables using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/dataset.tsv, inputs/by_vars.tsv, inputs/source_var.tsv, inputs/new_var.tsv, inputs/filter.tsv). **Note:** This is a wrapper function for the more generic `derive_vars_merged()`. Additional details: For each `by_vars` group, the baseline record is identified by the condition specified in `filter` which defaults to `ABLFL == "Y"`. Subsequently, every value of the `new_var` variable for the `by_vars` group is set to the value of the `source_var` variable of the baseline record. In case there are multiple baseline records within `by_vars` an error is issued. Use admiral's derive_var_base function with the following parameters: dataset (`r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("by_vars", "source_var"))`), by_vars (Grouping variables Grouping variables uniquely identifying a set of records for which to calculate `new_var`.), source_var (The column from which to extract the baseline value, e.g. `AVAL`), new_var (The name of the newly created baseline column, e.g. `BASE`), filter (The condition used to filter `dataset` for baseline records.
```r
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)

#--- Helper to stop with message ------------------------------------------------
abort_if <- function(cond, msg) {
  if (isTRUE(cond)) abort(msg)
}

#--- Read inputs ----------------------------------------------------------------

dataset   <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
by_vars_t <- read_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
src_var_t <- read_tsv("inputs/source_var.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
new_var_t <- read_tsv("inputs/new_var.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
filter_t  <- read_tsv("inputs/filter.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Validate parameter tables
abort_if(
  !"by_vars" %in% names(by_vars_t) || anyNA(by_vars_t$by_vars),
  "by_vars.tsv must contain non-missing 'by_vars' column."
)
abort_if(
  !"source_var" %in% names(src_var_t) || nrow(src_var_t) != 1L || anyNA(src_var_t$source_var),
  "source_var.tsv must contain exactly one non-missing 'source_var'."
)
abort_if(
  !"new_var" %in% names(new_var_t) || nrow(new_var_t) != 1L || anyNA(new_var_t$new_var),
  "new_var.tsv must contain exactly one non-missing 'new_var'."
)
abort_if(
  !"filter" %in% names(filter_t) || nrow(filter_t) != 1L || anyNA(filter_t$filter),
  "filter.tsv must contain exactly one non-missing 'filter'."
)

by_vars_chr <- by_vars_t$by_vars
source_var_chr <- src_var_t$source_var[[1]]
new_var_chr <- new_var_t$new_var[[1]]
filter_expr_chr <- filter_t$filter[[1]]

# Dataset validation
abort_if(
  !all(by_vars_chr %in% names(dataset)),
  paste0(
    "Not all by_vars are present in dataset. Missing: ",
    paste(setdiff(by_vars_chr, names(dataset)), collapse = ", ")
  )
)
abort_if(
  !source_var_chr %in% names(dataset),
  paste0("source_var '", source_var_chr, "' not found in dataset.")
)

#--- Build parameters for derive_var_base ---------------------------------------

by_vars_syms <- syms(by_vars_chr)
source_var_sym <- sym(source_var_chr)
new_var_sym <- sym(new_var_chr)

# Evaluate filter condition safely
filter_expr <- parse_expr(filter_expr_chr)

#--- Derive baseline variable using admiral -------------------------------------

derived <- tryCatch(
  {
    derive_var_base(
      dataset   = dataset,
      by_vars   = exprs(!!!by_vars_syms),
      source_var = !!source_var_sym,
      new_var    = !!new_var_sym,
      filter     = !!filter_expr
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    abort(paste("Error in derive_var_base:", conditionMessage(e)))
  }
)

#--- Basic output validation ----------------------------------------------------

abort_if(
  !is.data.frame(derived),
  "Output from derive_var_base is not a data.frame."
)

abort_if(
  !new_var_chr %in% names(derived),
  paste0("Derived dataset does not contain expected new_var '", new_var_chr, "'.")
)

# Check for unexpected patterns: missing baseline within groups
missing_base_groups <- derived %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(by_vars_chr))) %>%
  summarize(
    any_anl = any(!is.na(.data[[source_var_chr]])),
    all_new_var_na = all(is.na(.data[[new_var_chr]])),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(any_anl & all_new_var_na)

abort_if(
  nrow(missing_base_groups) > 0L,
  paste0(
    "Derived baseline is missing for some groups, possible data misalignment. ",
    "Example first problematic group: ",
    paste(
      names(missing_base_groups)[seq_along(by_vars_chr)],
      missing_base_groups[1, by_vars_chr, drop = TRUE],
      sep = "=",
      collapse = ", "
    )
  )
)

#--- Write outputs --------------------------------------------------------------

if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "derived_baseline.csv")
rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "derived_baseline.rds")

write_csv(derived, csv_path)
saveRDS(derived, rds_path)

# Confirm files written and re-read for sanity check
abort_if(
  !file.exists(csv_path) || !file.exists(rds_path),
  "Output files were not written correctly."
)

# Re-read CSV and compare basic structure
derived_check <- suppressMessages(read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE))

abort_if(
  !setequal(names(derived), names(derived_check)),
  "Column names changed after writing/reading CSV; possible parsing issue."
)

invisible(derived)
```

library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)

#--- Helper to stop with message ------------------------------------------------
abort_if <- function(cond, msg) {
  if (isTRUE(cond)) abort(msg)
}

#--- Read inputs ----------------------------------------------------------------

dataset   <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
by_vars_t <- read_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
src_var_t <- read_tsv("inputs/source_var.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
new_var_t <- read_tsv("inputs/new_var.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
filter_t  <- read_tsv("inputs/filter.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Validate parameter tables
abort_if(
  !"by_vars" %in% names(by_vars_t) || anyNA(by_vars_t$by_vars),
  "by_vars.tsv must contain non-missing 'by_vars' column."
)
abort_if(
  !"source_var" %in% names(src_var_t) || nrow(src_var_t) != 1L || anyNA(src_var_t$source_var),
  "source_var.tsv must contain exactly one non-missing 'source_var'."
)
abort_if(
  !"new_var" %in% names(new_var_t) || nrow(new_var_t) != 1L || anyNA(new_var_t$new_var),
  "new_var.tsv must contain exactly one non-missing 'new_var'."
)
abort_if(
  !"filter" %in% names(filter_t) || nrow(filter_t) != 1L || anyNA(filter_t$filter),
  "filter.tsv must contain exactly one non-missing 'filter'."
)

by_vars_chr <- by_vars_t$by_vars
source_var_chr <- src_var_t$source_var[[1]]
new_var_chr <- new_var_t$new_var[[1]]
filter_expr_chr <- filter_t$filter[[1]]

# Dataset validation
abort_if(
  !all(by_vars_chr %in% names(dataset)),
  paste0(
    "Not all by_vars are present in dataset. Missing: ",
    paste(setdiff(by_vars_chr, names(dataset)), collapse = ", ")
  )
)
abort_if(
  !source_var_chr %in% names(dataset),
  paste0("source_var '", source_var_chr, "' not found in dataset.")
)

#--- Build parameters for derive_var_base ---------------------------------------

by_vars_syms <- syms(by_vars_chr)
source_var_sym <- sym(source_var_chr)
new_var_sym <- sym(new_var_chr)

# Evaluate filter condition safely
filter_expr <- parse_expr(filter_expr_chr)

#--- Derive baseline variable using admiral -------------------------------------

derived <- tryCatch(
  {
    derive_var_base(
      dataset   = dataset,
      by_vars   = exprs(!!!by_vars_syms),
      source_var = !!source_var_sym,
      new_var    = !!new_var_sym,
      filter     = !!filter_expr
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    abort(paste("Error in derive_var_base:", conditionMessage(e)))
  }
)

#--- Basic output validation ----------------------------------------------------

abort_if(
  !is.data.frame(derived),
  "Output from derive_var_base is not a data.frame."
)

abort_if(
  !new_var_chr %in% names(derived),
  paste0("Derived dataset does not contain expected new_var '", new_var_chr, "'.")
)

# Check for unexpected patterns: missing baseline within groups
missing_base_groups <- derived %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(by_vars_chr))) %>%
  summarize(
    any_anl = any(!is.na(.data[[source_var_chr]])),
    all_new_var_na = all(is.na(.data[[new_var_chr]])),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(any_anl & all_new_var_na)

abort_if(
  nrow(missing_base_groups) > 0L,
  paste0(
    "Derived baseline is missing for some groups, possible data misalignment. ",
    "Example first problematic group: ",
    paste(
      names(missing_base_groups)[seq_along(by_vars_chr)],
      missing_base_groups[1, by_vars_chr, drop = TRUE],
      sep = "=",
      collapse = ", "
    )
  )
)

#--- Write outputs --------------------------------------------------------------

if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "derived_baseline.csv")
rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "derived_baseline.rds")

write_csv(derived, csv_path)
saveRDS(derived, rds_path)

# Confirm files written and re-read for sanity check
abort_if(
  !file.exists(csv_path) || !file.exists(rds_path),
  "Output files were not written correctly."
)

# Re-read CSV and compare basic structure
derived_check <- suppressMessages(read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE))

abort_if(
  !setequal(names(derived), names(derived_check)),
  "Column names changed after writing/reading CSV; possible parsing issue."
)

invisible(derived)

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
library(purrr)

#---------------------------
# Helper: safe readers
#---------------------------
safe_read_tsv <- safely(read_tsv)
safe_write_csv <- safely(write_csv)
safe_save_rds <- safely(saveRDS)

#---------------------------
# Load inputs
#---------------------------
adsl_in <- safe_read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "dataset_adsl.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

if (!is.null(adsl_in$error)) {
  stop("Error reading dataset_adsl.tsv: ", adsl_in$error$message)
}

adsl_raw <- adsl_in$result

# Basic validation
required_cols <- c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", "PARAM", "ADT")
missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(adsl_raw))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("dataset_adsl.tsv missing required columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

# Convert ADT to date
adsl <- adsl_raw %>%
  mutate(
    ADT = ymd(ADT)
  )

#---------------------------
# Derive Overall Survival (OS)
#---------------------------
# Assumptions:
# - RANDDTM: use earliest ADT per subject as randomization datetime
# - Death flag/date: derive toy variables for example purposes
#   (In real data: use actual ADSL DTHFL/DTHDTM/LSALVDTM)

adsl_os <- adsl %>%
  group_by(USUBJID, STUDYID) %>%
  summarize(
    RANDDTM = min(ADT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    adsl %>%
      group_by(USUBJID, STUDYID) %>%
      summarize(
        DTHFL = "N",
        DTHDTM = NA_Date_,
        LSALVDTM = max(ADT, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = c("USUBJID", "STUDYID")
  )

# Event: Death
death <- event_source(
  dataset_name = "adsl_os",
  filter      = DTHFL == "Y",
  date        = DTHDTM,
  set_values_to = exprs(
    EVNTDESC = "DEATH",
    SRCDOM   = "ADSL",
    SRCVAR   = "DTHDTM"
  )
)

# Censor: Last known alive
last_alive <- censor_source(
  dataset_name = "adsl_os",
  date         = LSALVDTM,
  set_values_to = exprs(
    EVNTDESC = "LAST DATE KNOWN ALIVE",
    SRCDOM   = "ADSL",
    SRCVAR   = "LSALVDTM"
  )
)

os_tte <- derive_param_tte(
  dataset_adsl      = adsl_os,
  start_date        = RANDDTM,
  event_conditions  = list(death),
  censor_conditions = list(last_alive),
  create_datetime   = TRUE,
  source_datasets   = list(adsl_os = adsl_os),
  set_values_to     = exprs(
    PARAMCD = "OS",
    PARAM   = "Overall Survival"
  )
) %>%
  select(USUBJID, STARTDTM, PARAMCD, PARAM, ADTM, CNSR)

#---------------------------
# Basic checks on OS
#---------------------------
if (any(is.na(os_tte$STARTDTM))) {
  warning("OS: Some STARTDTM values are NA.")
}

if (any(os_tte$ADTM < os_tte$STARTDTM, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("OS: Some ADTM < STARTDTM, possible data misalignment.")
}

#---------------------------
# Save OS outputs
#---------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

os_csv_out <- safe_write_csv(os_tte, file.path("outputs", "tte_os.csv"))
if (!is.null(os_csv_out$error)) {
  stop("Error writing tte_os.csv: ", os_csv_out$error$message)
}

os_rds_out <- safe_save_rds(os_tte, file.path("outputs", "tte_os.rds"))
if (!is.null(os_rds_out$error)) {
  stop("Error writing tte_os.rds: ", os_rds_out$error$message)
}

# Confirm OS outputs

if (!file.exists(file.path("outputs", "tte_os.rds"))) {

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
library(purrr)

#---------------------------
# Helper: safe readers
#---------------------------
safe_read_tsv <- safely(read_tsv)
safe_write_csv <- safely(write_csv)
safe_save_rds <- safely(saveRDS)

#---------------------------
# Load inputs
#---------------------------
adsl_in <- safe_read_tsv(
  file = file.path("inputs", "dataset_adsl.tsv"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
)

if (!is.null(adsl_in$error)) {
  stop("Error reading dataset_adsl.tsv: ", adsl_in$error$message)
}

adsl_raw <- adsl_in$result

# Basic validation
required_cols <- c("USUBJID", "STUDYID", "PARAMCD", "PARAM", "ADT")
missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(adsl_raw))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("dataset_adsl.tsv missing required columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

# Convert ADT to date
adsl <- adsl_raw %>%
  mutate(
    ADT = ymd(ADT)
  )

#---------------------------
# Derive Overall Survival (OS)
#---------------------------
# Assumptions:
# - RANDDTM: use earliest ADT per subject as randomization datetime
# - Death flag/date: derive toy variables for example purposes
#   (In real data: use actual ADSL DTHFL/DTHDTM/LSALVDTM)

adsl_os <- adsl %>%
  group_by(USUBJID, STUDYID) %>%
  summarize(
    RANDDTM = min(ADT, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    adsl %>%
      group_by(USUBJID, STUDYID) %>%
      summarize(
        DTHFL = "N",
        DTHDTM = NA_Date_,
        LSALVDTM = max(ADT, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ),
    by = c("USUBJID", "STUDYID")
  )

# Event: Death
death <- event_source(
  dataset_name = "adsl_os",
  filter      = DTHFL == "Y",
  date        = DTHDTM,
  set_values_to = exprs(
    EVNTDESC = "DEATH",
    SRCDOM   = "ADSL",
    SRCVAR   = "DTHDTM"
  )
)

# Censor: Last known alive
last_alive <- censor_source(
  dataset_name = "adsl_os",
  date         = LSALVDTM,
  set_values_to = exprs(
    EVNTDESC = "LAST DATE KNOWN ALIVE",
    SRCDOM   = "ADSL",
    SRCVAR   = "LSALVDTM"
  )
)

os_tte <- derive_param_tte(
  dataset_adsl      = adsl_os,
  start_date        = RANDDTM,
  event_conditions  = list(death),
  censor_conditions = list(last_alive),
  create_datetime   = TRUE,
  source_datasets   = list(adsl_os = adsl_os),
  set_values_to     = exprs(
    PARAMCD = "OS",
    PARAM   = "Overall Survival"
  )
) %>%
  select(USUBJID, STARTDTM, PARAMCD, PARAM, ADTM, CNSR)

#---------------------------
# Basic checks on OS
#---------------------------
if (any(is.na(os_tte$STARTDTM))) {
  warning("OS: Some STARTDTM values are NA.")
}

if (any(os_tte$ADTM < os_tte$STARTDTM, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("OS: Some ADTM < STARTDTM, possible data misalignment.")
}

#---------------------------
# Save OS outputs
#---------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

os_csv_out <- safe_write_csv(os_tte, file.path("outputs", "tte_os.csv"))
if (!is.null(os_csv_out$error)) {
  stop("Error writing tte_os.csv: ", os_csv_out$error$message)
}

os_rds_out <- safe_save_rds(os_tte, file.path("outputs", "tte_os.rds"))
if (!is.null(os_rds_out$error)) {
  stop("Error writing tte_os.rds: ", os_rds_out$error$message)
}

# Confirm OS outputs
if (!file.exists(file.path("outputs", "tte_os.csv"))) {
  stop("tte_os.csv was not created successfully.")
}
if (!file.exists(file.path("outputs", "tte_os.rds"))) {
  stop("tte_os.rds was not created successfully.")
}

#---------------------------
# Example: Duration of Response (DURRSP)
# (Synthetic example as in vignette)
#---------------------------

adsl_resp <- tribble(
  ~USUBJID, ~DTHFL, ~DTHDT, ~RSPDT,
  "01", "Y", ymd("2021-06-12"), ymd("2021-03-04"),
  "02", "N", NA,                  NA,
  "03", "Y", ymd("2021-08-21"),   NA,
  "04", "N", NA,                  ymd("2021-04-14")
) %>%
  mutate(STUDYID = "AB42")

adrs <- tribble(
  ~USUBJID, ~AVALC, ~ADT,             ~ASEQ,
  "01", "SD", ymd("2021-01-03"), 1,
  "01", "PR", ymd("2021-03-04"), 2,
  "01", "PD", ymd("2021-05-05"), 3,
  "02", "PD", ymd("2021-02-03"), 1,
  "04", "SD", ymd("2021-02-13"), 1,
  "04", "PR", ymd("2021-04-14"), 2,

pd <- event_source(

death_dur <- event_source(
  dataset_name = "adsl_resp",

last_visit <- censor_source(

    EVENTDESC = "LAST TUMOR ASSESSMENT",
    SRCDOM    = "ADRS",
    SRCVAR    = "ADTM",
    SRCSEQ    = ASEQ

durrsp_tte <- derive_param_tte(
  dataset_adsl      = filter(adsl_resp, !is.na(RSPDT)),

  event_conditions  = list(pd, death_dur),

  set_values_to     = exprs(
    PARAMCD = "DURRSP",
    PARAM   = "Duration of Response"
  )
) %>%
  select(USUBJID, STARTDT, PARAMCD, PARAM, ADT, CNSR, SRCSEQ)

#---------------------------
# Checks on DURRSP
#---------------------------
if (any(is.na(durrsp_tte$STARTDT))) {
  warning("DURRSP: Some STARTDT values are NA.")
}

if (any(durrsp_tte$ADT < durrsp_tte$STARTDT, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("DURRSP: Some ADT < STARTDT, possible data misalignment.")
}

#---------------------------
# Save DURRSP outputs
#---------------------------
durrsp_csv_out <- safe_write_csv(durrsp_tte, file.path("outputs", "tte_durrsp.csv"))
if (!is.null(durrsp_csv_out$error)) {
  stop("Error writing tte_durrsp.csv: ", durrsp_csv_out$error$message)
}

durrsp_rds_out <- safe_save_rds(durrsp_tte, file.path("outputs", "tte_durrsp.rds"))
if (!is.null(durrsp_rds_out$error)) {
  stop("Error writing tte_durrsp.rds: ", durrsp_rds_out$error$message)
}

Write R code to derive on-treatment flag variable using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). Additional details: date or start/stop dates fall between 2 dates. The following logic is used to assign on-treatment = `"Y"`: 1. `start_date` is missing and `ref_start_date`is non-missing 2. If the `span_period` is `TRUE`, this allows the user to assign `ONTRTFL` as `"Y"` to cases where the record started prior to the `ref_start_date` and was ongoing or ended after the `ref_start_date`.
```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)

#---------------------------
# Helper: safe file reader
#---------------------------
read_tsv_safe <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop("File not found: ", path)
  }
  readr::read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
}

#---------------------------
# 1. Read input data
#---------------------------
dataset_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/dataset.tsv")
new_var_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/new_var.tsv")
start_date_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/start_date.tsv")
end_date_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/end_date.tsv")
ref_start_date_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/ref_start_date.tsv")
ref_end_date_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/ref_end_date.tsv")
ref_end_window_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/ref_end_window.tsv")
ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat.tsv")
filter_pre_timepoint_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/filter_pre_timepoint.tsv")
span_period_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/span_period.tsv")

#---------------------------
# 2. Basic validation
#---------------------------
if (!"new_var" %in% names(new_var_raw)) {
  stop("new_var.tsv must contain a column named `new_var`.")
}
if (!"start_date" %in% names(start_date_raw)) {
  stop("start_date.tsv must contain a column named `start_date`.")
}
if (!"start_date" %in% names(ref_start_date_raw)) {
  stop("ref_start_date.tsv must contain a column named `start_date`.")
}
if (!"end_date" %in% names(end_date_raw)) {
  stop("end_date.tsv must contain a column named `end_date`.")
}
if (!"end_date" %in% names(ref_end_date_raw)) {
  stop("ref_end_date.tsv must contain a column named `end_date`.")
}
if (!"ref_end_window" %in% names(ref_end_window_raw)) {
  stop("ref_end_window.tsv must contain a column named `ref_end_window`.")
}
if (!"filter" %in% names(filter_pre_timepoint_raw)) {
  stop("filter_pre_timepoint.tsv must contain a column named `filter`.")
}
if (!"span_period" %in% names(span_period_raw)) {
  stop("span_period.tsv must contain a column named `span_period`.")
}

#---------------------------
# 3. Prepare parameter values
#---------------------------
# new variable name
new_var_name <- new_var_raw$new_var[1]

# convert date vectors
to_date_vec <- function(x) as.Date(x)

start_date_vec      <- to_date_vec(start_date_raw$start_date)
end_date_vec        <- to_date_vec(end_date_raw$end_date)
ref_start_date_vec  <- to_date_vec(ref_start_date_raw$start_date)
ref_end_date_vec    <- to_date_vec(ref_end_date_raw$end_date)

# ref_end_window: expect a single numeric value (days)
suppressWarnings({
  ref_end_window_val <- as.numeric(ref_end_window_raw$ref_end_window[1])
})
if (is.na(ref_end_window_val)) {
  stop("ref_end_window must be a single numeric value (days).")
}

# ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat: expect single logical (default FALSE if cannot parse)
ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat_val <- FALSE

# span_period: expect single logical
span_period_val <- tolower(span_period_raw$span_period[1])
span_period_val <- ifelse(span_period_val %in% c("true", "t", "1"), TRUE, FALSE)

# filter_pre_timepoint: expression from string (may be empty / NA = no filter)
filter_expr_txt <- filter_pre_timepoint_raw$filter[1]
filter_pre_timepoint_expr <- if (!is.na(filter_expr_txt) && nzchar(filter_expr_txt)) {
  rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr_txt)
} else {
  NULL
}

#---------------------------
# 4. Align parameter vectors with dataset
#---------------------------
n <- nrow(dataset_raw)

recycle_vec <- function(x, n_target, name) {
  if (length(x) == 0) return(rep(NA, n_target))
  if (length(x) == n_target) return(x)
  if (length(x) == 1) return(rep(x, n_target))
  stop("Length of ", name, " (", length(x), ") does not match dataset rows (", n_target, ") and is not 1.")
}

start_date_vec     <- recycle_vec(start_date_vec, n, "start_date")
end_date_vec       <- recycle_vec(end_date_vec, n, "end_date")
ref_start_date_vec <- recycle_vec(ref_start_date_vec, n, "ref_start_date")
ref_end_date_vec   <- recycle_vec(ref_end_date_vec, n, "ref_end_date")

#---------------------------
# 5. Construct analysis dataset
#---------------------------
adsl_like <- dataset_raw %>%
  mutate(
    start_date     = start_date_vec,
    end_date       = end_date_vec,
    ref_start_date = ref_start_date_vec,
    ref_end_date   = ref_end_date_vec
  )

# sanity checks for required columns
req_cols <- c("start_date", "end_date", "ref_start_date", "ref_end_date")
missing_cols <- setdiff(req_cols, names(adsl_like))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required columns in working dataset: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

#---------------------------
# 6. Derive ONTRTFL using admiral
#---------------------------
res <- derive_var_ontrtfl(
  dataset               = adsl_like,
  new_var               = !!sym(new_var_name),
  start_date            = start_date,
  end_date              = end_date,
  ref_start_date        = ref_start_date,
  ref_end_date          = ref_end_date,
  ref_end_window        = ref_end_window_val,
  ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat = ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat_val,
  filter_pre_timepoint  = !!filter_pre_timepoint_expr,
  span_period           = span_period_val
)

# Rename new_var to ONTRTFL as required
res <- res %>%
  rename(ONTRTFL = !!sym(new_var_name))

#---------------------------
# 7. Basic QC on result
#---------------------------
# Check that ONTRTFL only contains "Y" or NA
valid_vals <- unique(res$ONTRTFL)
if (!all(is.na(valid_vals) | valid_vals == "Y")) {
  warning("Unexpected values found in ONTRTFL: ",
          paste(valid_vals[!(is.na(valid_vals) | valid_vals == "Y")], collapse = ", "))
}

# Simple check for obvious date misalignment: start_date after ref_end_date+window where ONTRTFL == "Y"
if ("start_date" %in% names(res) && "ref_end_date" %in% names(res)) {
  flag_misaligned <- res %>%
    mutate(ref_end_plus = ref_end_date + ref_end_window_val) %>%
    filter(!is.na(ONTRTFL) & ONTRTFL == "Y" &
             !is.na(start_date) & !is.na(ref_end_plus) &
             start_date > ref_end_plus)
  if (nrow(flag_misaligned) > 0) {
    warning("Potential date misalignment detected for some rows where ONTRTFL == 'Y'.")
  }
}

#---------------------------
# 8. Write outputs
#---------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

out_csv <- "outputs/dataset_ontrtfl.csv"
out_rds <- "outputs/dataset_ontrtfl.rds"

write.csv(res, out_csv, row.names = FALSE)
saveRDS(res, out_rds)

# Confirm files written
if (!file.exists(out_csv)) stop("CSV output not written: ", out_csv)
if (!file.exists(out_rds)) stop("RDS output not written: ", out_rds)
```

library(admiral)

#---------------------------
# Helper: safe file reader
#---------------------------
read_tsv_safe <- function(path) {

  }
  readr::read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE)
}

#---------------------------
# 1. Read input data
#---------------------------

end_date_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/end_date.tsv")

ref_end_window_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/ref_end_window.tsv")

filter_pre_timepoint_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/filter_pre_timepoint.tsv")
span_period_raw <- read_tsv_safe("inputs/span_period.tsv")

#---------------------------
# 2. Basic validation
#---------------------------

if (!"end_date" %in% names(ref_end_date_raw)) {

}
if (!"span_period" %in% names(span_period_raw)) {

}

#---------------------------
# 3. Prepare parameter values
#---------------------------
# new variable name
new_var_name <- new_var_raw$new_var[1]

# convert date vectors
to_date_vec <- function(x) as.Date(x)

start_date_vec      <- to_date_vec(start_date_raw$start_date)
end_date_vec        <- to_date_vec(end_date_raw$end_date)
ref_start_date_vec  <- to_date_vec(ref_start_date_raw$start_date)
ref_end_date_vec    <- to_date_vec(ref_end_date_raw$end_date)

# ref_end_window: expect a single numeric value (days)
suppressWarnings({
  ref_end_window_val <- as.numeric(ref_end_window_raw$ref_end_window[1])
})
if (is.na(ref_end_window_val)) {
  stop("ref_end_window must be a single numeric value (days).")
}

# ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat: expect single logical (default FALSE if cannot parse)
ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat_val <- FALSE

# span_period: expect single logical
span_period_val <- tolower(span_period_raw$span_period[1])
span_period_val <- ifelse(span_period_val %in% c("true", "t", "1"), TRUE, FALSE)

# filter_pre_timepoint: expression from string (may be empty / NA = no filter)
filter_expr_txt <- filter_pre_timepoint_raw$filter[1]
filter_pre_timepoint_expr <- if (!is.na(filter_expr_txt) && nzchar(filter_expr_txt)) {
  rlang::parse_expr(filter_expr_txt)
} else {
  NULL
}

#---------------------------
# 4. Align parameter vectors with dataset
#---------------------------
n <- nrow(dataset_raw)

recycle_vec <- function(x, n_target, name) {
  if (length(x) == 0) return(rep(NA, n_target))
  if (length(x) == n_target) return(x)
  if (length(x) == 1) return(rep(x, n_target))

#---------------------------
# 5. Construct analysis dataset
#---------------------------
adsl_like <- dataset_raw %>%
  mutate(
    start_date     = start_date_vec,
    end_date       = end_date_vec,
    ref_start_date = ref_start_date_vec,
    ref_end_date   = ref_end_date_vec
  )

# sanity checks for required columns
req_cols <- c("start_date", "end_date", "ref_start_date", "ref_end_date")
missing_cols <- setdiff(req_cols, names(adsl_like))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required columns in working dataset: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

#---------------------------
# 6. Derive ONTRTFL using admiral
#---------------------------
res <- derive_var_ontrtfl(
  dataset               = adsl_like,
  new_var               = !!sym(new_var_name),
  start_date            = start_date,
  end_date              = end_date,
  ref_start_date        = ref_start_date,
  ref_end_date          = ref_end_date,
  ref_end_window        = ref_end_window_val,
  ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat = ignore_time_for_ref_end_dat_val,
  filter_pre_timepoint  = !!filter_pre_timepoint_expr,
  span_period           = span_period_val
)

# Rename new_var to ONTRTFL as required
res <- res %>%
  rename(ONTRTFL = !!sym(new_var_name))

#---------------------------
# 7. Basic QC on result
#---------------------------
# Check that ONTRTFL only contains "Y" or NA
valid_vals <- unique(res$ONTRTFL)
if (!all(is.na(valid_vals) | valid_vals == "Y")) {
  warning("Unexpected values found in ONTRTFL: ",
          paste(valid_vals[!(is.na(valid_vals) | valid_vals == "Y")], collapse = ", "))
}

# Simple check for obvious date misalignment: start_date after ref_end_date+window where ONTRTFL == "Y"
if ("start_date" %in% names(res) && "ref_end_date" %in% names(res)) {
  flag_misaligned <- res %>%
    mutate(ref_end_plus = ref_end_date + ref_end_window_val) %>%
    filter(!is.na(ONTRTFL) & ONTRTFL == "Y" &
             !is.na(start_date) & !is.na(ref_end_plus) &
             start_date > ref_end_plus)
  if (nrow(flag_misaligned) > 0) {
    warning("Potential date misalignment detected for some rows where ONTRTFL == 'Y'.")
  }
}

#---------------------------
# 8. Write outputs
#---------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

out_csv <- "outputs/dataset_ontrtfl.csv"
out_rds <- "outputs/dataset_ontrtfl.rds"

write.csv(res, out_csv, row.names = FALSE)
saveRDS(res, out_rds)

# Confirm files written
if (!file.exists(out_csv)) stop("CSV output not written: ", out_csv)
if (!file.exists(out_rds)) stop("RDS output not written: ", out_rds)

Write R code to derive analysis age using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/start_date.tsv, inputs/end_date.tsv, inputs/age_unit.tsv, inputs/type.tsv). **Note:** This is a wrapper function for the more generic `derive_vars_duration()`. For years: `"year"`, `"years"`, `"yr"`, `"yrs"`, `"y"` For months: `"month"`, `"months"`, `"mo"`, `"mos"` For weeks: `"week"`, `"weeks"`, `"wk"`, `"wks"`, `"w"` For days: `"day"`, `"days"`, `"d"` For hours: `"hour"`, `"hours"`, `"hr"`, `"hrs"`, `"h"` For minutes: `"minute"`, `"minutes"`, `"min"`, `"mins"` For seconds: `"second"`, `"seconds"`, `"sec"`, `"secs"`, `"s"` Additional details: specified output unit. If the end date is before the start date, the duration is negative. The start and end date variable must be present in the specified input dataset. The [lubridate](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/) package calculates two types of spans between two dates: duration and interval. While these calculations are largely the same, when the unit of the time period is month or year the result can be slightly different. The difference arises from the ambiguity in the length of `"1 month"` or `"1 year"`. Months may have 31, 30, 28, or 29 days, and years are 365 days and 366 during leap years. Durations and intervals help solve the ambiguity in these measures. The **interval** between `2000-02-01` and `2000-03-01` is `1` (i.e. one month). The **duration** between these two dates is `0.95`, which accounts for the fact that the year 2000 is a leap year, February has 29 days, and the average month length is `30.4375`, i.e. `29 / 30.4375 = 0.95`. For additional details, review the [lubridate time span reference page](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/timespan.html). Use admiral's derive_vars_aage function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("start_date", "end_date"))`), start_date (The start date A date or date-time object is expected.), end_date (The end date A date or date-time object is expected.), age_unit (Age unit The age is derived in the specified unit), type (lubridate duration type See below for details. Default: `"interval"` Permitted Values: `"duration"`, `"interval"`). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with ``AAGE`` and ``AAGEU`` added. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). library(admiral)
```r
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)

#----------------------------
# Helper: standardize age unit
#----------------------------
standardize_age_unit <- function(unit_raw) {
  u <- tolower(trimws(unit_raw))
  case_when(
    u %in% c("year", "years", "yr", "yrs", "y") ~ "years",
    u %in% c("month", "months", "mo", "mos") ~ "months",
    u %in% c("week", "weeks", "wk", "wks", "w") ~ "weeks",
    u %in% c("day", "days", "d") ~ "days",
    u %in% c("hour", "hours", "hr", "hrs", "h") ~ "hours",
    u %in% c("minute", "minutes", "min", "mins") ~ "minutes",
    u %in% c("second", "seconds", "sec", "secs", "s") ~ "seconds",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

#----------------------------
# Helper: validate dates
#----------------------------
validate_date_vec <- function(x, var_name) {
  if (!inherits(x, c("Date", "POSIXct", "POSIXt"))) {
    stop(sprintf("`%s` must be Date or POSIXt; got class %s", var_name, paste(class(x), collapse = ", ")), call. = FALSE)
  }
  x
}

#----------------------------
# Read inputs
#----------------------------
datase_path      <- file.path("inputs", "datase.tsv")
start_date_path  <- file.path("inputs", "start_date.tsv")
end_date_path    <- file.path("inputs", "end_date.tsv")
age_unit_path    <- file.path("inputs", "age_unit.tsv")
type_path        <- file.path("inputs", "type.tsv")

datase <- read_tsv(datase_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character())) %>%
  mutate(
    ADT = ymd(ADT)
  )

start_tbl <- read_tsv(start_date_path, col_types = cols(start_date = col_character()))
end_tbl   <- read_tsv(end_date_path,   col_types = cols(end_date   = col_character()))
age_tbl   <- read_tsv(age_unit_path,   col_types = cols(age_unit   = col_character()))
type_tbl  <- read_tsv(type_path,       col_types = cols(type       = col_character()))

#----------------------------
# Basic input validation
#----------------------------
if (nrow(age_tbl) != 1L) {
  stop("`age_unit.tsv` must contain exactly one row.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (nrow(type_tbl) != 1L) {
  stop("`type.tsv` must contain exactly one row.", call. = FALSE)
}

age_unit_std <- standardize_age_unit(age_tbl$age_unit[1])

if (is.na(age_unit_std)) {
  stop(sprintf("Unsupported age unit: %s", age_tbl$age_unit[1]), call. = FALSE)
}

type_val_raw <- tolower(trimws(type_tbl$type[1]))
type_val <- if (type_val_raw %in% c("duration", "interval")) type_val_raw else "interval"

# Align start/end vectors to datase rows (recycle if needed but check)
n_datase <- nrow(datase)
n_start  <- nrow(start_tbl)
n_end    <- nrow(end_tbl)

if (n_start == 0L || n_end == 0L) {
  stop("`start_date.tsv` and `end_date.tsv` must not be empty.", call. = FALSE)
}

if (n_start != n_datase && n_start != 1L) {
  stop("Row count of `start_date.tsv` must be 1 or match `datase.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (n_end != n_datase && n_end != 1L) {
  stop("Row count of `end_date.tsv` must be 1 or match `datase.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}

start_vec <- ymd(start_tbl$start_date)
end_vec   <- ymd(end_tbl$end_date)

if (any(is.na(start_vec))) {
  stop("Invalid date(s) detected in `start_date.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (any(is.na(end_vec))) {
  stop("Invalid date(s) detected in `end_date.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}

start_vec <- rep(start_vec, length.out = n_datase)
end_vec   <- rep(end_vec,   length.out = n_datase)

validate_date_vec(start_vec, "start_date")
validate_date_vec(end_vec,   "end_date")

#----------------------------
# Construct analysis dataset
#----------------------------
datase_aage_input <- datase %>%
  mutate(
    start_date = start_vec,
    end_date   = end_vec
  )

# Ensure required vars present
required_vars <- c("start_date", "end_date")
missing_vars <- setdiff(required_vars, names(datase_aage_input))
if (length(missing_vars) > 0L) {
  stop(sprintf("Missing required variable(s) in dataset: %s", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", ")), call. = FALSE)
}

#----------------------------
# Derive analysis age
#----------------------------
datase_aage <- derive_vars_aage(
  dataset   = datase_aage_input,
  start_date = start_date,
  end_date   = end_date,
  age_unit   = age_unit_std,
  type       = type_val
)

#----------------------------
# Post-derivation validation
#----------------------------
if (!all(c("AAGE", "AAGEU") %in% names(datase_aage))) {
  stop("AAGE or AAGEU not found after calling derive_vars_aage().", call. = FALSE)
}

if (any(is.na(datase_aage$AAGE))) {
  warning("Missing values detected in AAGE; check input dates and unit.", call. = FALSE)
}

# Check for unexpected patterns (e.g., extreme ages, sign inconsistencies)
if (any(datase_aage$AAGE < -200 | datase_aage$AAGE > 200, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("Unusually large magnitude AAGE values detected (outside +/-200).", call. = FALSE)
}

#----------------------------
# Write outputs
#----------------------------
output_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

out_csv_path <- file.path(output_dir, "datase_aage.csv")
out_rds_path <- file.path(output_dir, "datase_aage.rds")

write_csv(datase_aage, out_csv_path, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_aage, out_rds_path)

#----------------------------
# Confirm outputs were written
#----------------------------
if (!file.exists(out_csv_path)) {
  stop("Output CSV file was not written successfully.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (!file.exists(out_rds_path)) {
  stop("Output RDS file was not written successfully.", call. = FALSE)
}

# Basic integrity check: re-read and compare row counts
datase_aage_check <- read_csv(out_csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
if (nrow(datase_aage_check) != nrow(datase_aage)) {
  stop("Row count mismatch between in-memory and written CSV dataset.", call. = FALSE)
}
```

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)

#----------------------------
# Helper: standardize age unit
#----------------------------
standardize_age_unit <- function(unit_raw) {
  u <- tolower(trimws(unit_raw))
  case_when(
    u %in% c("year", "years", "yr", "yrs", "y") ~ "years",
    u %in% c("month", "months", "mo", "mos") ~ "months",
    u %in% c("week", "weeks", "wk", "wks", "w") ~ "weeks",
    u %in% c("day", "days", "d") ~ "days",
    u %in% c("hour", "hours", "hr", "hrs", "h") ~ "hours",
    u %in% c("minute", "minutes", "min", "mins") ~ "minutes",
    u %in% c("second", "seconds", "sec", "secs", "s") ~ "seconds",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )
}

#----------------------------
# Helper: validate dates
#----------------------------
validate_date_vec <- function(x, var_name) {
  if (!inherits(x, c("Date", "POSIXct", "POSIXt"))) {
    stop(sprintf("`%s` must be Date or POSIXt; got class %s", var_name, paste(class(x), collapse = ", ")), call. = FALSE)
  }
  x
}

#----------------------------
# Read inputs
#----------------------------
datase_path      <- file.path("inputs", "datase.tsv")
start_date_path  <- file.path("inputs", "start_date.tsv")
end_date_path    <- file.path("inputs", "end_date.tsv")
age_unit_path    <- file.path("inputs", "age_unit.tsv")
type_path        <- file.path("inputs", "type.tsv")

datase <- read_tsv(datase_path, col_types = cols(.default = col_character())) %>%
  mutate(
    ADT = ymd(ADT)
  )

start_tbl <- read_tsv(start_date_path, col_types = cols(start_date = col_character()))
end_tbl   <- read_tsv(end_date_path,   col_types = cols(end_date   = col_character()))
age_tbl   <- read_tsv(age_unit_path,   col_types = cols(age_unit   = col_character()))
type_tbl  <- read_tsv(type_path,       col_types = cols(type       = col_character()))

#----------------------------
# Basic input validation
#----------------------------
if (nrow(age_tbl) != 1L) {
  stop("`age_unit.tsv` must contain exactly one row.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (nrow(type_tbl) != 1L) {
  stop("`type.tsv` must contain exactly one row.", call. = FALSE)
}

age_unit_std <- standardize_age_unit(age_tbl$age_unit[1])

if (is.na(age_unit_std)) {
  stop(sprintf("Unsupported age unit: %s", age_tbl$age_unit[1]), call. = FALSE)
}

type_val_raw <- tolower(trimws(type_tbl$type[1]))
type_val <- if (type_val_raw %in% c("duration", "interval")) type_val_raw else "interval"

# Align start/end vectors to datase rows (recycle if needed but check)
n_datase <- nrow(datase)
n_start  <- nrow(start_tbl)
n_end    <- nrow(end_tbl)

if (n_start == 0L || n_end == 0L) {
  stop("`start_date.tsv` and `end_date.tsv` must not be empty.", call. = FALSE)
}

if (n_start != n_datase && n_start != 1L) {
  stop("Row count of `start_date.tsv` must be 1 or match `datase.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (n_end != n_datase && n_end != 1L) {
  stop("Row count of `end_date.tsv` must be 1 or match `datase.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}

start_vec <- ymd(start_tbl$start_date)
end_vec   <- ymd(end_tbl$end_date)

if (any(is.na(start_vec))) {
  stop("Invalid date(s) detected in `start_date.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (any(is.na(end_vec))) {
  stop("Invalid date(s) detected in `end_date.tsv`.", call. = FALSE)
}

start_vec <- rep(start_vec, length.out = n_datase)
end_vec   <- rep(end_vec,   length.out = n_datase)

validate_date_vec(start_vec, "start_date")
validate_date_vec(end_vec,   "end_date")

#----------------------------
# Construct analysis dataset
#----------------------------
datase_aage_input <- datase %>%
  mutate(
    start_date = start_vec,
    end_date   = end_vec
  )

# Ensure required vars present
required_vars <- c("start_date", "end_date")
missing_vars <- setdiff(required_vars, names(datase_aage_input))
if (length(missing_vars) > 0L) {
  stop(sprintf("Missing required variable(s) in dataset: %s", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", ")), call. = FALSE)
}

#----------------------------
# Derive analysis age
#----------------------------
datase_aage <- derive_vars_aage(
  dataset   = datase_aage_input,
  start_date = start_date,
  end_date   = end_date,
  age_unit   = age_unit_std,
  type       = type_val
)

#----------------------------
# Post-derivation validation
#----------------------------
if (!all(c("AAGE", "AAGEU") %in% names(datase_aage))) {
  stop("AAGE or AAGEU not found after calling derive_vars_aage().", call. = FALSE)
}

if (any(is.na(datase_aage$AAGE))) {
  warning("Missing values detected in AAGE; check input dates and unit.", call. = FALSE)
}

# Check for unexpected patterns (e.g., extreme ages, sign inconsistencies)
if (any(datase_aage$AAGE < -200 | datase_aage$AAGE > 200, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("Unusually large magnitude AAGE values detected (outside +/-200).", call. = FALSE)
}

#----------------------------
# Write outputs
#----------------------------
output_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

out_csv_path <- file.path(output_dir, "datase_aage.csv")
out_rds_path <- file.path(output_dir, "datase_aage.rds")

write_csv(datase_aage, out_csv_path, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_aage, out_rds_path)

#----------------------------
# Confirm outputs were written
#----------------------------
if (!file.exists(out_csv_path)) {
  stop("Output CSV file was not written successfully.", call. = FALSE)
}
if (!file.exists(out_rds_path)) {
  stop("Output RDS file was not written successfully.", call. = FALSE)
}

# Basic integrity check: re-read and compare row counts
datase_aage_check <- read_csv(out_csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
if (nrow(datase_aage_check) != nrow(datase_aage)) {
  stop("Row count mismatch between in-memory and written CSV dataset.", call. = FALSE)
}

```r
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(lubridate)

#-----------------------------
# Helper: safe date parser
#-----------------------------
parse_date_safe <- function(x, col_name) {
  d <- suppressWarnings(ymd(x))
  if (any(is.na(d) & !is.na(x))) {
    warning(
      sprintf(
        "Some values in '%s' could not be parsed as dates:\n%s",
        col_name,
        paste(unique(x[is.na(d) & !is.na(x)]), collapse = ", ")
      )
    )
  }
  d
}

#-----------------------------
# Read & validate input data
#-----------------------------
# Main dataset
datase_path <- file.path("inputs", "datase.tsv")
datase <- read_tsv(datase_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Basic validation
req_vars <- c("TRTSDT", "TRTEDT")
missing_vars <- setdiff(req_vars, names(datase))
if (length(missing_vars) > 0) {
  stop("datase is missing required variables: ", paste(missing_vars, collapse = ", "))
}

# Parse TRTSDT/TRTEDT as dates
datase <- datase %>%
  mutate(
    start_date = parse_date_safe(TRTSDT, "TRTSDT"),
    end_date   = parse_date_safe(TRTEDT, "TRTEDT")
  )

# Check for missing or inverted dates
if (any(is.na(datase$start_date))) {
  warning("Some start_date values are NA after parsing.")
}
if (any(is.na(datase$end_date))) {
  warning("Some end_date values are NA after parsing.")
}
if (any(datase$end_date < datase$start_date, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("Some records have end_date < start_date; TRTDURD may be negative.")
}

#-----------------------------
# Optional: load standalone start/end tables
# (for validation / alignment checks)
#-----------------------------
start_date_tbl_path     <- file.path("inputs", "start_date.tsv")
end_date_tbl_path       <- file.path("inputs", "end_date.tsv")
start_date_df_tbl_path  <- file.path("inputs", "start_date_df.tsv")
end_date_df_tbl_path    <- file.path("inputs", "end_date_df.tsv")

start_date_tbl    <- read_tsv(start_date_tbl_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
end_date_tbl      <- read_tsv(end_date_tbl_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
start_date_df_tbl <- read_tsv(start_date_df_tbl_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
end_date_df_tbl   <- read_tsv(end_date_df_tbl_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

start_date_tbl <- start_date_tbl %>%
  mutate(start_date = parse_date_safe(start_date, "start_date"))
end_date_tbl <- end_date_tbl %>%
  mutate(end_date = parse_date_safe(end_date, "end_date"))
start_date_df_tbl <- start_date_df_tbl %>%
  mutate(start_date = parse_date_safe(start_date, "start_date"))
end_date_df_tbl <- end_date_df_tbl %>%
  mutate(end_date = parse_date_safe(end_date, "end_date"))

# Simple alignment checks (no join key provided, so only length checks)
if (nrow(start_date_tbl) != nrow(end_date_tbl)) {
  warning("start_date.tsv and end_date.tsv have different row counts; potential misalignment.")
}
if (nrow(start_date_df_tbl) != nrow(end_date_df_tbl)) {
  warning("start_date_df.tsv and end_date_df.tsv have different row counts; potential misalignment.")
}

#-----------------------------
# Wrapper: derive_var_trtdurd
#-----------------------------
#' Derive Treatment Duration (Days)
#'
#' Wrapper around `derive_vars_duration()` to derive TRTDURD
#' as (end_date - start_date + 1) in days.
#'
#' @param datase `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("start_date", "end_date"))`
#' @param start_date The start date. A date or date-time object is expected.
#' @param end_date   The end date. A date or date-time object is expected.
#'
#' @return The input dataset with `TRTDURD` added.
derive_var_trtdurd <- function(datase,
                               start_date,
                               end_date) {
  if (!all(c("start_date", "end_date") %in% names(datase))) {
    stop("datase must contain 'start_date' and 'end_date' variables.")
  }

  datase %>%
    derive_vars_duration(
      new_var        = TRTDURD,
      start_date     = {{ start_date }},
      end_date       = {{ end_date }},
      out_unit       = "days",
      add_one        = TRUE
    )
}

#-----------------------------
# Apply wrapper to datase
#-----------------------------
datase_trt <- derive_var_trtdurd(
  datase    = datase,
  start_date = start_date,
  end_date   = end_date
)

#-----------------------------
# Basic post-derivation checks
#-----------------------------
if (!"TRTDURD" %in% names(datase_trt)) {
  stop("TRTDURD was not created in datase_trt.")
}

if (any(is.na(datase_trt$TRTDURD) & !is.na(datase_trt$start_date) & !is.na(datase_trt$end_date))) {
  warning("Some TRTDURD values are NA despite non-missing start_date and end_date.")
}

if (any(datase_trt$TRTDURD <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("Some TRTDURD values are non-positive; check for date issues.")
}

#-----------------------------
# Write outputs
#-----------------------------
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

datase_csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "datase_trt.csv")
datase_rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "datase_trt.rds")

write_csv(datase_trt, datase_csv_path, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_trt, datase_rds_path)

#-----------------------------
# Confirm output files
#-----------------------------
if (!file.exists(datase_csv_path)) {
  stop("CSV output file was not written: ", datase_csv_path)
}
if (!file.exists(datase_rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not written: ", datase_rds_path)
}

# Quick sanity check on written CSV
datase_trt_check <- suppressWarnings(read_csv(datase_csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE))
if (!"TRTDURD" %in% names(datase_trt_check)) {
  warning("TRTDURD not found in reloaded CSV; possible write/read issue.")
}
```

#-----------------------------

#-----------------------------
# Wrapper: derive_var_trtdurd
#-----------------------------
#' Derive Treatment Duration (Days)
#'
#' Wrapper around `derive_vars_duration()` to derive TRTDURD
#' as (end_date - start_date + 1) in days.
#'
#' @param datase `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("start_date", "end_date"))`
#' @param start_date The start date. A date or date-time object is expected.
#' @param end_date   The end date. A date or date-time object is expected.

                               start_date,

#-----------------------------
# Apply wrapper to datase
#-----------------------------
datase_trt <- derive_var_trtdurd(
  datase    = datase,
  start_date = start_date,
  end_date   = end_date
)

#-----------------------------
# Basic post-derivation checks
#-----------------------------
if (!"TRTDURD" %in% names(datase_trt)) {
  stop("TRTDURD was not created in datase_trt.")
}

if (any(is.na(datase_trt$TRTDURD) & !is.na(datase_trt$start_date) & !is.na(datase_trt$end_date))) {
  warning("Some TRTDURD values are NA despite non-missing start_date and end_date.")
}

if (any(datase_trt$TRTDURD <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
  warning("Some TRTDURD values are non-positive; check for date issues.")
}

#-----------------------------
# Write outputs
#-----------------------------
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

datase_csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "datase_trt.csv")
datase_rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "datase_trt.rds")

write_csv(datase_trt, datase_csv_path, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_trt, datase_rds_path)

#-----------------------------
# Confirm output files
#-----------------------------
if (!file.exists(datase_csv_path)) {
  stop("CSV output file was not written: ", datase_csv_path)
}
if (!file.exists(datase_rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not written: ", datase_rds_path)
}

# Quick sanity check on written CSV
datase_trt_check <- suppressWarnings(read_csv(datase_csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE))
if (!"TRTDURD" %in% names(datase_trt_check)) {
  warning("TRTDURD not found in reloaded CSV; possible write/read issue.")
}
