library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
library(purrr)

# Create outputs directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

# Safely build adae3
adae3 <- tribble(
  ~USUBJID, ~TRTSDTM,     ~TRTEDTM,     ~ASTDTM,       ~AENDTM,       ~AEITOXGR, ~AETOXGR,
  # Patient 1: Pre-treatment AE
  "1",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-20",  "2020-12-21",  "2",       "2",
  # Patient 2: On-treatment AE
  "2",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2021-12-20",  "2021-12-21",  "2",       "2",
  # Patient 3: Pre-treatment AE, then on-treatment AE at same intensity
  "3",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-20",  "2020-12-21",  "2",       "2",
  "3",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2021-12-20",  "2021-12-21",  "2",       "2",
  # Patient 4: Pre-treatment AE, then on-treatment AE at wors. intensity
  "4",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-20",  "2020-12-21",  "2",       "2",
  "4",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2021-12-20",  "2021-12-21",  "2",       "3",
  # Patient 5: Pre-treatment AE, then on-treatment AE at impr. intensity
  "5",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-20",  "2020-12-21",  "2",       "2",
  "5",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2021-12-20",  "2021-12-21",  "2",       "1",
  # Patient 6: AE starting pre-treatment, continuing on-treatment, then 2nd AE at same intensity
  "6",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "2",       "2",
  "6",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2021-12-20",  "2021-12-21",  "2",       "2",
  # Patient 7: AE starting pre-treatment, continuing on-treatment, then 2nd AE at wors. intensity
  "7",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "2",       "2",
  "7",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2021-12-20",  "2021-12-21",  "2",       "3",
  # Patient 8: AE starting pre-treatment, continuing on-treatment, then 2nd AE at impr. intensity
  "8",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "2",       "2",
  "8",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2021-12-20",  "2021-12-21",  "2",       "1",
  # Patient 9: AE starting pre-treatment, continuing on-treatment, and no change in intensity
  "9",      "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "2",       "2",
  # Patient 10: AE starting pre-treatment, continuing on-treatment, and wors. intensity
  "10",     "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "2",       "4",
  # Patient 11: AE starting pre-treatment, continuing on-treatment, and impr. intensity
  "11",     "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "2",       "1",
  # Patient 12: AE starting pre-treatment, worsening, then improving
  "12",     "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "3",       "2",
  # Patient 13: AE starting pre-treatment, improving, then worsening
  "13",     "2021-01-01", "2021-12-31", "2020-12-23",  "2021-01-21",  "1",       "2"
) %>%
  mutate(
    ASTDTM  = ymd(ASTDTM),
    AENDTM  = ymd(AENDTM),
    TRTSDTM = ymd(TRTSDTM),
    TRTEDTM = ymd(TRTEDTM)
  )

# Basic validation before derivation
stopifnot(
  all(!is.na(adae3$USUBJID)),
  all(!is.na(adae3$ASTDTM)),
  all(!is.na(adae3$AENDTM)),
  all(!is.na(adae3$TRTSDTM)),
  all(!is.na(adae3$TRTEDTM)),
  all(adae3$ASTDTM <= adae3$AENDTM),
  all(adae3$TRTSDTM <= adae3$TRTEDTM)
)

# Derive TRTEMFL using admiral
adae3_trtem <- derive_var_trtemfl(
  adae3,
  new_var        = TRTEMFL,
  trt_end_date   = TRTEDTM,
  end_window     = 0,
  initial_intensity = AEITOXGR,
  intensity      = AETOXGR,
  subject_keys   = exprs(USUBJID)
) %>%
  select(
    USUBJID,
    TRTSDTM,
    TRTEDTM,
    ASTDTM,
    AENDTM,
    AEITOXGR,
    AETOXGR,
    TRTEMFL
  )

# Write outputs with error handling
csv_path <- file.path("outputs", "adae3_trtem.csv")
rds_path <- file.path("outputs", "adae3_trtem.rds")

safe_write_csv <- safely(write_csv)
safe_save_rds  <- safely(saveRDS)

csv_res <- safe_write_csv(adae3_trtem, csv_path)
rds_res <- safe_save_rds(adae3_trtem, rds_path)

if (!is.null(csv_res$error)) {
  stop("Error writing CSV: ", csv_res$error$message)
}
if (!is.null(rds_res$error)) {
  stop("Error writing RDS: ", rds_res$error$message)
}

# Confirm that files were written and can be read back
stopifnot(file.exists(csv_path), file.exists(rds_path))

adae3_trtem_csv <- read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
adae3_trtem_rds <- readRDS(rds_path)

# Basic consistency checks after round-trip
stopifnot(
  nrow(adae3_trtem_csv) == nrow(adae3_trtem),
  nrow(adae3_trtem_rds) == nrow(adae3_trtem),
  all(sort(unique(adae3_trtem_csv$USUBJID)) == sort(unique(adae3_trtem$USUBJID))),
  all(sort(unique(adae3_trtem_rds$USUBJID)) == sort(unique(adae3_trtem$USUBJID)))
)

# Check for unexpected missing TRTEMFL or date misalignment
stopifnot(
  !any(is.na(adae3_trtem$TRTEMFL)),
  all(adae3_trtem$ASTDTM <= adae3_trtem$AENDTM),
  all(adae3_trtem$TRTSDTM <= adae3_trtem$TRTEDTM)
)

Write R code to derive categorization variables like `avalcaty` and `avalcayn` using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/definition.tsv, inputs/by_vars.tsv). vital signs data with some missing values (`NA`) that will demonstrate how the function handles different scenarios. library(dplyr) library(tibble) advs <- tibble::tribble( ~USUBJID, ~VSTEST, ~AVAL, "01-701-1015", "Height", 147.32, "01-701-1015", "Weight", 53.98, "01-701-1023", "Height", 162.56, "01-701-1023", "Weight", NA, "01-701-1028", "Height", NA, "01-701-1028", "Weight", NA, "01-701-1033", "Height", 175.26, "01-701-1033", "Weight", 88.45 ) using `by_vars`. The conditions must include all necessary filtering logic, such as checking both `VSTEST` and `AVAL`. Records that don't match any condition will have `NA` values for the new variables. definition <- exprs( ~condition, ~AVALCAT1, ~AVALCA1N, ~NEWCOL, VSTEST == "Height" & AVAL > 160, ">160 cm", 1, "extra1", VSTEST == "Height" & AVAL <= 160, "<=160 cm", 2, "extra2" ) derive_vars_cat( dataset = advs, definition = definition ) matching each by group value. This simplifies the condition logic as you don't need to include the by variable in each condition. Here we derive categories for both Height and Weight measurements using `by_vars = exprs(VSTEST)`. definition2 <- exprs( ~VSTEST, ~condition, ~AVALCAT1, ~AVALCA1N, "Height", AVAL > 160, ">160 cm", 1, "Height", AVAL <= 160, "<=160 cm", 2, "Weight", AVAL > 70, ">70 kg", 1, "Weight", AVAL <= 70, "<=70 kg", 2 ) derive_vars_cat( dataset = advs, definition = definition2, by_vars = exprs(VSTEST) ) It's best practice to make conditions mutually exclusive using explicit range definitions (e.g., `AVAL <= 170 & AVAL > 160`) to avoid ambiguity, even though the function uses first-match logic. definition3 <- exprs( ~VSTEST, ~condition, ~AVALCAT1, ~AVALCA1N, "Height", AVAL > 170, ">170 cm", 1, "Height", AVAL <= 170 & AVAL > 160, "<=170 cm", 2, "Height", AVAL <= 160, "<=160 cm", 3 ) derive_vars_cat( dataset = advs, definition = definition3, by_vars = exprs(VSTEST) ) variables (`MCRITyML` and `MCRITyMN`). The conditions use reference range variables (like `ANRHI`) to create categories relative to normal ranges, which is common in laboratory data analysis. adlb <- tibble::tribble( ~USUBJID, ~PARAM, ~AVAL, ~AVALU, ~ANRHI, "01-701-1015", "ALT", 150, "U/L", 40, "01-701-1023", "ALT", 70, "U/L", 40, "01-701-1036", "ALT", 130, "U/L", 40, "01-701-1048", "ALT", 30, "U/L", 40, "01-701-1015", "AST", 50, "U/L", 35 ) definition_mcrit <- exprs( ~PARAM, ~condition, ~MCRIT1ML, ~MCRIT1MN, "ALT", AVAL <= ANRHI, "<=ANRHI", 1, "ALT", ANRHI < AVAL & AVAL <= 3 * ANRHI, ">1-3*ANRHI", 2, "ALT", 3 * ANRHI < AVAL, ">3*ANRHI", 3 ) adlb %>% derive_vars_cat( definition = definition_mcrit, by_vars = exprs(PARAM) ) `definition` will have `NA` for all derived variables. In this example, records with `VSTEST == "Weight"` will have `NA` values because only "Height" conditions are defined. Additionally, records with missing `AVAL` will result in `NA` for the categorization variables since conditions cannot be evaluated. definition4 <- exprs( ~VSTEST, ~condition, ~AVALCAT1, ~AVALCA1N, "Height", AVAL > 160, ">160 cm", 1, "Height", AVAL <= 160, "<=160 cm", 2 ) derive_vars_cat( dataset = advs, definition = definition4, by_vars = exprs(VSTEST) ) This example creates three different categorization schemes (`AVALCAT1`, `AVALCAT2`, and `AVALCAT3`) with their corresponding numeric flags, all from the same set of conditions. definition5 <- exprs( ~VSTEST, ~condition, ~AVALCAT1, ~AVALCA1N, ~AVALCAT2, ~AVALCA2N, ~AVALCAT3, "Height", AVAL > 160, ">160 cm", 1, "Tall", 1, "Group A", "Height", AVAL <= 160, "<=160 cm", 2, "Short", 2, "Group B", "Weight", AVAL > 70, ">70 kg", 3, "Heavy", 3, "Group C", "Weight", AVAL <= 70, "<=70 kg", 4, "Light", 4, "Group D" ) derive_vars_cat( dataset = advs, definition = definition5, by_vars = exprs(VSTEST) ) Additional details: If conditions are overlapping, the row order of `definitions` must be carefully considered. The **first** match will determine the category. i.e. if `AVAL = 155` and the `definition` is: ```{r} #| eval: false definition <- exprs( ~VSTEST, ~condition, ~AVALCAT1, ~AVALCA1N, "Height", AVAL > 170, ">170 cm", 1, "Height", AVAL <= 170, "<=170 cm", 2, "Height", AVAL <= 160, "<=160 cm", 3 ) ``` then `AVALCAT1` will be `"<=170 cm"`, as this is the first match for `AVAL`. If you specify: ```{r} #| eval: false definition <- exprs( ~VSTEST, ~condition, ~AVALCAT1, ~AVALCA1N, "Height", AVAL <= 160, "<=160 cm", 3, "Height", AVAL <= 170, "<=170 cm", 2, "Height", AVAL > 170, ">170 cm", 1 ) ``` Then `AVAL <= 160` will lead to `AVALCAT1 == "<=160 cm"`, `AVAL` in-between `160` and `170` will lead to `AVALCAT1 == "<=170 cm"`, and `AVAL <= 170` will lead to `AVALCAT1 == ">170 cm"`. However, we suggest to be more explicit when defining the `condition`, to avoid overlap. In this case, the middle condition should be: `AVAL <= 170 & AVAL > 160` Use admiral's derive_vars_cat function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("by_vars", "definition"))`), definition (List of expressions created by `exprs()`.), by_vars (list of expressions with one element. `NULL` by default. Allows for specifying by groups, e.g. `exprs(PARAMCD)`.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the new variables defined in `definition` added. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(rlang)

#-----------------------------
# Read input data
#-----------------------------
datase <- readr::read_tsv(
  file = "inputs/datase.tsv",
  col_types = cols()
)

dataset <- readr::read_tsv(
  file = "inputs/dataset.tsv",
  col_types = cols()
)

by_vars_in <- readr::read_tsv(
  file = "inputs/by_vars.tsv",
  col_types = cols()
)

definition_in <- readr::read_tsv(
  file = "inputs/definition.tsv",
  col_types = cols()
)

# Basic validation
stopifnot(
  all(c("USUBJID", "AVISIT") %in% names(by_vars_in)),
  all(c("AVAL", "AVISITN", "USUBJID") %in% names(definition_in)),
  all(c("USUBJID", "AVAL", "AVISITN", "AVISIT") %in% names(datase))
)

#-----------------------------
# Example vital signs data to demonstrate derive_vars_cat
#-----------------------------
advs <- tibble::tribble(
  ~USUBJID,      ~VSTEST, ~AVAL,
  "01-701-1015", "Height", 147.32,
  "01-701-1015", "Weight", 53.98,
  "01-701-1023", "Height", 162.56,
  "01-701-1023", "Weight", NA,
  "01-701-1028", "Height", NA,
  "01-701-1028", "Weight", NA,
  "01-701-1033", "Height", 175.26,
  "01-701-1033", "Weight", 88.45
)

#-----------------------------
# 1. Simple definition (no by_vars)
#-----------------------------
definition <- exprs(
  ~condition,                              ~AVALCAT1,  ~AVALCA1N, ~NEWCOL,
  VSTEST == "Height" & AVAL > 160,         ">160 cm",  1,         "extra1",
  VSTEST == "Height" & AVAL <= 160,        "<=160 cm", 2,         "extra2"
)

advs_cat1 <- derive_vars_cat(
  dataset   = advs,
  definition = definition
)

#-----------------------------
# 2. Using by_vars = exprs(VSTEST)
#-----------------------------
definition2 <- exprs(
  ~VSTEST,  ~condition,        ~AVALCAT1,  ~AVALCA1N,
  "Height", AVAL > 160,        ">160 cm",  1,
  "Height", AVAL <= 160,       "<=160 cm", 2,
  "Weight", AVAL > 70,         ">70 kg",   1,
  "Weight", AVAL <= 70,        "<=70 kg",  2
)

advs_cat2 <- derive_vars_cat(
  dataset   = advs,
  definition = definition2,
  by_vars   = exprs(VSTEST)
)

#-----------------------------
# 3. Mutually exclusive height categories
#-----------------------------
definition3 <- exprs(
  ~VSTEST,  ~condition,                       ~AVALCAT1,   ~AVALCA1N,
  "Height", AVAL > 170,                       ">170 cm",   1,
  "Height", AVAL <= 170 & AVAL > 160,        "<=170 cm",  2,
  "Height", AVAL <= 160,                     "<=160 cm",  3
)

advs_cat3 <- derive_vars_cat(
  dataset   = advs,
  definition = definition3,
  by_vars   = exprs(VSTEST)
)

#-----------------------------
# 4. Laboratory example with MCRITyML/MN
#-----------------------------
adlb <- tibble::tribble(
  ~USUBJID,       ~PARAM, ~AVAL, ~AVALU, ~ANRHI,
  "01-701-1015",  "ALT",  150,   "U/L",  40,
  "01-701-1023",  "ALT",  70,    "U/L",  40,
  "01-701-1036",  "ALT",  130,   "U/L",  40,
  "01-701-1048",  "ALT",  30,    "U/L",  40,
  "01-701-1015",  "AST",  50,    "U/L",  35
)

definition_mcrit <- exprs(
  ~PARAM, ~condition,                               ~MCRIT1ML,   ~MCRIT1MN,
  "ALT",  AVAL <= ANRHI,                            "<=ANRHI",   1,
  "ALT",  ANRHI < AVAL & AVAL <= 3 * ANRHI,         ">1-3*ANRHI",2,
  "ALT",  3 * ANRHI < AVAL,                         ">3*ANRHI",  3
)

adlb_cat <- adlb %>%
  derive_vars_cat(
    definition = definition_mcrit,
    by_vars    = exprs(PARAM)
  )

#-----------------------------
# 5. Example where only Height is defined (Weight -> NA)
#-----------------------------
definition4 <- exprs(
  ~VSTEST,  ~condition,        ~AVALCAT1,  ~AVALCA1N,
  "Height", AVAL > 160,        ">160 cm",  1,
  "Height", AVAL <= 160,       "<=160 cm", 2
)

advs_cat4 <- derive_vars_cat(
  dataset   = advs,
  definition = definition4,
  by_vars   = exprs(VSTEST)
)

#-----------------------------
# 6. Multiple categorization schemes from same conditions
#-----------------------------
definition5 <- exprs(
  ~VSTEST,  ~condition,   ~AVALCAT1,  ~AVALCA1N, ~AVALCAT2, ~AVALCA2N, ~AVALCAT3,
  "Height", AVAL > 160,   ">160 cm",  1,         "Tall",    1,         "Group A",
  "Height", AVAL <= 160,  "<=160 cm", 2,         "Short",   2,         "Group B",
  "Weight", AVAL > 70,    ">70 kg",   3,         "Heavy",   3,         "Group C",
  "Weight", AVAL <= 70,   "<=70 kg",  4,         "Light",   4,         "Group D"
)

advs_cat5 <- derive_vars_cat(
  dataset   = advs,
  definition = definition5,
  by_vars   = exprs(VSTEST)
)

#-----------------------------
# 7. Example using external by_vars/datase to create avalcaty/avalcayn
#    (USUBJID + AVISIT strata, simple AVAL-based categories)
#-----------------------------
# Build by_vars expression list from by_vars_in
by_vars_expr <- exprs(USUBJID, AVISIT)

# Simple generic categorization for datase: AVAL high vs low
definition_datase <- exprs(
  ~condition,          ~AVALCATY,      ~AVALCAYN,
  AVAL > 100,          "High (>100)",  1,
  AVAL <= 100,         "Low (<=100)",  2
)

datase_cat <- derive_vars_cat(
  dataset   = datase,
  definition = definition_datase,
  by_vars   = by_vars_expr
)

#-----------------------------
# Write outputs
#-----------------------------
write_csv(advs_cat1,  "outputs/advs_cat1.csv")
write_csv(advs_cat2,  "outputs/advs_cat2.csv")
write_csv(advs_cat3,  "outputs/advs_cat3.csv")
write_csv(adlb_cat,   "outputs/adlb_cat.csv")
write_csv(advs_cat4,  "outputs/advs_cat4.csv")
write_csv(advs_cat5,  "outputs/advs_cat5.csv")
write_csv(datase_cat, "outputs/datase_cat.csv")

saveRDS(
  list(
    advs_cat1  = advs_cat1,
    advs_cat2  = advs_cat2,
    advs_cat3  = advs_cat3,
    adlb_cat   = adlb_cat,
    advs_cat4  = advs_cat4,
    advs_cat5  = advs_cat5,
    datase_cat = datase_cat
  ),
  file = "outputs/derived_objects.rds"
)

#-----------------------------
# Basic post-write checks
#-----------------------------
stopifnot(
  file.exists("outputs/advs_cat1.csv"),
  file.exists("outputs/advs_cat2.csv"),
  file.exists("outputs/advs_cat3.csv"),
  file.exists("outputs/adlb_cat.csv"),
  file.exists("outputs

library(admiral)

# Read input data

)

#-----------------------------

#-----------------------------

#-----------------------------

```r
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)

#--- Helper: safe write and basic checks ---------------------------------------

safe_write_csv <- function(df, path) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
  write_csv(df, path)
  stopifnot(file.exists(path))
}

safe_save_rds <- function(obj, path) {
  saveRDS(obj, path)
  stopifnot(file.exists(path))
}

#--- Read inputs ---------------------------------------------------------------

dataset_raw <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

crit_nr_df   <- read_tsv("inputs/crit_nr.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE)
condition_df <- read_tsv("inputs/condition.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
description_df <- read_tsv("inputs/description.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
values_yn_df <- read_tsv("inputs/values_yn.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
create_numeric_flag_df <- read_tsv("inputs/create_numeric_flag.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Basic validation of key inputs
stopifnot(
  nrow(condition_df) == 1L,
  "cond" %in% names(condition_df),
  nrow(description_df) >= 1L,
  "description" %in% names(description_df)
)

#--- Derive parameters for derive_vars_crit_flag ------------------------------

# Criterion number y => use first description row as numeric criterion index if possible
crit_nr <- 1L
crit_nr_candidates <- description_df$description
suppressWarnings({
  crit_nr_numeric <- as.numeric(crit_nr_candidates[1])
})
if (!is.na(crit_nr_numeric)) crit_nr <- as.integer(crit_nr_numeric)

# Condition string: only conditions depending on variables in same row are ADaM-compliant
cond_str <- condition_df$cond[1]

# Ensure condition only uses variables present in dataset_raw
vars_in_dataset <- names(dataset_raw)
# Very lightweight check: forbid functions that obviously break row-wise rule
forbidden_tokens <- c("lag", "lead", "cumsum", "cummax", "cummin", "cumprod")
if (any(vapply(forbidden_tokens, grepl, logical(1), x = cond_str))) {
  stop("Condition uses non-row-wise functions; not ADaM compliant.")
}

# Turn condition string into quosured expression to be evaluated row-wise
cond_expr <- parse_expr(cond_str)

# Description: derive a constant text for the criterion
crit_description <- paste0("Criterion ", crit_nr, ": ", cond_str)

# values_yn flag: if any id in values_yn_df is "Y" (or similar), set TRUE, else FALSE
values_yn <- FALSE
if ("id" %in% names(values_yn_df)) {
  yn_vals <- unique(trimws(toupper(values_yn_df$id)))
  if (any(yn_vals %in% c("Y", "YN", "TRUE", "T", "1"))) {
    values_yn <- TRUE
  }
}

# create_numeric_flag: if any numeric >= 1 in create_numeric_flag_df, set TRUE
create_numeric_flag <- FALSE
if ("creat" %in% names(create_numeric_flag_df)) {
  numeric_vals <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(create_numeric_flag_df$creat))
  if (any(!is.na(numeric_vals) & numeric_vals >= 1)) {
    create_numeric_flag <- TRUE
  }
}

#--- Derive criterion flag variables ------------------------------------------

dataset_crit <- derive_vars_crit_flag(
  dataset = dataset_raw,
  crit_nr = crit_nr,
  condition = !!cond_expr,
  description = crit_description,
  values_yn = values_yn,
  create_numeric_flag = create_numeric_flag
)

#--- Write outputs -------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Data frame as CSV
safe_write_csv(dataset_crit, file.path("outputs", "dataset_with_crit_flags.csv"))

# Also save as RDS for complete object preservation
safe_save_rds(dataset_crit, file.path("outputs", "dataset_with_crit_flags.rds"))

#--- Basic post-write checks for unexpected patterns ---------------------------

# Check CRITy, CRITyFL, CRITyFN consistency
crit_var  <- paste0("CRIT", crit_nr)
critfl_var <- paste0("CRIT", crit_nr, "FL")
critfn_var <- paste0("CRIT", crit_nr, "FN")

if (!all(c(crit_var, critfl_var) %in% names(dataset_crit))) {
  stop("Criterion variables were not created as expected.")
}

# Flag values must be Y/NA or Y/N/NA
flag_vals <- unique(dataset_crit[[critfl_var]])
flag_vals <- flag_vals[!is.na(flag_vals)]
if (!all(flag_vals %in% c("Y", "N"))) {
  stop("Unexpected values in criterion flag variable.")
}

# If numeric flag requested, ensure it exists and is numeric
if (create_numeric_flag) {
  if (!critfn_var %in% names(dataset_crit)) {
    stop("Numeric flag requested but not created.")
  }
  if (!is.numeric(dataset_crit[[critfn_var]])) {
    stop("Numeric flag variable is not numeric.")
  }
}

library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)

#--- Helper: safe write and basic checks ---------------------------------------

safe_write_csv <- function(df, path) {
  stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))
  write_csv(df, path)
  stopifnot(file.exists(path))
}

safe_save_rds <- function(obj, path) {
  saveRDS(obj, path)
  stopifnot(file.exists(path))
}

#--- Read inputs ---------------------------------------------------------------

dataset_raw <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

crit_nr_df   <- read_tsv("inputs/crit_nr.tsv",   show_col_types = FALSE)
condition_df <- read_tsv("inputs/condition.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
description_df <- read_tsv("inputs/description.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
values_yn_df <- read_tsv("inputs/values_yn.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
create_numeric_flag_df <- read_tsv("inputs/create_numeric_flag.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Basic validation of key inputs
stopifnot(
  nrow(condition_df) == 1L,
  "cond" %in% names(condition_df),
  nrow(description_df) >= 1L,
  "description" %in% names(description_df)
)

#--- Derive parameters for derive_vars_crit_flag ------------------------------

# Criterion number y => use first description row as numeric criterion index if possible
crit_nr <- 1L
crit_nr_candidates <- description_df$description
suppressWarnings({
  crit_nr_numeric <- as.numeric(crit_nr_candidates[1])
})
if (!is.na(crit_nr_numeric)) crit_nr <- as.integer(crit_nr_numeric)

# Condition string: only conditions depending on variables in same row are ADaM-compliant
cond_str <- condition_df$cond[1]

# Ensure condition only uses variables present in dataset_raw
vars_in_dataset <- names(dataset_raw)
# Very lightweight check: forbid functions that obviously break row-wise rule
forbidden_tokens <- c("lag", "lead", "cumsum", "cummax", "cummin", "cumprod")
if (any(vapply(forbidden_tokens, grepl, logical(1), x = cond_str))) {
  stop("Condition uses non-row-wise functions; not ADaM compliant.")
}

# Turn condition string into quosured expression to be evaluated row-wise
cond_expr <- parse_expr(cond_str)

# Description: derive a constant text for the criterion
crit_description <- paste0("Criterion ", crit_nr, ": ", cond_str)

# values_yn flag: if any id in values_yn_df is "Y" (or similar), set TRUE, else FALSE
values_yn <- FALSE
if ("id" %in% names(values_yn_df)) {
  yn_vals <- unique(trimws(toupper(values_yn_df$id)))
  if (any(yn_vals %in% c("Y", "YN", "TRUE", "T", "1"))) {
    values_yn <- TRUE
  }
}

# create_numeric_flag: if any numeric >= 1 in create_numeric_flag_df, set TRUE
create_numeric_flag <- FALSE
if ("creat" %in% names(create_numeric_flag_df)) {
  numeric_vals <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(create_numeric_flag_df$creat))
  if (any(!is.na(numeric_vals) & numeric_vals >= 1)) {
    create_numeric_flag <- TRUE
  }
}

#--- Derive criterion flag variables ------------------------------------------

dataset_crit <- derive_vars_crit_flag(
  dataset = dataset_raw,
  crit_nr = crit_nr,
  condition = !!cond_expr,
  description = crit_description,
  values_yn = values_yn,
  create_numeric_flag = create_numeric_flag
)

#--- Write outputs -------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Data frame as CSV
safe_write_csv(dataset_crit, file.path("outputs", "dataset_with_crit_flags.csv"))

# Also save as RDS for complete object preservation
safe_save_rds(dataset_crit, file.path("outputs", "dataset_with_crit_flags.rds"))

#--- Basic post-write checks for unexpected patterns ---------------------------

# Check CRITy, CRITyFL, CRITyFN consistency
crit_var  <- paste0("CRIT", crit_nr)
critfl_var <- paste0("CRIT", crit_nr, "FL")
critfn_var <- paste0("CRIT", crit_nr, "FN")

if (!all(c(crit_var, critfl_var) %in% names(dataset_crit))) {
  stop("Criterion variables were not created as expected.")
}

# Flag values must be Y/NA or Y/N/NA
flag_vals <- unique(dataset_crit[[critfl_var]])
flag_vals <- flag_vals[!is.na(flag_vals)]
if (!all(flag_vals %in% c("Y", "N"))) {
  stop("Unexpected values in criterion flag variable.")
}

# If numeric flag requested, ensure it exists and is numeric
if (create_numeric_flag) {
  if (!critfn_var %in% names(dataset_crit)) {
    stop("Numeric flag requested but not created.")
  }
  if (!is.numeric(dataset_crit[[critfn_var]])) {
    stop("Numeric flag variable is not numeric.")
  }
}

prompt:
Write R code to derive/impute a date from a character date using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/new_vars_prefix.tsv, inputs/flag_imputation.tsv). Derive a date (`*DT`) from a character date (`--DTC`). The date can be imputed (see `date_imputation` argument) and the date imputation flag (`*DTF`) can be added. In `{admiral}` we don't allow users to pick any single part of the date/time to impute, we only enable to impute up to a highest level, i.e. you couldn't choose to say impute months, but not days. done for partial dates. library(tibble) library(lubridate) mhdt <- tribble( ~MHSTDTC, "2019-07-18T15:25:40", "2019-07-18T15:25", "2019-07-18", "2019-02", "2019", "2019---07", "" ) derive_vars_dt( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC ) `highest_imputation = "M"` for month imputation is used, i.e. the highest imputation done on a partial date is up to the month. By default, missing date components are imputed to the first day/month/year. A date imputation flag variable, `ASTDTF`, is automatically created. The flag variable indicates if imputation was done on the date. derive_vars_dt( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC, highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "first" ) `date_imputation = "last"`. qsdt <- tribble( ~QSDTC, "2019-07-18T15:25:40", "2019-07-18T15:25", "2019-07-18", "2019-02", "2019", "2019---07", "" ) derive_vars_dt( qsdt, new_vars_prefix = "A", dtc = QSDTC, highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "last" ) imputation flag (`flag_imputation = "none"`) (`*DTF`) suppressed. Since `date_imputation = "mid"`, partial date imputation will be set to June 30th for missing month and 15th for missing day only. The `flag_imputation = "none"` call ensures no date imputation flag is created. In practice, as per CDISC requirements this option can only be selected if the imputed variable is not part of the final ADaM dataset. exdt <- tribble( ~EXSTDTC, "2019-07-18T15:25:40", "2019-07-18T15:25", "2019-07-18", "2019-02", "2019", "2019---07", "" ) derive_vars_dt( exdt, new_vars_prefix = "TRTS", dtc = EXSTDTC, highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "mid", flag_imputation = "none" ) `date_imputation = "04-06"`. Note that day portion, `"-06"`, is used in the imputation of the record with `"2019-02"`. derive_vars_dt( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC, highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "04-06" ) need of imputation. Using `min_dates = exprs(TRTSDTM)`, we are telling the function to apply the treatment start date `(TRTSDTM)` as a lower boundary for imputation via the `min_dates` argument. This means: - For partial dates that could potentially include `TRTSDTM` (case 1 & 2), the imputed date is adjusted to `TRTSDTM` - For partial dates that are entirely before `TRTSDTM` (case 3 & 4), standard imputation rules apply without adjustment - For partial dates that are entirely after `TRTSDTM` (case 5), standard imputation rules apply adae <- tribble( ~case, ~AESTDTC, ~TRTSDTM, 1, "2020-12", ymd_hms("2020-12-06T12:12:12"), 2, "2020", ymd_hms("2020-12-06T12:12:12"), 3, "2020-11", ymd_hms("2020-12-06T12:12:12"), 4, "2020-01", ymd_hms("2020-12-06T12:12:12"), 5, "2021-01", ymd_hms("2020-12-06T12:12:12") ) derive_vars_dt( adae, dtc = AESTDTC, new_vars_prefix = "AST", highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "first", min_dates = exprs(TRTSDTM) ) need of imputation. Using `max_dates = exprs(TRTEDTM)`, we are telling the function to apply the treatment end date `(TRTEDTM)` as an upper boundary for imputation via the `max_dates` argument. This means: - For partial dates that could potentially include `TRTEDTM` (case 1 & 2), the imputed date is adjusted to `TRTEDTM` - For partial dates that are entirely before `TRTEDTM` (case 3 & 4), standard imputation rules apply without adjustment - For partial dates that are entirely after `TRTEDTM` (case 5), standard imputation rules apply adae <- tribble( ~case, ~AESTDTC, ~TRTSDTM, ~TRTEDTM, 1, "2020-12", ymd_hms("2020-01-01T12:12:12"), ymd_hms("2020-12-20T23:59:59"), 2, "2020", ymd_hms("2020-01-01T12:12:12"), ymd_hms("2020-12-20T23:59:59"), 3, "2020-11", ymd_hms("2020-01-01T12:12:12"), ymd_hms("2020-12-20T23:59:59"), 4, "2020-01", ymd_hms("2020-01-01T12:12:12"), ymd_hms("2020-12-20T23:59:59"), 5, "2021-01", ymd_hms("2020-01-01T12:12:12"), ymd_hms("2020-12-20T23:59:59") ) derive_vars_dt( adae, dtc = AESTDTC, new_vars_prefix = "AST", highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "last", max_dates = exprs(TRTEDTM) ) For example, `"2019---07"`, will be displayed as `"2019-06-07"` rather than `"2019-06-30"` with `preserve = TRUE` and `date_imputation = "mid"` . derive_vars_dt( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC, highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "mid", preserve = TRUE ) `vignette("imputation")`. Additional details: The presence of a `*DTF` variable is checked and if it already exists in the input dataset, a warning is issued and `*DTF` will be overwritten. Use admiral's derive_vars_dt function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("dtc"))`), new_vars_prefix (Prefix used for the output variable(s). A character scalar is expected.), flag_imputation (Whether the date imputation flag must also be derived.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the date `*DT` (and the date imputation flag `*DTF` if requested) added.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

```r
library(admiral)
library(tibble)
library(lubridate)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)

#-------------------------
# Helper functions
#-------------------------

validate_required_cols <- function(df, required, df_name = deparse(substitute(df))) {
  missing <- setdiff(required, names(df))
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Required column(s) %s not found in %s",
        paste(missing, collapse = ", "),
        df_name
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
}

safe_write <- function(x, path_csv, path_rds = NULL) {
  dir.create(dirname(path_csv), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  write_csv(x, path_csv, na = "")
  if (!is.null(path_rds)) {
    saveRDS(x, path_rds)
  }

  if (!file.exists(path_csv)) {
    stop(sprintf("Failed to write output CSV: %s", path_csv), call. = FALSE)
  }
  if (!is.null(path_rds) && !file.exists(path_rds)) {
    stop(sprintf("Failed to write output RDS: %s", path_rds), call. = FALSE)
  }
}

check_dt_results <- function(df, dt_var, dtc_var) {
  if (!dt_var %in% names(df)) {
    stop(sprintf("Derived date variable %s not found.", dt_var), call. = FALSE)
  }
  # basic pattern checks
  if (any(is.na(df[[dtc_var]]) & !is.na(df[[dt_var]]))) {
    warning(
      sprintf(
        "Some records have NA in %s but non-NA in %s; check imputation.",
        dtc_var, dt_var
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  invisible(TRUE)
}

#-------------------------
# Read inputs
#-------------------------

datase_path <- file.path("inputs", "datase.tsv")
new_vars_prefix_path <- file.path("inputs", "new_vars_prefix.tsv")
flag_imputation_path <- file.path("inputs", "flag_imputation.tsv")

datase <- read_tsv(datase_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
new_vars_prefix_df <- read_tsv(new_vars_prefix_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
flag_imputation_df <- read_tsv(flag_imputation_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

#-------------------------
# Prepare parameters
#-------------------------

# Expect a column named 'dtc' as per requirement;
# if not present, attempt to construct it from ADT (YYYY-MM-DD) just for demo.
if (!"dtc" %in% names(datase)) {
  if ("ADT" %in% names(datase)) {
    datase <- datase %>%
      mutate(
        dtc = if_else(
          is.na(ADT),
          NA_character_,
          format(as.Date(ADT), "%Y-%m-%d")
        )
      )
  } else {
    stop(
      "Neither 'dtc' nor 'ADT' found in datase; cannot proceed.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
}

validate_required_cols(datase, c("dtc"))

# new_vars_prefix: use first non-NA value from the file
validate_required_cols(new_vars_prefix_df, c("new_vars_prefix"), "new_vars_prefix_df")
new_vars_prefix <- new_vars_prefix_df$new_vars_prefix[1]
if (is.na(new_vars_prefix) || !nzchar(new_vars_prefix)) {
  stop("new_vars_prefix is missing or empty in new_vars_prefix.tsv", call. = FALSE)
}

# flag_imputation: decide whether to create *DTF
# If any variable named "dtf" is listed in flag_imputation, we enable flag;
# here just check whether "DT" is in the list to indicate date imputation flag usage.
validate_required_cols(flag_imputation_df, c("flag_imputation"), "flag_imputation_df")
flag_vars <- flag_imputation_df$flag_imputation

flag_imputation_setting <- if ("DTF" %in% flag_vars) "all" else "all"  # default to create flag

#-------------------------
# Derive / impute dates: multiple examples
#-------------------------

# 1) Basic derivation, no imputation arguments (default behavior)
datase_dt_basic <- derive_vars_dt(
  dataset        = datase,
  dtc            = dtc,
  new_vars_prefix = new_vars_prefix
)

check_dt_results(datase_dt_basic, dt_var = paste0(new_vars_prefix, "DT"), dtc_var = "dtc")

safe_write(
  datase_dt_basic,
  path_csv = file.path("outputs", "datase_dt_basic.csv"),
  path_rds = file.path("outputs", "datase_dt_basic.rds")
)

# 2) highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "first"
datase_dt_firstM <- derive_vars_dt(
  dataset          = datase,
  dtc              = dtc,
  new_vars_prefix  = new_vars_prefix,
  highest_imputation = "M",
  date_imputation  = "first"
)

check_dt_results(datase_dt_firstM, dt_var = paste0(new_vars_prefix, "DT"), dtc_var = "dtc")

safe_write(
  datase_dt_firstM,
  path_csv = file.path("outputs", "datase_dt_firstM.csv"),
  path_rds = file.path("outputs", "datase_dt_firstM.rds")
)

# 3) highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "last"

  dataset          = datase,

)

check_dt_results(datase_dt_lastM, dt_var = paste0(new_vars_prefix, "DT"), dtc_var = "dtc")

safe_write(
  datase_dt_lastM,
  path_csv = file.path("outputs", "datase_dt_lastM.csv"),
  path_rds = file.path("outputs", "datase_dt_lastM.rds")
)

# 4) highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "mid", flag_imputation = "none"
datase_dt_midM_noflag <- derive_vars_dt(

)

check_dt_results(datase_dt_0406, dt_var = paste0(new_vars_prefix, "DT"), dtc_var = "dtc")

#-------------------------

validate_required_cols <- function(df, required, df_name = deparse(substitute(df))) {

}

safe_write <- function(x, path_csv, path_rds = NULL) {
  dir.create(dirname(path_csv), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

  }
  if (!is.null(path_rds) && !file.exists(path_rds)) {

}

check_dt_results <- function(df, dt_var, dtc_var) {
  if (!dt_var %in% names(df)) {
    stop(sprintf("Derived date variable %s not found.", dt_var), call. = FALSE)
  }

      mutate(
        dtc = if_else(
          is.na(ADT),

flag_imputation_setting <- if ("DTF" %in% flag_vars) "all" else "all"  # default to create flag

#-------------------------

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)

# Helper to read single-column TSVs into vectors
read_vector_tsv <- function(path, colname) {
  x <- readr::read_tsv(
    path,
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  )
  if (!colname %in% names(x)) {
    stop("Expected column `", colname, "` in ", path)
  }
  x[[colname]] %>%
    discard(is.na) %>%
    unique()
}

# Create outputs directory if missing
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# 1. Read input data
#-----------------------------
datase <- tryCatch(
  readr::read_tsv(
    "inputs/datase.tsv",
    col_types = cols(
      USUBJID = col_character(),
      STUDYID = col_character(),
      PARAMCD = col_character(),
      PARAM   = col_character(),
      AVAL    = col_double(),
      AVISITN = col_double(),
      AVISIT  = col_character(),
      ADT     = col_date(),
      ANL01FL = col_character(),
      BASE    = col_double(),
      CHG     = col_double(),
      PCHG    = col_double(),
      ABLFL   = col_character()
    )
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading inputs/datase.tsv: ", e$message)
)

dataset_add <- tryCatch(
  readr::read_tsv(
    "inputs/dataset_add.tsv",
    col_types = cols(
      USUBJID = col_character(),
      STUDYID = col_character(),
      PARAMCD = col_character(),
      PARAM   = col_character(),
      AVAL    = col_double(),
      AVISITN = col_double(),
      AVISIT  = col_character(),
      ADT     = col_date(),
      ANL01FL = col_character(),
      BASE    = col_double(),
      CHG     = col_double(),
      PCHG    = col_double(),
      ABLFL   = col_character()
    )
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading inputs/dataset_add.tsv: ", e$message)
)

filter_add_df <- tryCatch(
  readr::read_tsv(
    "inputs/filter_add.tsv",
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading inputs/filter_add.tsv: ", e$message)
)

parameters <- read_vector_tsv("inputs/parameters.tsv", "parameters")
constant_parameters <- read_vector_tsv("inputs/constant_parameters.tsv", "parameters")
by_vars_chr <- read_vector_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv", "by_vars")
constant_by_vars_chr <- read_vector_tsv("inputs/constant_by_vars.tsv", "by_vars")

# new_vars.tsv is ambiguous in the preview; treat it as a list of
# variables that will be newly created and retained.
# Here we assume it lists variable names.
new_vars_chr <- read_vector_tsv("inputs/new_vars.tsv", "by_vars")

#-----------------------------
# 2. Basic validation
#-----------------------------
# check that by variables exist
missing_by <- setdiff(by_vars_chr, names(datase))
if (length(missing_by) > 0) {
  stop("Missing by_vars in datase: ", paste(missing_by, collapse = ", "))
}

missing_by_add <- setdiff(by_vars_chr, names(dataset_add))
if (length(missing_by_add) > 0) {
  stop("Missing by_vars in dataset_add: ", paste(missing_by_add, collapse = ", "))
}

missing_cby <- setdiff(constant_by_vars_chr, names(datase))
if (length(missing_cby) > 0) {
  stop("Missing constant_by_vars in datase: ", paste(missing_cby, collapse = ", "))
}

missing_cby_add <- setdiff(constant_by_vars_chr, names(dataset_add))
if (length(missing_cby_add) > 0) {
  stop("Missing constant_by_vars in dataset_add: ", paste(missing_cby_add, collapse = ", "))
}

# check PARAMCD coverage
if (!"PARAMCD" %in% names(datase) || !"PARAMCD" %in% names(dataset_add)) {
  stop("Both datase and dataset_add must contain PARAMCD.")
}

missing_param_datase <- setdiff(parameters, unique(datase$PARAMCD))
if (length(missing_param_datase) > 0) {
  warning(
    "Some required parameters not found in datase: ",
    paste(missing_param_datase, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

missing_param_add <- setdiff(parameters, unique(dataset_add$PARAMCD))
if (length(missing_param_add) > 0) {
  warning(
    "Some required parameters not found in dataset_add: ",
    paste(missing_param_add, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

missing_cparam_datase <- setdiff(constant_parameters, unique(datase$PARAMCD))
if (length(missing_cparam_datase) > 0) {
  warning(
    "Some constant parameters not found in datase: ",
    paste(missing_cparam_datase, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

missing_cparam_add <- setdiff(constant_parameters, unique(dataset_add$PARAMCD))
if (length(missing_cparam_add) > 0) {
  warning(
    "Some constant parameters not found in dataset_add: ",
    paste(missing_cparam_add, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

#-----------------------------
# 3. Build filter expression
#-----------------------------
filter_expr_chr <- paste(na.omit(filter_add_df$filter), collapse = " & ")
if (filter_expr_chr == "") {
  filter_expr <- quote(TRUE)
} else {
  filter_expr <- parse(text = filter_expr_chr)[[1]]
}

#-----------------------------
# 4. Example: compute MAP (mean arterial pressure)
#    from SYSBP and DIABP as:
#    MAP = (2 * DIABP + SYSBP) / 3
#    on a per-visit basis using dataset_add AVALs
#-----------------------------
# Prepare dataset_add filtered and pivoted
dataset_add_f <- dataset_add %>%
  filter(!!filter_expr) %>%
  filter(PARAMCD %in% parameters)

# wide format for computation
add_wide <- dataset_add_f %>%
  select(all_of(by_vars_chr), PARAMCD, AVAL) %>%
  filter(PARAMCD %in% c("SYSBP", "DIABP")) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = PARAMCD,
    values_from = AVAL
  )

# merge constant parameters (e.g., PULSE) as constant across visits
const_add <- dataset_add %>%
  filter(PARAMCD %in% constant_parameters) %>%
  select(all_of(constant_by_vars_chr), PARAMCD, AVAL) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = PARAMCD,
    values_from = AVAL,
    names_prefix = "CONST_"
  )

# join constants to wide table where possible
const_key <- intersect(constant_by_vars_chr, by_vars_chr)
if (length(const_key) > 0) {
  add_wide <- add_wide %>%
    left_join(const_add, by = const_key)
}

# compute new variable(s); here MAP and SYS_DIAB_RATIO as examples
add_comp <- add_wide %>%
  mutate(
    MAP = if_else(
      !is.na(SYSBP) & !is.na(DIABP),
      (2 * DIABP + SYSBP) / 3,
      NA_real_
    ),
    SYS_DIAB_RATIO = if_else(
      !is.na(SYSBP) & !is.na(DIABP) & DIABP != 0,
      SYSBP / DIABP,
      NA_real_
    )
  )

# restrict to vars to merge back
vars_to_merge <- c(by_vars_chr, intersect(new_vars_chr, names(add_comp)))
vars_to_merge <- unique(vars_to_merge[vars_to_merge %in% names(add_comp)])

if (length(setdiff(intersect(new_vars_chr, c("MAP", "SYS_DIAB_RATIO")), vars_to_merge)) > 0) {
  warning("Not all requested new_vars are available in computed dataset.")
}

add_comp_sub <- add_comp %>%
  select(all_of(vars_to_merge))

#-----------------------------
# 5. Use admiral::derive_vars_computed
#-----------------------------
# Build quosures for by_vars and constant_by_vars
by_vars_syms <- rlang::syms(by_vars_chr)
constant_by_vars_syms <- rlang::syms(constant_by_vars_chr)

# Map the newly computed variables into a list for new_vars argument
# admiral expects something like exprs(MAP = MAP, SYS_DIAB_RATIO = SYS_DIAB_RATIO)
new_vars_expr <- rlang::set_names(
  rlang::syms(intersect(new_vars_chr, names(add_comp_sub))),
  intersect(new_vars_chr, names(add_comp_sub))
)

# Derive vars
result_df <- admiral::derive_vars_computed(
  dataset = datase,
  dataset_add = add_comp_sub,
  filter_add = !!filter_expr,
  parameters = parameters,
  by_vars = !!!by_vars_syms,
  constant_parameters = constant

  select(all_of(by_vars_chr), PARAMCD, AVAL) %>%
  filter(PARAMCD %in% c("SYSBP", "DIABP")) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = PARAMCD,
    values_from = AVAL
  )

# merge constant parameters (e.g., PULSE) as constant across visits
const_add <- dataset_add %>%
  filter(PARAMCD %in% constant_parameters) %>%
  select(all_of(constant_by_vars_chr), PARAMCD, AVAL) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = PARAMCD,
    values_from = AVAL,
    names_prefix = "CONST_"
  )

# join constants to wide table where possible
const_key <- intersect(constant_by_vars_chr, by_vars_chr)
if (length(const_key) > 0) {
  add_wide <- add_wide %>%
    left_join(const_add, by = const_key)
}

# compute new variable(s); here MAP and SYS_DIAB_RATIO as examples
add_comp <- add_wide %>%
  mutate(
    MAP = if_else(
      !is.na(SYSBP) & !is.na(DIABP),
      (2 * DIABP + SYSBP) / 3,
      NA_real_
    ),
    SYS_DIAB_RATIO = if_else(
      !is.na(SYSBP) & !is.na(DIABP) & DIABP != 0,
      SYSBP / DIABP,
      NA_real_
    )
  )

# restrict to vars to merge back
vars_to_merge <- c(by_vars_chr, intersect(new_vars_chr, names(add_comp)))
vars_to_merge <- unique(vars_to_merge[vars_to_merge %in% names(add_comp)])

if (length(setdiff(intersect(new_vars_chr, c("MAP", "SYS_DIAB_RATIO")), vars_to_merge)) > 0) {
  warning("Not all requested new_vars are available in computed dataset.")
}

add_comp_sub <- add_comp %>%
  select(all_of(vars_to_merge))

#-----------------------------
# 5. Use admiral::derive_vars_computed
#-----------------------------
# Build quosures for by_vars and constant_by_vars
by_vars_syms <- rlang::syms(by_vars_chr)
constant_by_vars_syms <- rlang::syms(constant_by_vars_chr)

# Map the newly computed variables into a list for new_vars argument
# admiral expects something like exprs(MAP = MAP, SYS_DIAB_RATIO = SYS_DIAB_RATIO)
new_vars_expr <- rlang::set_names(
  rlang::syms(intersect(new_vars_chr, names(add_comp_sub))),
  intersect(new_vars_chr, names(add_comp_sub))
)

# Derive vars
result_df <- admiral::derive_vars_computed(
  dataset = datase,
  dataset_add = add_comp_sub,
  filter_add = !!filter_expr,
  parameters = parameters,
  by_vars = !!!by_vars_syms,

```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(rlang)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)

#---------------------------
# Helper functions
#---------------------------

read_tsv_safe <- function(file, col_types = cols(.default = col_guess())) {
  if (!file.exists(file)) {
    stop("Input file not found: ", file)
  }
  tryCatch(
    readr::read_tsv(file, col_types = col_types, progress = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop("Failed to read TSV file: ", file, " | ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
}

validate_required_cols <- function(df, req, df_name) {
  missing_cols <- setdiff(req, names(df))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Missing required column(s) in ", df_name, ": ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }
  invisible(df)
}

#---------------------------
# Read inputs
#---------------------------

datase_path          <- "inputs/datase.tsv"
reference_date_path  <- "inputs/reference_date.tsv"
source_vars_path     <- "inputs/source_vars.tsv"

datase <- read_tsv_safe(
  datase_path,
  col_types = cols(
    USUBJID  = col_character(),
    STUDYID  = col_character(),
    PARAMCD  = col_character(),
    PARAM    = col_character(),
    AVAL     = col_double(),
    AVISITN  = col_double(),
    AVISIT   = col_character(),
    ADT      = col_date(format = ""),
    ANL01FL  = col_character(),
    BASE     = col_double(),
    CHG      = col_double(),
    PCHG     = col_double(),
    ABLFL    = col_character()
  )
)

reference_date_df <- read_tsv_safe(reference_date_path)
source_vars_df    <- read_tsv_safe(source_vars_path)

#---------------------------
# Validate inputs
#---------------------------

validate_required_cols(datase,
  req = c("USUBJID", "ADT"),
  df_name = "datase"
)

validate_required_cols(reference_date_df,
  req = c("reference_date"),
  df_name = "reference_date"
)

validate_required_cols(source_vars_df,
  req = c("source_var"),
  df_name = "source_vars"
)

ref_candidates <- setdiff(reference_date_df$reference_date, names(datase))
if (length(ref_candidates) > 0) {
  stop(
    "The following reference_date names are not present in datase: ",
    paste(ref_candidates, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

src_candidates <- setdiff(source_vars_df$source_var, names(datase))
if (length(src_candidates) > 0) {
  stop(
    "The following source_vars are not present in datase: ",
    paste(src_candidates, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# At this point we know all reference_date and source_vars names exist in datase

#---------------------------
# Construct parameters for derive_vars_dy
#---------------------------

# For this example we will assume a single reference_date variable
# (first row in reference_date.tsv)
ref_var_name <- reference_date_df$reference_date[1]

# Ensure the reference_date column is of Date or POSIXct class
if (!inherits(datase[[ref_var_name]], c("Date", "POSIXct", "POSIXt"))) {
  # If the column is numeric or character, try to coerce it to Date
  datase[[ref_var_name]] <- as.Date(datase[[ref_var_name]])
  if (all(is.na(datase[[ref_var_name]]))) {
    stop("reference_date column '", ref_var_name, "' could not be coerced to Date.")
  }
}

# Build exprs() for source_vars
src_syms <- syms(source_vars_df$source_var)
src_exprs <- exprs(!!!src_syms)

#---------------------------
# Derive relative day variables
#---------------------------

datase_dy <- tryCatch(
  {
    derive_vars_dy(
      dataset        = datase,
      reference_date = !!sym(ref_var_name),
      source_vars    = src_exprs
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_vars_dy: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

#---------------------------
# Basic validation of results
#---------------------------

# Check that *DY variables were created for the provided source_vars
dy_expected <- map_chr(source_vars_df$source_var, function(x) {
  str_replace(x, "DTM?", "DY")
})
dy_missing <- setdiff(dy_expected, names(datase_dy))

if (length(dy_missing) > 0) {
  stop("Expected DY variables not found after derive_vars_dy: ",
       paste(dy_missing, collapse = ", "))
}

# Quick sanity check for unexpected patterns (e.g., all NA, extreme values)
dy_summary <- datase_dy %>%
  select(any_of(dy_expected)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), list(
    n_nonmiss = ~sum(!is.na(.)),
    min       = ~suppressWarnings(min(., na.rm = TRUE)),
    max       = ~suppressWarnings(max(., na.rm = TRUE))
  )))

if (any(dy_summary[1, ] == 0)) {
  warning("One or more DY variables have zero non-missing values; please check input data / reference_date definition.")
}

#---------------------------
# Write outputs
#---------------------------

if (!dir.exists("outputs")) {
  dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)
}

datase_csv_path <- "outputs/datase_with_dy.csv"
datase_rds_path <- "outputs/datase_with_dy.rds"

write_csv(datase_dy, datase_csv_path, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_dy, datase_rds_path)

#---------------------------
# Confirm outputs
#---------------------------

if (!file.exists(datase_csv_path)) {
  stop("Output CSV file was not written: ", datase_csv_path)
}
if (!file.exists(datase_rds_path)) {
  stop("Output RDS file was not written: ", datase_rds_path)
}

datase_csv_check <- tryCatch(
  read_csv(datase_csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to re-read output CSV: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

datase_rds_check <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(datase_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to re-read output RDS: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

if (!all(dy_expected %in% names(datase_csv_check))) {
  stop("Re-read CSV is missing expected DY variables.")
}
if (!all(dy_expected %in% names(datase_rds_check))) {
  stop("Re-read RDS is missing expected DY variables.")
}

# Optional: check that CSV and original derived object have same row count
if (nrow(datase_csv_check) != nrow(datase_dy)) {
  warning("Row count mismatch between in-memory datase_dy and CSV output.")
}
```
solution:
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(rlang)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)

#---------------------------
# Helper functions
#---------------------------

read_tsv_safe <- function(file, col_types = cols(.default = col_guess())) {
  if (!file.exists(file)) {
    stop("Input file not found: ", file)
  }
  tryCatch(
    readr::read_tsv(file, col_types = col_types, progress = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop("Failed to read TSV file: ", file, " | ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
}

validate_required_cols <- function(df, req, df_name) {
  missing_cols <- setdiff(req, names(df))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Missing required column(s) in ", df_name, ": ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }
  invisible(df)
}

#---------------------------
# Read inputs
#---------------------------

datase_path          <- "inputs/datase.tsv"
reference_date_path  <- "inputs/reference_date.tsv"
source_vars_path     <- "inputs/source_vars.tsv"

datase <- read_tsv_safe(
  datase_path,
  col_types = cols(
    USUBJID  = col_character(),
    STUDYID  = col_character(),
    PARAMCD  = col_character(),
    PARAM    = col_character(),
    AVAL     = col_double(),
    AVISITN  = col_double(),
    AVISIT   = col_character(),
    ADT      = col_date(format = ""),
    ANL01FL  = col_character(),
    BASE     = col_double(),
    CHG      = col_double(),
    PCHG     = col_double(),
    ABLFL    = col_character()
  )
)

reference_date_df <- read_tsv_safe(reference_date_path)
source_vars_df    <- read_tsv_safe(source_vars_path)

#---------------------------
# Validate inputs
#---------------------------

validate_required_cols(datase,
  req = c("USUBJID", "ADT"),
  df_name = "datase"
)

validate_required_cols(reference_date_df,
  req = c("reference_date"),
  df_name = "reference_date"
)

validate_required_cols(source_vars_df,
  req = c("source_var"),
  df_name = "source_vars"
)

ref_candidates <- setdiff(reference_date_df$reference_date, names(datase))
if (length(ref_candidates) > 0) {
  stop(
    "The following reference_date names are not present in datase: ",
    paste(ref_candidates, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

src_candidates <- setdiff(source_vars_df$source_var, names(datase))
if (length(src_candidates) > 0) {
  stop(
    "The following source_vars are not present in datase: ",
    paste(src_candidates, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

# At this point we know all reference_date and source_vars names exist in datase

#---------------------------
# Construct parameters for derive_vars_dy
#---------------------------

# For this example we will assume a single reference_date variable
# (first row in reference_date.tsv)
ref_var_name <- reference_date_df$reference_date[1]

# Ensure the reference_date column is of Date or POSIXct class
if (!inherits(datase[[ref_var_name]], c("Date", "POSIXct", "POSIXt"))) {
  # If the column is numeric or character, try to coerce it to Date
  datase[[ref_var_name]] <- as.Date(datase[[ref_var_name]])
  if (all(is.na(datase[[ref_var_name]]))) {
    stop("reference_date column '", ref_var_name, "' could not be coerced to Date.")
  }
}

# Build exprs() for source_vars
src_syms <- syms(source_vars_df$source_var)
src_exprs <- exprs(!!!src_syms)

#---------------------------
# Derive relative day variables
#---------------------------

datase_dy <- tryCatch(
  {
    derive_vars_dy(
      dataset        = datase,
      reference_date = !!sym(ref_var_name),
      source_vars    = src_exprs
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_vars_dy: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

#---------------------------
# Basic validation of results
#---------------------------

# Check that *DY variables were created for the provided source_vars
dy_expected <- map_chr(source_vars_df$source_var, function(x) {
  str_replace(x, "DTM?", "DY")
})
dy_missing <- setdiff(dy_expected, names(datase_dy))

if (length(dy_missing) > 0) {
  stop("Expected DY variables not found after derive_vars_dy: ",
       paste(dy_missing, collapse = ", "))
}

# Quick sanity check for unexpected patterns (e.g., all NA, extreme values)
dy_summary <- datase_dy %>%
  select(any_of(dy_expected)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), list(
    n_nonmiss = ~sum(!is.na(.)),
    min       = ~suppressWarnings(min(., na.rm = TRUE)),
    max       = ~suppressWarnings(max(., na.rm = TRUE))
  )))

if (any(dy_summary[1, ] == 0)) {
  warning("One or more DY variables have zero non-missing values; please check input data / reference_date definition.")
}

#---------------------------
# Write outputs
#---------------------------

if (!dir.exists("outputs")) {
  dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)
}

datase_csv_path <- "outputs/datase_with_dy.csv"
datase_rds_path <- "outputs/datase_with_dy.rds"

write_csv(datase_dy, datase_csv_path, na = "")
saveRDS(datase_dy, datase_rds_path)

#---------------------------
# Confirm outputs
#---------------------------

if (!file.exists(datase_csv_path)) {
  stop("Output CSV file was not written: ", datase_csv_path)
}
if (!file.exists(datase_rds_path)) {
  stop("Output RDS file was not written: ", datase_rds_path)
}

datase_csv_check <- tryCatch(
  read_csv(datase_csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to re-read output CSV: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

datase_rds_check <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(datase_rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to re-read output RDS: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

if (!all(dy_expected %in% names(datase_csv_check))) {
  stop("Re-read CSV is missing expected DY variables.")
}
if (!all(dy_expected %in% names(datase_rds_check))) {
  stop("Re-read RDS is missing expected DY variables.")
}

# Optional: check that CSV and original derived object have same row count
if (nrow(datase_csv_check) != nrow(datase_dy)) {
  warning("Row count mismatch between in-memory datase_dy and CSV output.")
}

Write R code to derive duration using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/new_var.tsv, inputs/new_var_unit.tsv). Derives duration between two dates, specified by the variables present in input dataset e.g., duration of adverse events, relative day, age, ... Additional details: specified output unit. If the end date is before the start date, the duration is negative. The start and end date variable must be present in the specified input dataset. The [lubridate](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/) package calculates two types of spans between two dates: duration and interval. While these calculations are largely the same, when the unit of the time period is month or year the result can be slightly different. The difference arises from the ambiguity in the length of `"1 month"` or `"1 year"`. Months may have 31, 30, 28, or 29 days, and years are 365 days and 366 during leap years. Durations and intervals help solve the ambiguity in these measures. The **interval** between `2000-02-01` and `2000-03-01` is `1` (i.e. one month). The **duration** between these two dates is `0.95`, which accounts for the fact that the year 2000 is a leap year, February has 29 days, and the average month length is `30.4375`, i.e. `29 / 30.4375 = 0.95`. For additional details, review the [lubridate time span reference page](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/timespan.html). Use admiral's derive_vars_duration function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("start_date", "end_date"))`), new_var (Name of variable to create), new_var_unit (Name of the unit variable If the parameter is not specified, no variable for the unit is created.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the duration and unit variable added. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

Write R code to derive/impute a datetime from a character date using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/new_vars_prefix.tsv, inputs/flag_imputation.tsv). Derive a datetime object (`*DTM`) from a character date (`--DTC`). The date and time can be imputed (see `date_imputation`/`time_imputation` arguments) and the date/time imputation flag (`*DTF`, `*TMF`) can be added. In `{admiral}` we don't allow users to pick any single part of the date/time to impute, we only enable to impute up to a highest level, i.e. you couldn't choose to say impute months, but not days. imputes missing time components to `00` but doesn't impute missing date components and automatically produces the time imputation flag (`ASTTMF`). library(tibble) library(lubridate) mhdt <- tribble( ~MHSTDTC, "2019-07-18T15:25", "2019-07-18", "2019-02", "2019", "2019---07", "" ) derive_vars_dtm( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC ) for partial dates. We also set `time_imputation = "last"`. The function will use all or part of `23:59:59` for time imputation. Note that `highest_imputation` must be at least `"D"` to perform date imputation. Here we use `highest_imputation = "M"` to request imputation of month and day (and time). Also note that two flag variables are created. By default `ASTTMF` is set to `NA` if only seconds are imputed. Set `ignore_seconds_flag = FALSE` to have the `"S"` flag for `ASTTMF`. derive_vars_dtm( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC, date_imputation = "last", time_imputation = "last", highest_imputation = "M" ) function appends missing `"hh:mm:ss"` to `ASTDTM`. The `flag_imputation = "none"` call ensures no date/time imputation flag is created. In practice, as per CDISC requirements this option can only be selected if the imputed variable is not part of the final ADaM dataset. derive_vars_dtm( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC, flag_imputation = "none" ) To ensure that the imputed date is not after the death or data cut off date we can set `max_dates = exprs(DTHDT, DCUTDT)`. Note two flag variables: `ASTDTF` and `ASTTMF` are created. Setting `highest_imputation = "Y"` will allow for the missing `AEENDTC` record to be imputed from `max_dates = exprs(DTHDT, DCUTDT)`. adae <- tribble( ~AEENDTC, ~DTHDT, ~DCUTDT, "2020-12", ymd("2020-12-26"), ymd("2020-12-24"), "2020-11", ymd("2020-12-06"), ymd("2020-12-24"), "", ymd("2020-12-06"), ymd("2020-12-24"), "2020-12-20", ymd("2020-12-06"), ymd("2020-12-24") ) derive_vars_dtm( adae, dtc = AEENDTC, new_vars_prefix = "AEN", highest_imputation = "Y", date_imputation = "last", time_imputation = "last", max_dates = exprs(DTHDT, DCUTDT) ) seconds in the `ASTTMF` variable. The default value of `ignore_seconds_flag` is `TRUE` so the `"S"` is not normally displayed. The ADaM IG states that given SDTM (`--DTC`) variable, if only hours and minutes are ever collected, and seconds are imputed in (`*DTM`) as `00`, then it is not necessary to set (`*TMF`) to `"S"`. mhdt <- tribble( ~MHSTDTC, "2019-07-18T15:25", "2019-07-18", "2019-02", "2019", "2019---07", "" ) derive_vars_dtm( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC, highest_imputation = "M", ignore_seconds_flag = FALSE ) and impute time as last (`23:59:59`) with `time_imputation = "last"`. We use the `preserve` argument to "preserve" partial dates. For example, `"2019---18T15:-:05"`, will be displayed as `"2019-06-18 15:59:05"` by setting `preserve = TRUE`. mhdt <- tribble( ~MHSTDTC, "2019-07-18T15:25", "2019---18T15:-:05", "2019-07-18", "2019-02", "2019", "2019---07", "" ) derive_vars_dtm( mhdt, new_vars_prefix = "AST", dtc = MHSTDTC, highest_imputation = "M", date_imputation = "mid", time_imputation = "last", preserve = TRUE, ignore_seconds_flag = FALSE ) `vignette("imputation")`. Additional details: The presence of a `*DTF` variable is checked and the variable is not derived if it already exists in the input dataset. However, if `*TMF` already exists in the input dataset, a warning is issued and `*TMF` will be overwritten. Use admiral's derive_vars_dtm function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("dtc"))`), new_vars_prefix (Prefix used for the output variable(s). A character scalar is expected.), flag_imputation (Whether the date/time imputation flag(s) must also be derived.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the datetime `*DTM` (and the date/time imputation flag `*DTF`, `*TMF`) added.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

```r
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)
library(tibble)
library(lubridate)

# Create output directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# 1. Read input data
#-----------------------------
datase_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "datase.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading datase.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

dtc_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "dtc.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading dtc.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

new_vars_prefix_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "new_vars_prefix.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading new_vars_prefix.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

flag_imputation_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "flag_imputation.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading flag_imputation.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

mhdt_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "mhdt.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading mhdt.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

#-----------------------------
# 2. Basic validation
#-----------------------------
if (!"dtc" %in% names(dtc_raw)) {
  stop("dtc.tsv must contain a 'dtc' column.")
}

if (!"new_vars_prefix" %in% names(new_vars_prefix_raw)) {
  stop("new_vars_prefix.tsv must contain a 'new_vars_prefix' column.")
}

if (!"flag_imputation" %in% names(flag_imputation_raw)) {
  stop("flag_imputation.tsv must contain a 'flag_imputation' column.")
}

if (!"dt" %in% names(mhdt_raw)) {
  stop("mhdt.tsv must contain a 'dt' column.")
}

# use first value as prefix and flag handling option
new_prefix <- new_vars_prefix_raw$new_vars_prefix[1]
if (is.na(new_prefix) || new_prefix == "") {
  stop("new_vars_prefix must be a non-empty string.")
}

flag_imp_setting <- flag_imputation_raw$flag_imputation[1]
if (is.na(flag_imp_setting) || flag_imp_setting == "") {
  # default to "all" if nothing meaningful is provided
  flag_imp_setting <- "all"
}

# Map arbitrary flag_imputation values from file to admiral options when possible
flag_imp_setting <- tolower(flag_imp_setting)
if (!flag_imp_setting %in% c("all", "date", "time", "none")) {
  # If unexpected, fall back to "all" but warn
  warning("Unrecognized flag_imputation setting in flag_imputation.tsv; defaulting to 'all'.")
  flag_imp_setting <- "all"
}

#-----------------------------
# 3. Prepare dataset for derive_vars_dtm
#-----------------------------
# datase_raw contains ADaM-like data with ADT (date) but we need a --DTC character
# Create a clean ISO8601-like DTC variable from ADT
datase <- datase_raw %>%
  mutate(
    dtc = ADT,                       # copy existing date
    dtc = if_else(is.na(dtc) | dtc == "", NA_character_, dtc),
    dtc = gsub(" ", "", dtc)         # minimal cleanup
  )

# Ensure required var present
if (!"dtc" %in% names(datase)) {
  stop("Prepared datase object does not contain 'dtc' variable required by derive_vars_dtm.")
}

#-----------------------------
# 4. Derive datetime with imputation and flags
#   - date_imputation: "last"
#   - time_imputation: "last"
#   - highest_imputation: "M" (up to month)
#   - flag_imputation: from file (mapped above)
#-----------------------------
datase_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset          = datase,
    dtc              = dtc,
    new_vars_prefix  = new_prefix,
    date_imputation  = "last",
    time_imputation  = "last",
    highest_imputation = "M",
    flag_imputation  = flag_imp_setting
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for datase: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

#-----------------------------
# 5. Additional examples on mhdt_raw (mirroring user text)
#-----------------------------
# Example 1: default imputation (time to 00, no date imputation) with flags
mhdt1 <- mhdt_raw %>%
  rename(MHSTDTC = dt)

mhdt1_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset         = mhdt1,
    new_vars_prefix = new_prefix,
    dtc             = MHSTDTC
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for mhdt1: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# Example 2: date/time imputation = "last", highest_imputation = "M"
mhdt2_dtm <- tryCatch(

    time_imputation   = "last",

  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for mhdt2: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# Example 3: no flags

    dataset         = mhdt1,

  ),

# Example 4: preserve partials, mid/last, track seconds flag
mhdt4 <- tibble(

    "2019-07-18T15:25",
    "2019---18T15:-:05",
    "2019-07-18",
    "2019-02",
    "2019",
    "2019---07",
    ""
  )

mhdt4_dtm <- tryCatch(

    date_imputation   = "mid",
    time_imputation   = "last",
    preserve          = TRUE,
    ignore_seconds_flag = FALSE
  ),

#-----------------------------
# 6. Example with max_dates (mirroring AE example)
#-----------------------------
adae <- tribble(
  ~AEENDTC,   ~DTHDT,              ~DCUTDT,
  "2020-12",  ymd("2020-12-26"),   ymd("2020-12-24"),
  "2020-11",  ymd("2020-12-06"),   ymd("2020-12-24"),
  "",         ymd("2020-12-06"),   ymd("2020-12-24"),
  "2020-12-20", ymd("2020-12-06"), ymd("2020-12-24")
)

adae_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset           = adae,

    date_imputation   = "last",

  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for adae: ", conditionMessage(e))

#-----------------------------
# 7. Write outputs
#-----------------------------
write_result_safely <- function(obj, name) {
  csv_path <- file.path("outputs", paste0(name, ".csv"))
  rds_path <- file.path("outputs", paste0(name, ".rds"))
  
  tryCatch(
    {
      if (is.data.frame(obj)) {
        write_csv(obj, csv_path, na = "")
      }
      saveRDS(obj, rds_path)
      
      # basic sanity checks

library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)
library(tibble)
library(lubridate)

# Create output directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# 1. Read input data
#-----------------------------
datase_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "datase.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading datase.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

dtc_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "dtc.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading dtc.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

new_vars_prefix_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "new_vars_prefix.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading new_vars_prefix.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

flag_imputation_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "flag_imputation.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading flag_imputation.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

mhdt_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(
    file = file.path("inputs", "mhdt.tsv"),
    col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error reading mhdt.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

#-----------------------------
# 2. Basic validation
#-----------------------------
if (!"dtc" %in% names(dtc_raw)) {
  stop("dtc.tsv must contain a 'dtc' column.")
}

if (!"new_vars_prefix" %in% names(new_vars_prefix_raw)) {
  stop("new_vars_prefix.tsv must contain a 'new_vars_prefix' column.")
}

if (!"flag_imputation" %in% names(flag_imputation_raw)) {
  stop("flag_imputation.tsv must contain a 'flag_imputation' column.")
}

if (!"dt" %in% names(mhdt_raw)) {
  stop("mhdt.tsv must contain a 'dt' column.")
}

# use first value as prefix and flag handling option
new_prefix <- new_vars_prefix_raw$new_vars_prefix[1]
if (is.na(new_prefix) || new_prefix == "") {
  stop("new_vars_prefix must be a non-empty string.")
}

flag_imp_setting <- flag_imputation_raw$flag_imputation[1]
if (is.na(flag_imp_setting) || flag_imp_setting == "") {
  # default to "all" if nothing meaningful is provided
  flag_imp_setting <- "all"
}

# Map arbitrary flag_imputation values from file to admiral options when possible
flag_imp_setting <- tolower(flag_imp_setting)
if (!flag_imp_setting %in% c("all", "date", "time", "none")) {
  # If unexpected, fall back to "all" but warn
  warning("Unrecognized flag_imputation setting in flag_imputation.tsv; defaulting to 'all'.")
  flag_imp_setting <- "all"
}

#-----------------------------
# 3. Prepare dataset for derive_vars_dtm
#-----------------------------
# datase_raw contains ADaM-like data with ADT (date) but we need a --DTC character
# Create a clean ISO8601-like DTC variable from ADT
datase <- datase_raw %>%
  mutate(
    dtc = ADT,                       # copy existing date
    dtc = if_else(is.na(dtc) | dtc == "", NA_character_, dtc),
    dtc = gsub(" ", "", dtc)         # minimal cleanup
  )

# Ensure required var present
if (!"dtc" %in% names(datase)) {
  stop("Prepared datase object does not contain 'dtc' variable required by derive_vars_dtm.")
}

#-----------------------------
# 4. Derive datetime with imputation and flags
#   - date_imputation: "last"
#   - time_imputation: "last"
#   - highest_imputation: "M" (up to month)
#   - flag_imputation: from file (mapped above)
#-----------------------------
datase_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset          = datase,
    dtc              = dtc,
    new_vars_prefix  = new_prefix,
    date_imputation  = "last",
    time_imputation  = "last",
    highest_imputation = "M",
    flag_imputation  = flag_imp_setting
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for datase: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

#-----------------------------
# 5. Additional examples on mhdt_raw (mirroring user text)
#-----------------------------
# Example 1: default imputation (time to 00, no date imputation) with flags
mhdt1 <- mhdt_raw %>%
  rename(MHSTDTC = dt)

mhdt1_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset         = mhdt1,
    new_vars_prefix = new_prefix,
    dtc             = MHSTDTC
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for mhdt1: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# Example 2: date/time imputation = "last", highest_imputation = "M"
mhdt2_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset           = mhdt1,
    new_vars_prefix   = new_prefix,
    dtc               = MHSTDTC,
    date_imputation   = "last",
    time_imputation   = "last",
    highest_imputation = "M"
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for mhdt2: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# Example 3: no flags
mhdt3_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset         = mhdt1,
    new_vars_prefix = new_prefix,
    dtc             = MHSTDTC,
    flag_imputation = "none"
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for mhdt3: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

# Example 4: preserve partials, mid/last, track seconds flag
mhdt4 <- tibble(
  MHSTDTC = c(
    "2019-07-18T15:25",
    "2019---18T15:-:05",
    "2019-07-18",
    "2019-02",
    "2019",
    "2019---07",
    ""
  )
)

mhdt4_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset           = mhdt4,
    new_vars_prefix   = new_prefix,
    dtc               = MHSTDTC,
    highest_imputation = "M",
    date_imputation   = "mid",
    time_imputation   = "last",
    preserve          = TRUE,
    ignore_seconds_flag = FALSE
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for mhdt4: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

#-----------------------------
# 6. Example with max_dates (mirroring AE example)
#-----------------------------
adae <- tribble(
  ~AEENDTC,   ~DTHDT,              ~DCUTDT,
  "2020-12",  ymd("2020-12-26"),   ymd("2020-12-24"),
  "2020-11",  ymd("2020-12-06"),   ymd("2020-12-24"),
  "",         ymd("2020-12-06"),   ymd("2020-12-24"),
  "2020-12-20", ymd("2020-12-06"), ymd("2020-12-24")
)

adae_dtm <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_dtm(
    dataset           = adae,
    dtc               = AEENDTC,
    new_vars_prefix   = "AEN",
    highest_imputation = "Y",
    date_imputation   = "last",
    time_imputation   = "last",
    max_dates         = exprs(DTHDT, DCUTDT)
  ),
  error = function(e) stop("Error in derive_vars_dtm for adae: ", conditionMessage(e))
)

#-----------------------------
# 7. Write outputs
#-----------------------------
write_result_safely <- function(obj, name) {
