```r
library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)

#----------------------------
# Helper: safe file writing
#----------------------------
safe_write <- function(df, csv_path, rds_path) {
  tryCatch({
    write_csv(df, csv_path, na = "")
    saveRDS(df, rds_path)
    
    # Basic validation: re-read and compare column names and nrow
    df_csv <- suppressMessages(read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE))
    df_rds <- readRDS(rds_path)
    
    if (!identical(names(df), names(df_csv)) ||
        !identical(nrow(df), nrow(df_csv))) {
      stop("CSV round-trip validation failed: possible misalignment or parsing issue.")
    }
    if (!identical(names(df), names(df_rds)) ||
        !identical(nrow(df), nrow(df_rds))) {
      stop("RDS round-trip validation failed: possible misalignment or parsing issue.")
    }
    
    message("Files written and validated: ", csv_path, " ; ", rds_path)
  }, error = function(e) {
    stop("Error while writing or validating output files: ", conditionMessage(e))
  })
}

#----------------------------
# Read and validate inputs
#----------------------------
dataset_add_path      <- file.path("inputs", "dataset_add.tsv")
print_not_mapped_path <- file.path("inputs", "print_not_mapped.tsv")

if (!file.exists(dataset_add_path)) {
  stop("Input file not found: ", dataset_add_path)
}
if (!file.exists(print_not_mapped_path)) {
  stop("Input file not found: ", print_not_mapped_path)
}

dataset_add <- suppressMessages(
  read_tsv(dataset_add_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
)

print_not_mapped_tbl <- suppressMessages(
  read_tsv(print_not_mapped_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
)

if (!"print_not_mapped" %in% names(print_not_mapped_tbl)) {
  stop("The file print_not_mapped.tsv must contain a column named 'print_not_mapped'.")
}

print_not_mapped_vec <- unique(print_not_mapped_tbl$print_not_mapped)

if (!all(print_not_mapped_vec %in% c("TRUE", "FALSE"))) {
  stop("Values in 'print_not_mapped' column must be 'TRUE' or 'FALSE'.")
}

print_not_mapped_flag <- any(print_not_mapped_vec == "TRUE")

#----------------------------
# Derive source dataset and by_vars
#----------------------------
# Use dataset_add as the main/source dataset as requested
# Ensure required by_vars exist in dataset_add
required_by_vars <- c("USUBJID", "AVISITN", "PARAMCD")

missing_by_vars <- setdiff(required_by_vars, names(dataset_add))
if (length(missing_by_vars) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required by_vars in dataset_add: ", paste(missing_by_vars, collapse = ", "))
}

source_ds <- dataset_add %>%
  select(all_of(required_by_vars)) %>%
  distinct()

# Create lookup table with additional variables to be merged
lookup_vars <- setdiff(names(dataset_add), required_by_vars)

if (length(lookup_vars) == 0) {
  stop("No variables available in dataset_add to be used as lookup variables.")
}

lookup_ds <- dataset_add

#----------------------------
# Prepare new_vars mapping
#----------------------------
# Merge all non-by_vars from lookup
new_vars <- exprs(!!!syms(lookup_vars))

#----------------------------
# Perform lookup merge
#----------------------------
merged_ds <- derive_vars_merged_lookup(
  dataset      = source_ds,
  dataset_add  = lookup_ds,
  by_vars      = exprs(USUBJID, AVISITN, PARAMCD),
  new_vars     = new_vars,
  print_not_mapped = print_not_mapped_flag
)

#----------------------------
# Basic post-merge validation
#----------------------------
if (nrow(merged_ds) != nrow(source_ds)) {
  stop("Row count of merged dataset differs from source dataset: possible unintended duplication.")
}

# Check for unexpected NA inflation in key variables from lookup
key_check_vars <- intersect(c("AVAL", "AVISIT", "ADT"), names(merged_ds))
if (length(key_check_vars) > 0) {
  na_rates <- sapply(merged_ds[key_check_vars], function(x) mean(is.na(x)))
  if (any(na_rates > 0.5)) {
    warning(
      "High NA rate (>50%) detected in merged variables: ",
      paste(names(na_rates)[na_rates > 0.5], collapse = ", "),
      ". Check for data misalignment or parsing issues."
    )
  }
}

#----------------------------
# Write outputs
#----------------------------
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

safe_write(
  merged_ds,
  csv_path = file.path("outputs", "merged_lookup_dataset.csv"),
  rds_path = file.path("outputs", "merged_lookup_dataset.rds")
)
```

library(admiral)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(rlang)

#----------------------------
# Helper: safe file writing
#----------------------------
safe_write <- function(df, csv_path, rds_path) {
  tryCatch({
    write_csv(df, csv_path, na = "")
    saveRDS(df, rds_path)
    
    # Basic validation: re-read and compare column names and nrow
    df_csv <- suppressMessages(read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE))
    df_rds <- readRDS(rds_path)
    
    if (!identical(names(df), names(df_csv)) ||
        !identical(nrow(df), nrow(df_csv))) {
      stop("CSV round-trip validation failed: possible misalignment or parsing issue.")
    }
    if (!identical(names(df), names(df_rds)) ||
        !identical(nrow(df), nrow(df_rds))) {
      stop("RDS round-trip validation failed: possible misalignment or parsing issue.")
    }
    
    message("Files written and validated: ", csv_path, " ; ", rds_path)
  }, error = function(e) {
    stop("Error while writing or validating output files: ", conditionMessage(e))
  })
}

#----------------------------
# Read and validate inputs
#----------------------------
dataset_add_path      <- file.path("inputs", "dataset_add.tsv")
print_not_mapped_path <- file.path("inputs", "print_not_mapped.tsv")

if (!file.exists(dataset_add_path)) {
  stop("Input file not found: ", dataset_add_path)
}
if (!file.exists(print_not_mapped_path)) {
  stop("Input file not found: ", print_not_mapped_path)
}

dataset_add <- suppressMessages(
  read_tsv(dataset_add_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
)

print_not_mapped_tbl <- suppressMessages(
  read_tsv(print_not_mapped_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
)

if (!"print_not_mapped" %in% names(print_not_mapped_tbl)) {
  stop("The file print_not_mapped.tsv must contain a column named 'print_not_mapped'.")
}

print_not_mapped_vec <- unique(print_not_mapped_tbl$print_not_mapped)

if (!all(print_not_mapped_vec %in% c("TRUE", "FALSE"))) {
  stop("Values in 'print_not_mapped' column must be 'TRUE' or 'FALSE'.")
}

print_not_mapped_flag <- any(print_not_mapped_vec == "TRUE")

#----------------------------
# Derive source dataset and by_vars
#----------------------------
# Use dataset_add as the main/source dataset as requested
# Ensure required by_vars exist in dataset_add
required_by_vars <- c("USUBJID", "AVISITN", "PARAMCD")

missing_by_vars <- setdiff(required_by_vars, names(dataset_add))
if (length(missing_by_vars) > 0) {
  stop("Missing required by_vars in dataset_add: ", paste(missing_by_vars, collapse = ", "))
}

source_ds <- dataset_add %>%
  select(all_of(required_by_vars)) %>%
  distinct()

# Create lookup table with additional variables to be merged
lookup_vars <- setdiff(names(dataset_add), required_by_vars)

if (length(lookup_vars) == 0) {
  stop("No variables available in dataset_add to be used as lookup variables.")
}

lookup_ds <- dataset_add

#----------------------------
# Prepare new_vars mapping
#----------------------------
# Merge all non-by_vars from lookup
new_vars <- exprs(!!!syms(lookup_vars))

#----------------------------
# Perform lookup merge
#----------------------------
merged_ds <- derive_vars_merged_lookup(
  dataset      = source_ds,
  dataset_add  = lookup_ds,
  by_vars      = exprs(USUBJID, AVISITN, PARAMCD),
  new_vars     = new_vars,
  print_not_mapped = print_not_mapped_flag
)

#----------------------------
# Basic post-merge validation
#----------------------------
if (nrow(merged_ds) != nrow(source_ds)) {
  stop("Row count of merged dataset differs from source dataset: possible unintended duplication.")
}

# Check for unexpected NA inflation in key variables from lookup
key_check_vars <- intersect(c("AVAL", "AVISIT", "ADT"), names(merged_ds))
if (length(key_check_vars) > 0) {
  na_rates <- sapply(merged_ds[key_check_vars], function(x) mean(is.na(x)))
  if (any(na_rates > 0.5)) {
    warning(
      "High NA rate (>50%) detected in merged variables: ",
      paste(names(na_rates)[na_rates > 0.5], collapse = ", "),
      ". Check for data misalignment or parsing issues."
    )
  }
}

#----------------------------
# Write outputs
#----------------------------
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

safe_write(
  merged_ds,
  csv_path = file.path("outputs", "merged_lookup_dataset.csv"),
  rds_path = file.path("outputs", "merged_lookup_dataset.rds")
)

prompt:
Write R code to add variables from an additional dataset based on conditions from both using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/dataset_add.tsv, inputs/by_vars.tsv, inputs/order.tsv, inputs/new_vars.tsv, inputs/tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv, inputs/join_vars.tsv, inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv, inputs/first_cond_upper.tsv, inputs/filter_join.tsv, inputs/mode.tsv, inputs/check_type.tsv). Datasets The function adds variables from an additional dataset to the input dataset. The selection of the observations from the additional dataset can depend on variables from both datasets. For example, add the lowest value (nadir) before the current observation. `derive_vars_joined()` comes down to how you need to select the observations to be merged. - If the observations from `dataset_add` to merge can be selected by a condition (`filter_add`) using *only* variables from `dataset_add`, then always use `derive_vars_merged()` as it requires less resources (time and memory). A common example of this would be a randomization date in `ADSL`, where you are simply merging on a date from `DS` according to a certain `DSDECOD` condition such as `DSDECOD == "RANDOMIZATION"`. - However, if the selection of the observations from `dataset_add` can depend on variables from *both* datasets, then use `derive_vars_joined()`. An example of this would be assigning period variables from `ADSL` to an `ADAE`, where you now need to check each adverse event start date against the period start and end dates to decide which period value to join. scheduled set of time windows. - The `filter_join` argument here can check conditions using variables from both the `dataset` and `dataset_add`, so the study day is compared to the start and end of the time window. - As no grouping variables are assigned using the `by_vars` argument, a full join is performed keeping all variables from `dataset_add`. library(tibble) library(lubridate) library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE) library(tidyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE) adbds <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~ADY, ~AVAL, "1", -33, 11, "1", -7, 10, "1", 1, 12, "1", 8, 12, "1", 15, 9, "1", 20, 14, "1", 24, 12, "2", -1, 13, "2", 13, 8 ) %>% mutate(STUDYID = "AB42") windows <- tribble( ~AVISIT, ~AWLO, ~AWHI, "BASELINE", -30, 1, "WEEK 1", 2, 7, "WEEK 2", 8, 15, "WEEK 3", 16, 22, "WEEK 4", 23, 30 ) derive_vars_joined( adbds, dataset_add = windows, join_type = "all", filter_join = AWLO <= ADY & ADY <= AWHI ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AWLO, AWHI, AVISIT) `order` and `mode`) occurring before) by subject. - Note how `dataset` and `dataset_add` are the same here, so we are joining a dataset with itself. This enables us to compare records within the dataset to each other. - Now we use `by_vars` as we only want to perform the join by subject. - To find the lowest value we use the `order` and `mode` arguments. - We subsequently need to check `ADY` to only check assessments occurring before. As this is not included in `by_vars` or `order`, we have to ensure it also gets joined by adding to `join_vars`. Then in `filter_join` note how `ADY.join < ADY` is used as the same variable exists in both datasets, so the version from `dataset_add` has `.join` added. - According to the `AVAL` sort order used there could be duplicates (e.g. see subject `"1"` records at day 1 and 8), but given we only need to join `AVAL` itself here it doesn't actually matter to us which exact record is taken. So, in this example, we silence the uniqueness check by using `check_type = "none"`. derive_vars_joined( adbds, dataset_add = adbds, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(AVAL), new_vars = exprs(NADIR = AVAL), join_vars = exprs(ADY), join_type = "all", filter_join = ADY.join < ADY, mode = "first", check_type = "none" ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AVAL, NADIR) this allowing only post-baseline values to be possible for the nadir. - The `filter_add` argument can be used here as we only need to restrict the source data from `dataset_add`. derive_vars_joined( adbds, dataset_add = adbds, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(AVAL), new_vars = exprs(NADIR = AVAL), join_vars = exprs(ADY), join_type = "all", filter_add = ADY > 0, filter_join = ADY.join < ADY, mode = "first", check_type = "none" ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AVAL, NADIR) example to add to `ADAE` the highest hemoglobin value occurring within two weeks before each adverse event. Also join the day it occurred, taking the earliest occurrence if more than one assessment with the same value. - Note how we used `mode = "last"` to get the highest lab value, but then as we wanted the earliest occurrence if more than one it means we need to add `desc(ADY)` to `order`. i.e. the last day when in descending order is the first. adae <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~ASTDY, "1", 3, "1", 22, "2", 2 ) %>% mutate(STUDYID = "AB42") adlb <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~PARAMCD, ~ADY, ~AVAL, "1", "HGB", 1, 8.5, "1", "HGB", 3, 7.9, "1", "HGB", 5, 8.9, "1", "HGB", 8, 8.0, "1", "HGB", 9, 8.0, "1", "HGB", 16, 7.4, "1", "ALB", 1, 42, ) %>% mutate(STUDYID = "AB42") derive_vars_joined( adae, dataset_add = adlb, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(AVAL, desc(ADY)), new_vars = exprs(HGB_MAX = AVAL, HGB_DY = ADY), join_type = "all", filter_add = PARAMCD == "HGB", filter_join = ASTDY - 14 <= ADY & ADY <= ASTDY, mode = "last" ) %>% select(USUBJID, ASTDY, HGB_MAX, HGB_DY) 1 day. If the dose occurs on the same day as the AE then include it as the last dose. - In the `new_vars` argument, other functions can be utilized to modify the joined values using variables from both `dataset` and `dataset_add`. For example, in the below case we want to calculate the number of days between the AE and the last dose using `compute_duration()`. This function includes the plus 1 day as default. - Also note how in this example `EXSDT` is created via the `order` argument and then used for `new_vars`, `filter_add` and `filter_join`. - The reason to use `join_type = "all"` here instead of `"before"` is that we want to include any dose occurring on the same day as the AE, hence the `filter_join = EXSDT <= ASTDT`. Whereas using `join_type = "before"` would have resulted in the condition `EXSDT < ASTDT`. See the next example instead for `join_type = "before"`. adae <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~ASTDT, "1", "2020-02-02", "1", "2020-02-04", "2", "2021-01-08" ) %>% mutate( ASTDT = ymd(ASTDT), STUDYID = "AB42" ) ex <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~EXSDTC, "1", "2020-01-10", "1", "2020-01", "1", "2020-01-20", "1", "2020-02-03", "2", "2021-01-05" ) %>% mutate(STUDYID = "AB42") derive_vars_joined( adae, dataset_add = ex, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(EXSDT = convert_dtc_to_dt(EXSDTC)), join_type = "all", new_vars = exprs(LDRELD = compute_duration( start_date = EXSDT, end_date = ASTDT )), filter_add = !is.na(EXSDT), filter_join = EXSDT <= ASTDT, mode = "last" ) %>% select(USUBJID, ASTDT, LDRELD) or `"++"`, for any value of `"0"` derive the last occurring `"++"` day that occurs before the `"0"`. - The `AVAL.join == "++"` in `filter_join`, along with `order` and `mode` taking the last day, identifies the target records to join from `dataset_add` for each observation of `dataset`. - Then `join_type = "before"` is now used instead of `join_type = "all"`. This is because we only want to join the records occurring before the current observation in `dataset`. Including `AVAL == "0"` in `filter_join` ensures here that we only populate the new variable for records with `AVAL == "0"` in our `dataset`. myd <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~ADY, ~AVAL, "1", 1, "++", "1", 2, "-", "1", 3, "0", "1", 4, "+", "1", 5, "++", "1", 6, "-", "2", 1, "-", "2", 2, "++", "2", 3, "+", "2", 4, "0", "2", 5, "-", "2", 6, "++", "2", 7, "0" ) %>% mutate(STUDYID = "AB42") derive_vars_joined( myd, dataset_add = myd, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(ADY), mode = "last", new_vars = exprs(PREVPLDY = ADY), join_vars = exprs(AVAL), join_type = "before", filter_join = AVAL == "0" & AVAL.join == "++" ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AVAL, PREVPLDY) between (`first_cond_lower`, `join_type` and `filter_join`) `"++"` and the `"0"` all results must be either `"+"` or `"++"`. - Firstly, `first_cond_lower = AVAL.join == "++"` is used so that for each observation of `dataset` the joined records from `dataset_add` are restricted to only include from the last occurring `"++"` before. This is necessary because of the use of a summary function in `filter_join` only on a subset of the joined observations as explained below. - The `filter_join` condition used here now includes `all(AVAL.join %in% c("+", "++"))` to further restrict the joined records from `dataset_add` to only where all the values are either `"+"` or `"++"`. - The `order` and `mode` arguments ensure only the day of the `"++"` value is joined. For example, for subject `"2"` it selects the day 2 record instead of day 3, by using `"first"`. derive_vars_joined( myd, dataset_add = myd, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(ADY), mode = "first", new_vars = exprs(PREVPLDY = ADY), join_vars = exprs(AVAL), join_type = "before", first_cond_lower = AVAL.join == "++", filter_join = AVAL == "0" & all(AVAL.join %in% c("+", "++")) ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AVAL, PREVPLDY) (`first_cond_upper`, `join_type` and `filter_join`) where all results in between are either `"+"` or `"++"`. - Note how the main difference here is the use of `join_type = "after"`, `mode = "last"` and the `first_cond_upper` argument, instead of `first_cond_lower`. derive_vars_joined( myd, dataset_add = myd, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(ADY), mode = "last", new_vars = exprs(NEXTPLDY = ADY), join_vars = exprs(AVAL), join_type = "after", first_cond_upper = AVAL.join == "++", filter_join = AVAL == "0" & all(AVAL.join %in% c("+", "++")) ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AVAL, NEXTPLDY) - The `join_type = "after"` here essentially acts as a lag to join variables from the next occurring record, and `mode = "first"` selects the first of these. derive_vars_joined( myd, dataset_add = myd, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(ADY), mode = "first", new_vars = exprs(NEXTVAL = AVAL), join_vars = exprs(AVAL), join_type = "after" ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AVAL, NEXTVAL) (`tmp_obs_nr_var`, `join_type` and `filter_join`) - The `tmp_obs_nr_var` argument can be useful as shown here to help pick out records happening before or after with respect to `order`, as you can see in the `filter_join`. derive_vars_joined( myd, dataset_add = myd, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), order = exprs(ADY), mode = "last", new_vars = exprs(NEXTVAL = AVAL), tmp_obs_nr_var = tmp_obs_nr, join_vars = exprs(AVAL), join_type = "after", filter_join = tmp_obs_nr + 3 >= tmp_obs_nr.join ) %>% select(USUBJID, ADY, AVAL, NEXTVAL) to identify within which period each AE occurred. adsl <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~AP01SDT, ~AP01EDT, ~AP02SDT, ~AP02EDT, "1", "2021-01-04", "2021-02-06", "2021-02-07", "2021-03-07", "2", "2021-02-02", "2021-03-02", "2021-03-03", "2021-04-01" ) %>% mutate(across(ends_with("DT"), ymd)) %>% mutate(STUDYID = "AB42") period_ref <- create_period_dataset( adsl, new_vars = exprs(APERSDT = APxxSDT, APEREDT = APxxEDT) ) period_ref adae <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~ASTDT, "1", "2021-01-01", "1", "2021-01-05", "1", "2021-02-05", "1", "2021-03-05", "1", "2021-04-05", "2", "2021-02-15", ) %>% mutate( ASTDT = ymd(ASTDT), STUDYID = "AB42" ) derive_vars_joined( adae, dataset_add = period_ref, by_vars = exprs(STUDYID, USUBJID), join_vars = exprs(APERSDT, APEREDT), join_type = "all", filter_join = APERSDT <= ASTDT & ASTDT <= APEREDT ) %>% select(USUBJID, ASTDT, APERSDT, APEREDT, APERIOD) `vignette("generic")`. The variables specified by `order` are added to the additional dataset (`dataset_add`). 1. The variables specified by `join_vars` are added to the additional dataset (`dataset_add`). 1. The records from the additional dataset (`dataset_add`) are restricted to those matching the `filter_add` condition. 1. The input dataset and the (restricted) additional dataset are left joined by the grouping variables (`by_vars`). If no grouping variables are specified, a full join is performed. 1. If `first_cond_lower` is specified, for each observation of the input dataset the joined dataset is restricted to observations from the first observation where `first_cond_lower` is fulfilled (the observation fulfilling the condition is included) up to the observation of the input dataset. If for an observation of the input dataset the condition is not fulfilled, the observation is removed. If `first_cond_upper` is specified, for each observation of the input dataset the joined dataset is restricted to observations up to the first observation where `first_cond_upper` is fulfilled (the observation fulfilling the condition is included). If for an observation of the input dataset the condition is not fulfilled, the observation is removed. For an example, see the "Examples" section below. 1. The joined dataset is restricted by the `filter_join` condition. 1. If `order` is specified, for each observation of the input dataset the first or last observation (depending on `mode`) is selected. 1. The variables specified for `new_vars` are created (if requested) and merged to the input dataset. I.e., the output dataset contains all observations from the input dataset. For observations without a matching observation in the joined dataset the new variables are set as specified by `missing_values` (or to `NA` for variables not in `missing_values`).
```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
library(readr)
library(rlang)

# Helper to stop with message
stop_if_not <- function(expr, msg) {
  if (!rlang::is_true(expr)) stop(msg, call. = FALSE)
}

#-----------------------------
# Read input data
#-----------------------------
datase <- read_tsv(
  "inputs/datase.tsv",
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
) %>%
  mutate(
    AVAL = as.numeric(AVAL),
    AVISITN = as.numeric(AVISITN),
    ADT = as.Date(ADT)
  )

dataset_add <- read_tsv(
  "inputs/dataset_add.tsv",
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
) %>%
  mutate(
    AVAL = as.numeric(AVAL),
    AVISITN = as.numeric(AVISITN),
    ADT = as.Date(ADT)
  )

by_vars_df <- read_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv", col_types = cols())
by_vars_chr <- by_vars_df$by_vars[!is.na(by_vars_df$by_vars)]
stop_if_not(
  length(by_vars_chr) > 0,
  "by_vars.tsv must contain at least one variable name in column 'by_vars'."
)

order_df <- read_tsv("inputs/order.tsv", col_types = cols())
order_chr <- order_df$order[!is.na(order_df$order)]
stop_if_not(
  length(order_chr) == 1,
  "order.tsv must contain a single expression string in column 'order'."
)
order_exprs <- parse_exprs(order_chr)

new_vars_df <- read_tsv("inputs/new_vars.tsv", col_types = cols())
new_vars_chr <- new_vars_df$by_vars[!is.na(new_vars_df$by_vars)]
stop_if_not(
  length(new_vars_chr) > 0,
  "new_vars.tsv must contain variable names in column 'by_vars'."
)
# Use same names from dataset_add
new_vars_exprs <- exprs(!!!set_names(syms(new_vars_chr), new_vars_chr))

tmp_obs_df <- read_tsv("inputs/tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv", col_types = cols())
tmp_obs_chr <- tmp_obs_df$tmp_obs_nr_var[!is.na(tmp_obs_df$tmp_obs_nr_var)]
stop_if_not(
  length(tmp_obs_chr) == 1,
  "tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv must contain exactly one variable name."
)
tmp_obs_sym <- sym(tmp_obs_chr)

join_vars_df <- read_tsv("inputs/join_vars.tsv", col_types = cols())
join_vars_chr <- join_vars_df$join_vars[!is.na(join_vars_df$join_vars)]
stop_if_not(
  length(join_vars_chr) > 0,
  "join_vars.tsv must contain at least one variable name in column 'join_vars'."
)
join_vars_exprs <- exprs(!!!syms(join_vars_chr))

first_lower_df <- read_tsv("inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv", col_types = cols())
first_lower_chr <- first_lower_df$cond[!is.na(first_lower_df$cond)]
stop_if_not(
  length(first_lower_chr) == 1,
  "first_cond_lower.tsv must contain a single condition in column 'cond'."
)
first_cond_lower_expr <- parse_expr(first_lower_chr)

first_upper_df <- read_tsv("inputs/first_cond_upper.tsv", col_types = cols())
first_upper_chr <- first_upper_df$cond[!is.na(first_upper_df$cond)]
stop_if_not(
  length(first_upper_chr) == 1,
  "first_cond_upper.tsv must contain a single condition in column 'cond'."
)
first_cond_upper_expr <- parse_expr(first_upper_chr)

filter_join_df <- read_tsv("inputs/filter_join.tsv", col_types = cols())
filter_join_chr <- filter_join_df$filter[!is.na(filter_join_df$filter)]
stop_if_not(
  length(filter_join_chr) == 1,
  "filter_join.tsv must contain a single condition in column 'filter'."
)
filter_join_expr <- parse_expr(filter_join_chr)

mode_df <- read_tsv("inputs/mode.tsv", col_types = cols())
mode_chr <- mode_df$mode[!is.na(mode_df$mode)]
stop_if_not(
  length(mode_chr) == 1,
  "mode.tsv must contain a single value in column 'mode'."
)
mode_val <- mode_chr[[1]]
stop_if_not(
  mode_val %in% c("first", "last"),
  "mode must be either 'first' or 'last'."
)

check_type_df <- read_tsv("inputs/check_type.tsv", col_types = cols())
check_type_chr <- check_type_df$check_type[!is.na(check_type_df$check_type)]
stop_if_not(
  length(check_type_chr) == 1,
  "check_type.tsv must contain a single value in column 'check_type'."
)
check_type_val <- check_type_chr[[1]]
stop_if_not(
  check_type_val %in% c("none", "message", "warning", "error"),
  "check_type must be one of 'none', 'message', 'warning', 'error'."
)

# Validate that by_vars, new_vars, join_vars exist
stop_if_not(
  all(by_vars_chr %in% names(datase)),
  "Some by_vars are not present in datase."
)
stop_if_not(
  all(by_vars_chr %in% names(dataset_add)),
  "Some by_vars are not present in dataset_add."
)
stop_if_not(
  all(new_vars_chr %in% names(dataset_add)),
  "Some new_vars are not present in dataset_add."
)
stop_if_not(
  all(join_vars_chr %in% names(dataset_add)),
  "Some join_vars are not present in dataset_add."
)

#-----------------------------
# Derive joined variables
#-----------------------------
result <- derive_vars_joined(
  dataset = datase,
  dataset_add = dataset_add,
  by_vars = exprs(!!!syms(by_vars_chr)),
  order = order_exprs,
  new_vars = new_vars_exprs,
  tmp_obs_nr_var = !!tmp_obs_sym,
  join_vars = join_vars_exprs,
  first_cond_lower = !!first_cond_lower_expr,
  first_cond_upper = !!first_cond_upper_expr,
  filter_join = !!filter_join_expr,
  join_type = "all",
  mode = mode_val,
  check_type = check_type_val
)

#-----------------------------
# Write outputs
#-----------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

write_csv(result, "outputs/derive_vars_joined_result.csv")
saveRDS(result, "outputs/derive_vars_joined_result.rds")

#-----------------------------
# Basic validation
#-----------------------------
re_read_csv <- read_csv(
  "outputs/derive_vars_joined_result.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

re_read_rds <- readRDS("outputs/derive_vars_joined_result.rds")

stop_if_not(
  nrow(re_read_csv) == nrow(result),
  "Row count mismatch between in-memory result and CSV output."
)
stop_if_not(
  nrow(re_read_rds) == nrow(result),
  "Row count mismatch between in-memory result and RDS output."
)

# Check for obvious misalignment: by_vars should match between original and result
key_cols <- intersect(by_vars_chr, names(result))
stop_if_not(
  all(datase[order(datase[[key_cols[1]]]), key_cols] ==
        result[order(result[[key_cols[1]]]), key_cols]),
  "Key variables appear misaligned between input and output."
)
```

# Helper to stop with message
stop_if_not <- function(expr, msg) {

#-----------------------------

by_vars_df <- read_tsv("inputs/by_vars.tsv", col_types = cols())

  length(order_chr) == 1,

# Use same names from dataset_add

tmp_obs_df <- read_tsv("inputs/tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv", col_types = cols())

tmp_obs_sym <- sym(tmp_obs_chr)

join_vars_df <- read_tsv("inputs/join_vars.tsv", col_types = cols())

first_lower_df <- read_tsv("inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv", col_types = cols())

filter_join_df <- read_tsv("inputs/filter_join.tsv", col_types = cols())

mode_df <- read_tsv("inputs/mode.tsv", col_types = cols())

#-----------------------------

# Check for obvious misalignment: by_vars should match between original and result
key_cols <- intersect(by_vars_chr, names(result))

prompt:
Write R code to add the worst or best observation for each by group as new variables using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/events.tsv, inputs/tmp_event_nr_var.tsv, inputs/order.tsv, inputs/mode.tsv, inputs/source_datasets.tsv, inputs/new_vars.tsv). Add the first available record from `events` for each by group as new variables, all variables of the selected observation are kept. It can be used for selecting the extreme observation from a series of user-defined events. `exprs(ADT, desc(AVAL))` Additional details: 1. For each event select the observations to consider: 1. If the event is of class `event`, the observations of the source dataset are restricted by `condition` and then the first or last (`mode`) observation per by group (`by_vars`) is selected. If the event is of class `event_joined`, `filter_joined()` is called to select the observations. 1. The variables specified by the `set_values_to` field of the event are added to the selected observations. 1. The variable specified for `tmp_event_nr_var` is added and set to the number of the event. 1. All selected observations are bound together. 1. For each group (with respect to the variables specified for the `by_vars` parameter) the first or last observation (with respect to the order specified for the `order` parameter and the mode specified for the `mode` parameter) is selected. 1. The variables specified by the `new_vars` parameter are added to the selected observations. 1. The variables are added to input dataset. Use admiral's derive_vars_extreme_event function with the following parameters: events (Conditions and new values defining events A list of `event()` or `event_joined()` objects is expected.), tmp_event_nr_var (Temporary event number variable The specified variable is added to all source datasets and is set to the number of the e.), order (Sort order If a particular event from `events` has more than one observation, within the event and by group, the records.), mode (Selection mode (first or last) If a particular event from `events` has more than one observation, `"first"`/`"last"` is .), source_datasets (Source datasets A named list of datasets is expected.), new_vars (Variables to add The specified variables from the events are added to the output dataset.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with the best or worst observation of each by group added as new variables.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects). Before finishing, confirm that the output files were written correctly and check for any unexpected patterns or errors that might indicate data misalignment or parsing issues. Ensure proper error handling and data validation throughout the code.

```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(rlang)

#--- Helper: safe read with basic checks ---------------------------------------

safe_read_tsv <- function(path, ...) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop("File not found: ", path)
  }
  df <- tryCatch(
    readr::read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE, progress = FALSE, ...),
    error = function(e) stop("Error reading ", path, ": ", e$message)
  )
  if (!is.data.frame(df) || nrow(df) == 0L) {
    stop("File has no rows or is not a data frame: ", path)
  }
  df
}

#--- Read inputs ---------------------------------------------------------------

events_raw          <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/events.tsv")
mode_raw            <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/mode.tsv")
new_vars_raw        <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/new_vars.tsv")
order_raw           <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/order.tsv")
source_datasets_raw <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/source_datasets.tsv")
tmp_event_nr_raw    <- safe_read_tsv("inputs/tmp_event_nr_var.tsv")

#--- Validate and process parameter tables -------------------------------------

## events: variable names defining the event(s)
event_vars <- events_raw$events
if (length(event_vars) == 0L) stop("No event variables specified in events.tsv")

## mode: single value "first" or "last"
sel_mode <- tolower(mode_raw$mode[1])
if (!sel_mode %in% c("first", "last")) {
  stop("Invalid mode in mode.tsv: ", sel_mode, " (expected 'first' or 'last')")
}

## new_vars: variables to add back to input dataset
# Here we treat all rows under column 'by_vars' as variable names for new_vars
new_vars_vec <- new_vars_raw$by_vars
new_vars_vec <- new_vars_vec[!is.na(new_vars_vec) & nzchar(new_vars_vec)]
if (length(new_vars_vec) == 0L) stop("No new_vars specified in new_vars.tsv")

## order: comma-separated list
order_str <- order_raw$order[1]
if (is.na(order_str) || !nzchar(order_str)) {
  stop("Order specification in order.tsv is empty")
}
order_vars <- strsplit(order_str, ",")[[1]]
order_vars <- trimws(order_vars)
order_vars <- order_vars[order_vars != ""]
if (length(order_vars) == 0L) stop("No order variables parsed from order.tsv")

## tmp_event_nr_var: pick a single variable name for temp event number
tmp_event_candidates <- tmp_event_nr_raw$tmp_event_nr_var
tmp_event_candidates <- tmp_event_candidates[!is.na(tmp_event_candidates) & nzchar(tmp_event_candidates)]
if (length(tmp_event_candidates) == 0L) {
  stop("No tmp_event_nr_var specified in tmp_event_nr_var.tsv")
}
# Use the first provided name, but ensure it does not clash with existing vars
tmp_event_name <- tmp_event_candidates[1]
if (tmp_event_name %in% names(source_datasets_raw)) {
  tmp_event_name <- paste0(tmp_event_name, "_EVNTNR")
}
tmp_event_sym <- sym(tmp_event_name)

#--- Prepare source datasets ---------------------------------------------------

# Convert ADT to Date if it's character-like
if ("ADT" %in% names(source_datasets_raw) && !inherits(source_datasets_raw$ADT, "Date")) {
  source_datasets_raw <- source_datasets_raw %>%
    mutate(
      ADT = suppressWarnings(as.Date(ADT))
    )
}

# Basic validation of key variables
required_vars <- c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD", "AVAL", "AVISITN", "ADT")
missing_req <- setdiff(required_vars, names(source_datasets_raw))
if (length(missing_req) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing required variables in source_datasets.tsv: ",
       paste(missing_req, collapse = ", "))
}

# For admiral::derive_vars_extreme_event, source_datasets must be a named list
source_datasets <- list(
  events = source_datasets_raw
)

#--- Build events list ---------------------------------------------------------
# We want: "Add the first available record from `events` for each by group as new variables,
# all variables of the selected observation are kept."
# Here we implement that by a single event() with no additional filter (keep all),
# then derive_vars_extreme_event will take the first per group defined via by_vars.

# Determine by_vars for the extreme selection: use all new_vars plus USUBJID and PARAMCD
# From the description, new_vars are the variables to add; by_vars for the final
# selection are implied by the structure of the input – we set them here explicitly.
by_vars_syms <- syms(intersect(c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD"), names(source_datasets_raw)))
if (length(by_vars_syms) == 0L) {
  stop("Could not determine by_vars (USUBJID and/or PARAMCD not in source dataset)")
}

# Build the event object:
# - dataset_name: "events" (our only dataset in source_datasets list)
# - condition: NULL (use all rows)
# - set_values_to: empty list (no extra constants)
ev <- event(
  dataset_name  = "events",
  condition     = NULL,
  set_values_to = exprs()
)

events_list <- list(ev)

#--- Build new_vars expression -------------------------------------------------

# new_vars: Variables to add from the selected extreme record.
# We accept only variables that truly exist in the source dataset.
new_vars_in_data <- intersect(new_vars_vec, names(source_datasets_raw))
if (length(new_vars_in_data) == 0L) {
  stop("None of the new_vars from new_vars.tsv exist in source_datasets.tsv: ",
       paste(unique(new_vars_vec), collapse = ", "))
}
new_vars_expr <- rlang::syms(new_vars_in_data) %>%
  set_names(new_vars_in_data) %>%
  rlang::exprs(!!!.)

#--- Build order expression ----------------------------------------------------

missing_order <- setdiff(order_vars, names(source_datasets_raw))
if (length(missing_order) > 0L) {
  stop("Order variables not found in source_datasets.tsv: ",
       paste(missing_order, collapse = ", "))
}

order_expr <- rlang::exprs(!!!rlang::syms(order_vars))

#--- Derive extremes using admiral ---------------------------------------------

res <- tryCatch(
  {
    derive_vars_extreme_event(
      dataset        = source_datasets_raw,
      by_vars        = exprs(USUBJID, PARAMCD),
      events         = events_list,
      tmp_event_nr_var = !!tmp_event_sym,
      order          = order_expr,
      mode           = sel_mode,
      source_datasets = source_datasets,
      new_vars       = new_vars_expr
    )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_vars_extreme_event: ", e$message)
  }
)

#--- Validate result -----------------------------------------------------------

if (!is.data.frame(res)) {
  stop("Result of derive_vars_extreme_event is not a data frame")
}
if (nrow(res) == 0L) {
  stop("Result of derive_vars_extreme_event has zero rows")
}

# Simple alignment / parsing checks
# 1) Check that each USUBJID-PARAMCD still has same number of rows as original
orig_counts <- source_datasets_raw %>%
  count(USUBJID, PARAMCD, name = "n_orig")
res_counts <- res %>%
  count(USUBJID, PARAMCD, name = "n_res")

count_check <- orig_counts %>%
  left_join(res_counts, by = c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD")) %>%
  mutate(n_res = if_else(is.na(n_res), 0L, n_res))

if (any(count_check$n_orig != count_check$n_res)) {
  warning("Row count per USUBJID/PARAMCD differs between input and result; possible misalignment.")
}

# 2) Check for duplicate key rows that should be unique
dup_keys <- res %>%
  count(USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVISITN, name = "n") %>%
  filter(n > 1L)

if (nrow(dup_keys) > 0L) {
  warning("Found duplicated USUBJID/PARAMCD/AVISITN combinations in result; please inspect.")
}

#--- Write outputs -------------------------------------------------------------

# CSV
out_csv <- "outputs/derived_extreme_events.csv"
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
write_csv(res, out_csv, na = "")

# RDS
out_rds <- "outputs/derived_extreme_events.rds"
saveRDS(res, out_rds)

# Confirm files written
if (!file.exists(out_csv)) stop("CSV output was not written: ", out_csv)
if (!file.exists(out_rds)) stop("RDS output was not written: ", out_rds)

# Basic final sanity check: inspect head and summary for unexpected patterns
print(utils::head(res, 10))
print(summary(res))
```

library(admiral)
library(dplyr)

#--- Helper: safe read with basic checks ---------------------------------------

safe_read_tsv <- function(path, ...) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {

    stop("File has no rows or is not a data frame: ", path)
  }
  df
}

#--- Read inputs ---------------------------------------------------------------

#--- Validate and process parameter tables -------------------------------------

## events: variable names defining the event(s)

if (length(event_vars) == 0L) stop("No event variables specified in events.tsv")

## mode: single value "first" or "last"

}

## new_vars: variables to add back to input dataset

new_vars_vec <- new_vars_vec[!is.na(new_vars_vec) & nzchar(new_vars_vec)]
if (length(new_vars_vec) == 0L) stop("No new_vars specified in new_vars.tsv")

## order: comma-separated list

if (is.na(order_str) || !nzchar(order_str)) {
  stop("Order specification in order.tsv is empty")
}
order_vars <- strsplit(order_str, ",")[[1]]
order_vars <- trimws(order_vars)
order_vars <- order_vars[order_vars != ""]
if (length(order_vars) == 0L) stop("No order variables parsed from order.tsv")

## tmp_event_nr_var: pick a single variable name for temp event number
tmp_event_candidates <- tmp_event_nr_raw$tmp_event_nr_var
tmp_event_candidates <- tmp_event_candidates[!is.na(tmp_event_candidates) & nzchar(tmp_event_candidates)]
if (length(tmp_event_candidates) == 0L) {
  stop("No tmp_event_nr_var specified in tmp_event_nr_var.tsv")
}
# Use the first provided name, but ensure it does not clash with existing vars

if (tmp_event_name %in% names(source_datasets_raw)) {
  tmp_event_name <- paste0(tmp_event_name, "_EVNTNR")
}
tmp_event_sym <- sym(tmp_event_name)

#--- Prepare source datasets ---------------------------------------------------

# Convert ADT to Date if it's character-like
if ("ADT" %in% names(source_datasets_raw) && !inherits(source_datasets_raw$ADT, "Date")) {
  source_datasets_raw <- source_datasets_raw %>%
    mutate(
      ADT = suppressWarnings(as.Date(ADT))
    )
}

# Basic validation of key variables
required_vars <- c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD", "AVAL", "AVISITN", "ADT")
missing_req <- setdiff(required_vars, names(source_datasets_raw))
if (length(missing_req) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing required variables in source_datasets.tsv: ",
       paste(missing_req, collapse = ", "))
}

# For admiral::derive_vars_extreme_event, source_datasets must be a named list
source_datasets <- list(
  events = source_datasets_raw
)

#--- Build events list ---------------------------------------------------------
# We want: "Add the first available record from `events` for each by group as new variables,

# Here we implement that by a single event() with no additional filter (keep all),

# Determine by_vars for the extreme selection: use all new_vars plus USUBJID and PARAMCD
# From the description, new_vars are the variables to add; by_vars for the final
# selection are implied by the structure of the input – we set them here explicitly.
by_vars_syms <- syms(intersect(c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD"), names(source_datasets_raw)))
if (length(by_vars_syms) == 0L) {
  stop("Could not determine by_vars (USUBJID and/or PARAMCD not in source dataset)")
}

# Build the event object:
# - dataset_name: "events" (our only dataset in source_datasets list)
# - condition: NULL (use all rows)
# - set_values_to: empty list (no extra constants)
ev <- event(
  dataset_name  = "events",
  condition     = NULL,
  set_values_to = exprs()
)

events_list <- list(ev)

#--- Build new_vars expression -------------------------------------------------

# new_vars: Variables to add from the selected extreme record.

new_vars_in_data <- intersect(new_vars_vec, names(source_datasets_raw))
if (length(new_vars_in_data) == 0L) {

#--- Derive extremes using admiral ---------------------------------------------

)

#--- Validate result -----------------------------------------------------------

}

}

# Simple alignment / parsing checks

orig_counts <- source_datasets_raw %>%

res_counts <- res %>%
  count(USUBJID, PARAMCD, name = "n_res")

count_check <- orig_counts %>%
  left_join(res_counts, by = c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD")) %>%
  mutate(n_res = if_else(is.na(n_res), 0L, n_res))

if (any(count_check$n_orig != count_check$n_res)) {
  warning("Row count per USUBJID/PARAMCD differs between input and result; possible misalignment.")
}

# 2) Check for duplicate key rows that should be unique

  count(USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVISITN, name = "n") %>%
  filter(n > 1L)

if (nrow(dup_keys) > 0L) {
  warning("Found duplicated USUBJID/PARAMCD/AVISITN combinations in result; please inspect.")
}

#--- Write outputs -------------------------------------------------------------

# CSV
out_csv <- "outputs/derived_extreme_events.csv"
dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

prompt:
Write R code to summarize variables from an additional dataset based on conditions from both using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/datase.tsv, inputs/dataset_add.tsv, inputs/by_vars.tsv, inputs/order.tsv, inputs/new_vars.tsv, inputs/tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv, inputs/join_vars.tsv, inputs/join_type.tsv, inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv, inputs/first_cond_upper.tsv, inputs/filter_join.tsv, inputs/check_type.tsv). Datasets The function summarizes variables from an additional dataset and adds the summarized values as new variables to the input dataset. The selection of the observations from the additional dataset can depend on variables from both datasets. For example, all doses before the current observation can be selected and the sum be added to the input dataset. examples of functionality common to all "joined" functions like `filter_join`, `filter_add`, `join_vars`, ... please see the examples of [derive_vars_joined()]. in the `ADAE` dataset. - `USUBJID` is specified for `by_vars` to join the `ADAE` and the `ADEX` dataset by subject. - `filter_join` is specified to restrict the `ADEX` dataset to the days up to the adverse event. `ADY.join` refers to the study day in `ADEX`. - The new variable `CUMDOSA` is defined by the `new_vars` argument. It is set to the sum of `AVAL`. - As `ADY` from `ADEX` is used in `filter_join` (but not in `new_vars`), it needs to be specified for `join_vars`. - The `join_type` is set to `"all"` to consider all records in the joined dataset. `join_type = "before"` can't by used here because then doses at the same day as the adverse event would be excluded. library(tibble) library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE) adex <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~ADY, ~AVAL, "1", 1, 10, "1", 8, 20, "1", 15, 10, "2", 8, 5 ) adae <- tribble( ~USUBJID, ~ADY, ~AEDECOD, "1", 2, "Fatigue", "1", 9, "Influenza", "1", 15, "Theft", "1", 15, "Fatigue", "2", 4, "Parasomnia", "3", 2, "Truancy" ) derive_vars_joined_summary( dataset = adae, dataset_add = adex, by_vars = exprs(USUBJID), filter_join = ADY.join <= ADY, join_type = "all", join_vars = exprs(ADY), new_vars = exprs(CUMDOSA = sum(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE)) ) matching records in the restricted additional dataset. This can be changed by specifying the `missing_values` argument. derive_vars_joined_summary( dataset = adae, dataset_add = adex, by_vars = exprs(USUBJID), filter_join = ADY.join <= ADY, join_type = "all", join_vars = exprs(ADY), new_vars = exprs(CUMDOSE = sum(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE)), missing_values = exprs(CUMDOSE = 0) ) additional dataset. For example, if `join_type = "before"` is specified, only records before the current observation are selected. If `join_type = "after"` is specified, only records after the current observation are selected. To illustrate this, a variable (`SELECTED_DAYS`) is derived which contains the selected days. mydata <- tribble( ~DAY, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ) derive_vars_joined_summary( mydata, dataset_add = mydata, order = exprs(DAY), join_type = "before", new_vars = exprs(SELECTED_DAYS = paste(DAY, collapse = ", ")) ) derive_vars_joined_summary( mydata, dataset_add = mydata, order = exprs(DAY), join_type = "after", new_vars = exprs(SELECTED_DAYS = paste(DAY, collapse = ", ")) ) restrict the joined dataset to a certain range of records. For example, if `first_cond_lower` is specified, the joined dataset is restricted to the last observation before the current record where the condition is fulfilled. Please note: - If the condition is not fulfilled for any of the records, no records are selected. - The restriction implied by `join_type` is applied first. - If a variable is contained in both `dataset` and `dataset_add` like `DAY` in the example below, `DAY` refers to the value from `dataset` and `DAY.join` to the value from `dataset_add`. To illustrate this, a variable (`SELECTED_DAYS`) is derived which contains the selected days. derive_vars_joined_summary( mydata, dataset_add = mydata, order = exprs(DAY), join_type = "before", first_cond_lower = DAY.join == 2, new_vars = exprs(SELECTED_DAYS = paste(sort(DAY), collapse = ", ")) ) derive_vars_joined_summary( mydata, dataset_add = mydata, order = exprs(DAY), join_type = "after", first_cond_upper = DAY.join == 4, new_vars = exprs(SELECTED_DAYS = paste(DAY, collapse = ", ")) ) derive_vars_joined_summary( mydata, dataset_add = mydata, order = exprs(DAY), join_type = "all", first_cond_lower = DAY.join == 2, first_cond_upper = DAY.join == 4, new_vars = exprs(SELECTED_DAYS = paste(sort(DAY), collapse = ", ")) ) visit should be derived if at least three assessments are available. Please note that the condition for the number of assessments is specified in `new_vars` and not in `filter_join`. This is because the number of assessments within the week before the visit should be counted but not the number of assessments available for the subject. planned_visits <- tribble( ~AVISIT, ~ADY, "WEEK 1", 8, "WEEK 4", 29, "WEEK 8", 57 ) %>% mutate(USUBJID = "1", .before = AVISIT) adqs <- tribble( ~ADY, ~AVAL, 1, 10, 2, 12, 4, 9, 5, 9, 7, 10, 25, 11, 27, 10, 29, 10, 41, 8, 42, 9, 44, 5 ) %>% mutate(USUBJID = "1") derive_vars_joined_summary( planned_visits, dataset_add = adqs, by_vars = exprs(USUBJID), filter_join = ADY - 7 <= ADY.join & ADY.join < ADY, join_type = "all", join_vars = exprs(ADY), new_vars = exprs(AVAL = if_else(n() >= 3, mean(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE), NA)) ) Additional details: 1. The variables specified by `order` are added to the additional dataset (`dataset_add`). 1. The variables specified by `join_vars` are added to the additional dataset (`dataset_add`). 1. The records from the additional dataset (`dataset_add`) are restricted to those matching the `filter_add` condition. 1. The input dataset and the (restricted) additional dataset are left joined by the grouping variables (`by_vars`). If no grouping variables are specified, a full join is performed. 1. If `first_cond_lower` is specified, for each observation of the input dataset the joined dataset is restricted to observations from the first observation where `first_cond_lower` is fulfilled (the observation fulfilling the condition is included) up to the observation of the input dataset. If for an observation of the input dataset the condition is not fulfilled, the observation is removed. If `first_cond_upper` is specified, for each observation of the input dataset the joined dataset is restricted to observations up to the first observation where `first_cond_upper` is fulfilled (the observation fulfilling the condition is included). If for an observation of the input dataset the condition is not fulfilled, the observation is removed. For an example see the last example in the "Examples" section. 1. The joined dataset is restricted by the `filter_join` condition. 1. The variables specified for `new_vars` are created and merged to the input dataset. I.e., the output dataset contains all observations from the input dataset. For observations without a matching observation in the joined dataset the new variables are set as specified by `missing_values` (or to `NA` for variables not in `missing_values`). Observations in the additional dataset which have no matching observation in the input dataset are ignored. `r roxygen_save_memory()` Use admiral's derive_vars_joined_summary function with the following parameters: datase (t `r roxygen_param_dataset(expected_vars = c("by_vars"))`), dataset_add (Additional dataset The variables specified by the `by_vars`, the `new_vars`, the `join_vars`, and the `order` argument a.), by_vars (Grouping variables The two datasets are joined by the specified variables. `r roxygen_param_by_vars(rename = TRUE)`), order (Sort order The specified variables are used to determine the order of the records if `first_cond_lower` or `first_cond_u.), new_vars (Variables to add The new variables can be defined by named expressions, i.e.), tmp_obs_nr_var (Temporary observation number The specified variable is added to the input dataset (`dataset`) and the restricted additio.), join_vars (Variables to use from additional dataset Any extra variables required from the additional dataset for `filter_join` shou.), join_type (Observations to keep after joining The argument determines which of the joined observations are kept with respect to the.), first_cond_lower (Condition for selecting range of data (before) If this argument is specified, the other observations are restricted from.), first_cond_upper (Condition for selecting range of data (after) If this argument is specified, the other observations are restricted up to.), filter_join (Filter for the joined dataset The specified condition is applied to the joined dataset.), check_type (Check uniqueness? If `"message"`, `"warning"` or `"error"` is specified, the specified message is issued if the observat.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The output dataset contains all observations and variables of the input dataset and additionally the variables specified for `new_vars` derived from the additional dataset (`dataset_add`).. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects).
```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
library(readr)
library(rlang)

#---------------------------
# Helper readers
#---------------------------
read_single_value <- function(path, col) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if (!col %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Column ", col, " not found in ", path)
  }
  vals <- df[[col]]
  vals <- vals[!is.na(vals)]
  if (length(vals) == 0L) stop("No non-NA values in ", path)
  vals[1]
}

read_vector <- function(path, col) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if (!col %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Column ", col, " not found in ", path)
  }
  df[[col]]
}

#---------------------------
# Read main datasets
#---------------------------
dataset <- read_tsv("inputs/datase.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_add <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset_add.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

#---------------------------
# Read parameter files
#---------------------------
by_vars_chr   <- read_vector("inputs/by_vars.tsv", "by_vars")
order_vars_chr <- read_vector("inputs/order.tsv", "order")
join_vars_chr <- read_vector("inputs/join_vars.tsv", "join_vars")
tmp_obs_nr_chr <- read_vector("inputs/tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv", "tmp_obs_nr_var")

join_type_val <- read_single_value("inputs/join_type.tsv", "join_type")
check_type_val <- read_single_value("inputs/check_type.tsv", "check_type")

filter_join_txt <- read_single_value("inputs/filter_join.tsv", "filter")
first_cond_lower_txt <- read_single_value("inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv", "cond")
first_cond_upper_txt <- read_single_value("inputs/first_cond_upper.tsv", "cond")

#---------------------------
# Validate variables exist
#---------------------------
all_needed_vars <- unique(c(
  by_vars_chr,
  order_vars_chr,
  join_vars_chr,
  tmp_obs_nr_chr
))

missing_in_dataset <- setdiff(all_needed_vars, names(dataset))
missing_in_add <- setdiff(all_needed_vars, names(dataset_add))

if (length(missing_in_dataset) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset: ", paste(missing_in_dataset, collapse = ", "))
}
if (length(missing_in_add) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset_add: ", paste(missing_in_add, collapse = ", "))
}

#---------------------------
# Build quosures from text
#---------------------------
filter_join_expr <- parse_expr(filter_join_txt)
first_cond_lower_expr <- parse_expr(first_cond_lower_txt)
first_cond_upper_expr <- parse_expr(first_cond_upper_txt)

by_vars_syms   <- syms(by_vars_chr)
order_syms     <- syms(order_vars_chr)
join_vars_syms <- syms(join_vars_chr)

#---------------------------
# Define new_vars
# Example: summarize baseline (AVISITN == 0 in dataset_add) AVAL
# For illustration we compute mean and n of AVAL in the selected records
#---------------------------
new_vars_exprs <- exprs(
  AVBLN_SUM = sum(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
  AVBLN_MEAN = mean(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
  AVBLN_N = dplyr::n()
)

#---------------------------
# Run derive_vars_joined_summary
#---------------------------
res <- derive_vars_joined_summary(
  dataset = dataset,
  dataset_add = dataset_add,
  by_vars = !!!by_vars_syms,
  order = !!!order_syms,
  new_vars = new_vars_exprs,
  tmp_obs_nr_var = !!sym(tmp_obs_nr_chr[1]),
  join_vars = !!!join_vars_syms,
  join_type = join_type_val,
  first_cond_lower = !!first_cond_lower_expr,
  first_cond_upper = !!first_cond_upper_expr,
  filter_join = !!filter_join_expr,
  check_type = check_type_val
)

#---------------------------
# Save outputs
#---------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

out_csv <- "outputs/derived_joined_summary.csv"
out_rds <- "outputs/derived_joined_summary.rds"

write_csv(res, out_csv)
saveRDS(res, out_rds)

#---------------------------
# Basic validation / sanity checks
#---------------------------
stopifnot(file.exists(out_csv), file.exists(out_rds))

# Check for obvious alignment issues:
# 1) number of rows must equal input dataset
if (nrow(res) != nrow(dataset)) {
  stop("Row count mismatch between input dataset and result.")
}

# 2) Spot check: by_vars should be unchanged
if (!all(res[by_vars_chr] == dataset[by_vars_chr], na.rm = TRUE)) {
  stop("by_vars columns differ between input dataset and result.")
}

# 3) Check new variables for extreme values suggesting misalignment
num_summary <- res %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      c(AVBLN_SUM, AVBLN_MEAN, AVBLN_N),
      list(
        min = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        max = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        na = ~sum(is.na(.x))
      ),
      .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
    )
  )

print(num_summary)
```

library(admiral)
library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
library(readr)
library(rlang)

#---------------------------
# Helper readers
#---------------------------
read_single_value <- function(path, col) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if (!col %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Column ", col, " not found in ", path)
  }
  vals <- df[[col]]
  vals <- vals[!is.na(vals)]
  if (length(vals) == 0L) stop("No non-NA values in ", path)
  vals[1]
}

read_vector <- function(path, col) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if (!col %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Column ", col, " not found in ", path)
  }
  df[[col]]
}

#---------------------------
# Read main datasets
#---------------------------
dataset <- read_tsv("inputs/datase.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_add <- read_tsv("inputs/dataset_add.tsv", show_col_types = FALSE)

#---------------------------
# Read parameter files
#---------------------------
by_vars_chr   <- read_vector("inputs/by_vars.tsv", "by_vars")
order_vars_chr <- read_vector("inputs/order.tsv", "order")
join_vars_chr <- read_vector("inputs/join_vars.tsv", "join_vars")
tmp_obs_nr_chr <- read_vector("inputs/tmp_obs_nr_var.tsv", "tmp_obs_nr_var")

join_type_val <- read_single_value("inputs/join_type.tsv", "join_type")
check_type_val <- read_single_value("inputs/check_type.tsv", "check_type")

filter_join_txt <- read_single_value("inputs/filter_join.tsv", "filter")
first_cond_lower_txt <- read_single_value("inputs/first_cond_lower.tsv", "cond")
first_cond_upper_txt <- read_single_value("inputs/first_cond_upper.tsv", "cond")

#---------------------------
# Validate variables exist
#---------------------------
all_needed_vars <- unique(c(
  by_vars_chr,
  order_vars_chr,
  join_vars_chr,
  tmp_obs_nr_chr
))

missing_in_dataset <- setdiff(all_needed_vars, names(dataset))
missing_in_add <- setdiff(all_needed_vars, names(dataset_add))

if (length(missing_in_dataset) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset: ", paste(missing_in_dataset, collapse = ", "))
}
if (length(missing_in_add) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing variables in dataset_add: ", paste(missing_in_add, collapse = ", "))
}

#---------------------------
# Build quosures from text
#---------------------------
filter_join_expr <- parse_expr(filter_join_txt)
first_cond_lower_expr <- parse_expr(first_cond_lower_txt)
first_cond_upper_expr <- parse_expr(first_cond_upper_txt)

by_vars_syms   <- syms(by_vars_chr)
order_syms     <- syms(order_vars_chr)
join_vars_syms <- syms(join_vars_chr)

#---------------------------
# Define new_vars
# Example: summarize baseline (AVISITN == 0 in dataset_add) AVAL
# For illustration we compute mean and n of AVAL in the selected records
#---------------------------
new_vars_exprs <- exprs(
  AVBLN_SUM = sum(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
  AVBLN_MEAN = mean(AVAL, na.rm = TRUE),
  AVBLN_N = dplyr::n()
)

#---------------------------
# Run derive_vars_joined_summary
#---------------------------
res <- derive_vars_joined_summary(
  dataset = dataset,
  dataset_add = dataset_add,
  by_vars = !!!by_vars_syms,
  order = !!!order_syms,
  new_vars = new_vars_exprs,
  tmp_obs_nr_var = !!sym(tmp_obs_nr_chr[1]),
  join_vars = !!!join_vars_syms,
  join_type = join_type_val,
  first_cond_lower = !!first_cond_lower_expr,
  first_cond_upper = !!first_cond_upper_expr,
  filter_join = !!filter_join_expr,
  check_type = check_type_val
)

#---------------------------
# Save outputs
#---------------------------
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

out_csv <- "outputs/derived_joined_summary.csv"
out_rds <- "outputs/derived_joined_summary.rds"

write_csv(res, out_csv)
saveRDS(res, out_rds)

#---------------------------
# Basic validation / sanity checks
#---------------------------
stopifnot(file.exists(out_csv), file.exists(out_rds))

# Check for obvious alignment issues:
# 1) number of rows must equal input dataset
if (nrow(res) != nrow(dataset)) {
  stop("Row count mismatch between input dataset and result.")
}

# 2) Spot check: by_vars should be unchanged
if (!all(res[by_vars_chr] == dataset[by_vars_chr], na.rm = TRUE)) {
  stop("by_vars columns differ between input dataset and result.")
}

# 3) Check new variables for extreme values suggesting misalignment
num_summary <- res %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      c(AVBLN_SUM, AVBLN_MEAN, AVBLN_N),
      list(
        min = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        max = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
        na = ~sum(is.na(.x))
      ),
      .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
    )
  )

print(num_summary)

Write R code to derive query variables using admiral. At the beginning, load the required packages: library(admiral). The input data files are stored in inputs/ directory (inputs/dataset.tsv, inputs/dataset_queries.tsv). Additional details: `SMQzzNAM`, `SMQzzCD`, `SMQzzSC`, `SMQzzSCN`, and `CQzzNAM` in ADAE and ADMH, and variables such as `SDGzzNAM`, `SDGzzCD`, and `SDGzzSC` in ADCM. An example usage of this function can be found in the `vignette("occds")`. A query dataset is expected as an input to this function. See the `vignette("queries_dataset")` for descriptions, or call `data("queries")` for an example of a query dataset. For each unique element in `PREFIX`, the corresponding "NAM" variable will be created. For each unique `PREFIX`, if `GRPID` is not "" or NA, then the corresponding "CD" variable is created; similarly, if `SCOPE` is not "" or NA, then the corresponding "SC" variable will be created; if `SCOPEN` is not "" or NA, then the corresponding "SCN" variable will be created. For each record in `dataset`, the "NAM" variable takes the value of `GRPNAME` if the value of `TERMCHAR` or `TERMNUM` in `dataset_queries` matches the value of the respective SRCVAR in `dataset`. Note that `TERMCHAR` in `dataset_queries` dataset may be NA only when `TERMNUM` is non-NA and vice versa. The matching is case insensitive. The "CD", "SC", and "SCN" variables are derived accordingly based on `GRPID`, `SCOPE`, and `SCOPEN` respectively, whenever not missing. Use admiral's derive_vars_query function with the following parameters: dataset (`r roxygen_param_dataset()`), dataset_queries (A dataset containing required columns `PREFIX`, `GRPNAME`, `SRCVAR`, `TERMCHAR` and/or `TERMNUM`, and optional columns `.). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: The input dataset with query variables derived..
```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

#-----------------------------#
# Helper: safe file writer    #
#-----------------------------#

safe_write <- function(x, file_csv, file_rds = NULL) {
  tryCatch({
    write_csv(x, file_csv, na = "")
    if (!is.null(file_rds)) saveRDS(x, file_rds)
    # Read back CSV to confirm
    tmp <- read_csv(file_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
    if (!nrow(tmp)) {
      stop("Written file is empty: ", file_csv)
    }
    invisible(TRUE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    stop("Error writing or validating output file: ", conditionMessage(e))
  })
}

#-----------------------------#
# 1. Read input datasets      #
#-----------------------------#

dataset_path          <- file.path("inputs", "dataset.tsv")
dataset_queries_path  <- file.path("inputs", "dataset_queries.tsv")

dataset <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(dataset_path, show_col_types = FALSE),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error reading dataset.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

dataset_queries_raw <- tryCatch(
  read_tsv(dataset_queries_path, show_col_types = FALSE),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error reading dataset_queries.tsv: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

#-----------------------------#
# 2. Build a queries dataset  #
#    for derive_vars_query    #
#-----------------------------#
# We will create a simple query definition:
# - PREFIX: "SDG" (typical for SDTM type groupings)
# - GRPNAME: use TERMCHAR (copied from SRCVAR) so each variable
#            is its own "group" name
# - SRCVAR: the analysis variable in 'dataset' to match against
#           (use AVISITN as an example of numeric TERMNUM and
#            AVAL as example of TERMCHAR)
# NOTE: Adapt this to project-specific query definitions as needed.

# Example: create one set of TERMCHAR-based queries on PARAMCD,
# and another TERMNUM-based queries on AVISITN

queries_char <- dataset_queries_raw %>%
  transmute(
    PREFIX   = "SDG",
    GRPNAME  = PARAMCD,
    SRCVAR   = "PARAMCD",
    TERMCHAR = PARAMCD,
    TERMNUM  = NA_real_
  )

queries_num <- dataset_queries_raw %>%
  transmute(
    PREFIX   = "SDG",
    GRPNAME  = paste0(PARAMCD, "_VISIT", AVISITN),
    SRCVAR   = "AVISITN",
    TERMCHAR = NA_character_,
    TERMNUM  = AVISITN
  )

queries <- bind_rows(queries_char, queries_num) %>%
  distinct()

# Basic validation of queries dataset
required_qvars <- c("PREFIX", "GRPNAME", "SRCVAR", "TERMCHAR", "TERMNUM")
missing_qvars <- setdiff(required_qvars, names(queries))
if (length(missing_qvars) > 0) {
  stop("Queries dataset missing required columns: ",
       paste(missing_qvars, collapse = ", "))
}

if (any(is.na(queries$TERMCHAR) & is.na(queries$TERMNUM))) {
  stop("Queries must have either TERMCHAR or TERMNUM non-missing for each row.")
}

#-----------------------------#
# 3. Derive query variables   #
#-----------------------------#

# Case-insensitive matching is handled within admiral's
# derive_vars_query implementation.

adsl_like <- dataset

adsl_with_queries <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_query(
    dataset         = adsl_like,
    dataset_queries = queries
  ),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_vars_query: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

#-----------------------------#
# 4. Basic data checks        #
#-----------------------------#

if (!nrow(adsl_with_queries)) {
  stop("Resulting dataset after derive_vars_query has zero rows.")
}

# Check for obvious misalignment: for NAM variables created by PREFIX "SDG"
sdg_nam_vars <- grep("^SDG.*NAM$", names(adsl_with_queries), value = TRUE)

if (length(sdg_nam_vars) == 0) {
  warning("No SDG*NAM variables were created. Check queries and mapping.")
}

# Quick pattern check: look for all-NA query variables
all_na_qvars <- sapply(adsl_with_queries[sdg_nam_vars], function(x) all(is.na(x)))
if (any(all_na_qvars)) {
  warning(
    "Some query-derived variables are entirely NA: ",
    paste(names(all_na_qvars)[all_na_qvars], collapse = ", ")
  )
}

#-----------------------------#
# 5. Write outputs            #
#-----------------------------#

dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE)

safe_write(
  adsl_with_queries,
  file_csv = file.path("outputs", "dataset_with_queries.csv"),
  file_rds = file.path("outputs", "dataset_with_queries.rds")
)
```

library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

#-----------------------------#
# Helper: safe file writer    #
#-----------------------------#

safe_write <- function(x, file_csv, file_rds = NULL) {
  tryCatch({
    write_csv(x, file_csv, na = "")

    # Read back CSV to confirm
    tmp <- read_csv(file_csv, show_col_types = FALSE)
    if (!nrow(tmp)) {
      stop("Written file is empty: ", file_csv)
    }
    invisible(TRUE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    stop("Error writing or validating output file: ", conditionMessage(e))
  })
}

#-----------------------------#
# 1. Read input datasets      #
#-----------------------------#

dataset_path          <- file.path("inputs", "dataset.tsv")
dataset_queries_path  <- file.path("inputs", "dataset_queries.tsv")

  read_tsv(dataset_path, show_col_types = FALSE),

#            is its own "group" name

#            AVAL as example of TERMCHAR)

# and another TERMNUM-based queries on AVISITN

queries_char <- dataset_queries_raw %>%
  transmute(
    PREFIX   = "SDG",

  )

queries <- bind_rows(queries_char, queries_num) %>%
  distinct()

# Basic validation of queries dataset

if (length(missing_qvars) > 0) {

if (any(is.na(queries$TERMCHAR) & is.na(queries$TERMNUM))) {

}

#-----------------------------#

# Case-insensitive matching is handled within admiral's
# derive_vars_query implementation.

adsl_like <- dataset

adsl_with_queries <- tryCatch(
  derive_vars_query(
    dataset         = adsl_like,

  ),
  error = function(e) {
    stop("Error in derive_vars_query: ", conditionMessage(e))
  }
)

#-----------------------------#
# 4. Basic data checks        #
#-----------------------------#

if (!nrow(adsl_with_queries)) {
  stop("Resulting dataset after derive_vars_query has zero rows.")
}

# Check for obvious misalignment: for NAM variables created by PREFIX "SDG"
sdg_nam_vars <- grep("^SDG.*NAM$", names(adsl_with_queries), value = TRUE)

if (length(sdg_nam_vars) == 0) {
  warning("No SDG*NAM variables were created. Check queries and mapping.")
}

# Quick pattern check: look for all-NA query variables
all_na_qvars <- sapply(adsl_with_queries[sdg_nam_vars], function(x) all(is.na(x)))
if (any(all_na_qvars)) {
  warning(
    "Some query-derived variables are entirely NA: ",

  )
}

#-----------------------------#
# 5. Write outputs            #
#-----------------------------#

dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE)

safe_write(

```r
library(admiral)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(rlang)
library(purrr)

#---- Helper functions ----

read_single_value <- function(path, colname) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if (!colname %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Expected column `", colname, "` in file ", path, call. = FALSE)
  }
  vals <- na.omit(df[[colname]])
  if (length(vals) != 1L) {
    stop("File ", path, " must contain exactly one non-missing value in column `",
         colname, "`; got ", length(vals), call. = FALSE)
  }
  vals[[1]]
}

read_vector <- function(path, colname) {
  df <- read_tsv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if (!colname %in% names(df)) {
    stop("Expected column `", colname, "` in file ", path, call. = FALSE)
  }
  unique(na.omit(df[[colname]]))
}

#---- Read input datasets ----

dataset_path        <- file.path("inputs", "datase.tsv")
dataset_merge_path  <- file.path("inputs", "dataset_merge.tsv")
by_vars_path        <- file.path("inputs", "by_vars.tsv")
id_vars_path        <- file.path("inputs", "id_vars.tsv")
key_var_path        <- file.path("inputs", "key_var.tsv")
value_var_path      <- file.path("inputs", "value_var.tsv")
filter_path         <- file.path("inputs", "filter.tsv")
relationship_path   <- file.path("inputs", "relationship.tsv")

dataset <- read_tsv(dataset_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
dataset_merge <- read_tsv(dataset_merge_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

by_vars_chr   <- read_vector(by_vars_path,   "by_vars")
id_vars_chr   <- read_vector(id_vars_path,   "id_vars")
key_var_chr   <- read_single_value(key_var_path,   "key_var")
value_var_chr <- read_single_value(value_var_path, "value_var")

# filter and relationship are textual specifications; allow possible absence
filter_expr_txt <- NULL
if (file.exists(filter_path)) {
  filter_tbl <- read_tsv(filter_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if ("filter" %in% names(filter_tbl)) {
    filter_vals <- na.omit(filter_tbl[["filter"]])
    if (length(filter_vals) > 0L) {
      filter_expr_txt <- paste(filter_vals, collapse = " & ")
    }
  }
}

relationship_chr <- NULL
if (file.exists(relationship_path)) {
  rel_tbl <- read_tsv(relationship_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  if ("relationship" %in% names(rel_tbl)) {
    rel_vals <- na.omit(rel_tbl[["relationship"]])
    if (length(rel_vals) > 0L) {
      # if exactly one relationship string, use it; otherwise treat as invalid
      if (length(rel_vals) == 1L) {
        relationship_chr <- rel_vals[[1]]
      } else {
        stop("`relationship.tsv` must contain exactly one non-missing value.", call. = FALSE)
      }
    }
  }
}

# Safety: ensure required columns exist in datasets
required_in_merge <- unique(c(by_vars_chr, id_vars_chr, key_var_chr, value_var_chr))
missing_in_merge <- setdiff(required_in_merge, names(dataset_merge))
if (length(missing_in_merge) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing required columns in dataset_merge: ",
       paste(missing_in_merge, collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

missing_in_dataset <- setdiff(by_vars_chr, names(dataset))
if (length(missing_in_dataset) > 0L) {
  stop("Missing required by_vars columns in dataset: ",
       paste(missing_in_dataset, collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

# Build quosures for by_vars and id_vars
by_vars_syms <- syms(by_vars_chr)
id_vars_syms <- syms(id_vars_chr)

# Filter expression
filter_expr <- if (!is.null(filter_expr_txt) && nzchar(filter_expr_txt)) {
  parse_expr(filter_expr_txt)
} else {
  NULL
}

# Relationship: if not provided or invalid, set to NULL
valid_relationships <- c(
  "one-to-one", "one-to-many", "many-to-one",
  "many-to-many", "one-to-one/one-to-many",
  "one-to-one/many-to-one"
)
if (!is.null(relationship_chr)) {
  if (!relationship_chr %in% valid_relationships) {
    stop("Invalid relationship specified: ", relationship_chr,
         ". Must be one of: ", paste(valid_relationships, collapse = ", "),
         call. = FALSE)
  }
  relationship_arg <- relationship_chr
} else {
  relationship_arg <- NULL
}

#---- Validate uniqueness in dataset_merge before transpose ----

# Per requirements: uniquely identified by by_vars, id_vars, key_var
id_check_vars <- c(by_vars_chr, id_vars_chr, key_var_chr)
id_check_vars <- unique(id_check_vars)

dup_groups <- dataset_merge %>%
  group_by(across(all_of(id_check_vars))) %>%
  filter(n() > 1L) %>%
  ungroup()

if (nrow(dup_groups) > 0L) {
  stop(
    "dataset_merge has duplicate records for the combination of ",
    paste(id_check_vars, collapse = ", "),
    ". Number of duplicate rows: ", nrow(dup_groups),
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

#---- Derive transposed variables and merge ----

result <- derive_vars_transposed(
  dataset        = dataset,
  dataset_merge  = dataset_merge,
  by_vars        = exprs(!!!by_vars_syms),
  id_vars        = exprs(!!!id_vars_syms),
  key_var        = !!sym(key_var_chr),
  value_var      = !!sym(value_var_chr),
  filter         = !!filter_expr,
  relationship   = relationship_arg
)

#---- Basic diagnostics / validation ----

if (!is.data.frame(result)) {
  stop("Result object is not a data.frame; got class: ",
       paste(class(result), collapse = ", "), call. = FALSE)
}

# Check for unexpected NA inflation in by_vars
orig_by_complete <- dataset %>%
  select(all_of(by_vars_chr)) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(.flag = "orig")

res_by_complete <- result %>%
  select(all_of(by_vars_chr)) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  mutate(.flag = "res")

by_compare <- bind_rows(orig_by_complete, res_by_complete)

# Warn if new by_vars combinations appeared and relationship does not allow it
new_combos <- anti_join(
  res_by_complete %>% select(-.flag),
  orig_by_complete %>% select(-.flag),
  by = by_vars_chr
)
if (nrow(new_combos) > 0L && !identical(relationship_arg, "many-to-many")) {
  warning(
    "New by_vars combinations were introduced in result but relationship is not 'many-to-many'.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

# Check for completely duplicated rows (potential misalignment)
dup_rows <- result %>%
  mutate(.row_id = row_number()) %>%
  add_count(across(everything()), name = ".n_dup") %>%
  filter(.n_dup > 1L)
if (nrow(dup_rows) > 0L) {
  warning("Result contains ", nrow(dup_rows),
          " rows that are part of duplicate-rows groups.", call. = FALSE)
}

#---- Write outputs ----

dir.create("outputs", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

out_csv  <- file.path("outputs", "derived_transposed.csv")
out_rds  <- file.path("outputs", "derived_transposed.rds")

write_csv(result, out_csv, na = "")
saveRDS(result, out_rds)

# Confirm files written and readable
stopifnot(file.exists(out_csv), file.exists(out_rds))

# Simple read-back sanity check
chk_df  <- suppressMessages(read_csv(out_csv, show_col_types = FALSE))
chk_rds <- readRDS(out_rds)

if (!is.data.frame(chk_df) || !is.data.frame(chk_rds)) {
  stop("Output files were written but could not be read back as data.frames.", call. = FALSE)
}

invisible(result)
```
