```r
library(admiral)

# Directories
in_dir  <- "inputs"
out_dir <- "outputs"

if (!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

#-----------------------------
# Safe readers with validation
#-----------------------------
read_single_column <- function(path, col_name_expected) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop(sprintf("File not found: %s", path))
  }
  df <- tryCatch(
    read.csv(path, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) stop(sprintf("Failed to read %s: %s", path, e$message))
  )
  if (!is.data.frame(df) || ncol(df) != 1L) {
    stop(sprintf("File %s must contain exactly one column.", path))
  }
  if (!identical(names(df), col_name_expected)) {
    stop(sprintf("File %s must have a single column named '%s', found: %s",
                 path, col_name_expected, paste(names(df), collapse = ", ")))
  }
  if (anyNA(df[[1]])) {
    stop(sprintf("File %s contains NA values in column '%s'.", path, col_name_expected))
  }
  df[[1]]
}

#-----------------------------
# Read and validate inputs
#-----------------------------
list_path   <- file.path(in_dir, "list.tsv")
indent_path <- file.path(in_dir, "indent.tsv")

list_vals   <- read_single_column(list_path,   "list")
indent_vals <- read_single_column(indent_path, "indent")

# Validate that both vectors are identical; otherwise, warn of possible misalignment
if (!identical(list_vals, indent_vals)) {
  warning("Values in list.tsv and indent.tsv differ; data may be misaligned.")
}

# Construct a named list from the 'list' values
# Use the values as both names and elements (example construction)
named_list <- as.list(list_vals)
names(named_list) <- list_vals

#-----------------------------
# Derive indent value
#-----------------------------
# Using the number of rows in indent.tsv as indent width
indent_width <- length(indent_vals)

if (!is.numeric(indent_width) || length(indent_width) != 1L || indent_width < 0) {
  stop("Derived indent width is invalid.")
}

#-----------------------------
# Use admiral::print_named_list
#-----------------------------
# Capture output to check for issues and then print
print_output <- capture.output(
  admiral::print_named_list(
    list   = named_list,
    indent = indent_width
  )
)

# Also print to console (side effect as intended)
cat(paste0(print_output, collapse = "\n"), "\n")

#-----------------------------
# Save outputs
#-----------------------------

# 1) Save the named list (complex object) as RDS
rds_path <- file.path(out_dir, "named_list.rds")
saveRDS(named_list, rds_path)

# 2) Save the printed representation as a data frame to CSV
print_df <- data.frame(
  line_number = seq_along(print_output),
  text        = print_output,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

csv_path <- file.path(out_dir, "print_named_list_output.csv")
write.csv(print_df, csv_path, row.names = FALSE)

#-----------------------------
# Post-write verification
#-----------------------------
# Check that files exist and are readable
if (!file.exists(rds_path)) {
  stop("RDS output file was not created.")
}
if (!file.exists(csv_path)) {
  stop("CSV output file was not created.")
}

# Re-load and perform simple integrity checks
reloaded_list <- tryCatch(
  readRDS(rds_path),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to read back RDS file: ", e$message)
)

if (!is.list(reloaded_list)) {
  stop("Reloaded RDS object is not a list; possible corruption or save error.")
}

reloaded_csv <- tryCatch(
  read.csv(csv_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE),
  error = function(e) stop("Failed to read back CSV file: ", e$message)
)

if (!all(c("line_number", "text") %in% names(reloaded_csv))) {
  stop("Reloaded CSV does not have expected columns; possible misalignment.")
}

# Basic pattern checks for unexpected issues
if (anyNA(reloaded_csv$line_number) || anyNA(reloaded_csv$text)) {
  warning("NA values detected in reloaded CSV; possible parsing or write issues.")
}

# Heuristic check: look for obviously malformed indentation (e.g., tabs instead of spaces)
if (any(grepl("\t", reloaded_csv$text))) {
  warning("Tab characters detected in printed output; indentation may not match expectations.")
}
```

```r
library(admiraldev)

# Create outputs directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-------------------------------
# Read and preprocess inputs
#-------------------------------

arg_df <- read.delim(
  file = "inputs/arg.tsv",
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  na.strings = c("", "NA")
)

optional_df <- read.delim(
  file = "inputs/optional.tsv",
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  na.strings = c("", "NA")
)

# Basic validation
stopifnot(
  "arg" %in% names(arg_df),
  "optional" %in% names(optional_df)
)

#-------------------------------
# Prepare vectors for checking
#-------------------------------

arg_vec_raw <- arg_df$arg

# Try to parse as Date first, then POSIXct; keep as character if parsing fails
arg_date <- suppressWarnings(as.Date(arg_vec_raw))
arg_posix <- suppressWarnings(as.POSIXct(arg_vec_raw, tz = "UTC"))

# A simple heuristic: if at least one value parses as Date or POSIXct, keep that type
if (any(!is.na(arg_date))) {
  arg_vec <- arg_date
} else if (any(!is.na(arg_posix))) {
  arg_vec <- arg_posix
} else {
  arg_vec <- arg_vec_raw
}

# For `optional`, interpret any non-empty value as TRUE
optional_vec_raw <- optional_df$optional
optional_flag <- any(!is.na(optional_vec_raw) & nzchar(optional_vec_raw))

#-------------------------------
# Run admiraldev::assert_date_vector
#-------------------------------

res <- try(
  admiraldev::assert_date_vector(
    arg = arg_vec,
    optional = optional_flag
  ),
  silent = TRUE
)

# Capture result info
result_list <- list(
  arg_class = class(arg_vec),
  arg_str = utils::capture.output(str(arg_vec)),
  optional_flag = optional_flag,
  assert_result_class = class(res),
  assert_result_is_error = inherits(res, "try-error"),
  assert_result_message = if (inherits(res, "try-error")) as.character(res) else NA_character_
)

#-------------------------------
# Save outputs
#-------------------------------

# Save result_list as RDS
saveRDS(result_list, file = "outputs/assert_date_vector_result.rds")

# If arg_vec is atomic, wrap in data.frame for CSV; otherwise save as RDS
if (is.atomic(arg_vec)) {
  arg_out_df <- data.frame(arg = arg_vec, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  write.csv(arg_out_df, file = "outputs/arg_checked.csv", row.names = FALSE)
} else {
  saveRDS(arg_vec, file = "outputs/arg_checked.rds")
}

# Save optional information
optional_out_df <- data.frame(
  optional_flag = optional_flag,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
write.csv(optional_out_df, file = "outputs/optional_flag.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#-------------------------------
# Post-write checks
#-------------------------------

# Re-read written files to confirm integrity
arg_checked_exists_csv <- file.exists("outputs/arg_checked.csv")
arg_checked_exists_rds <- file.exists("outputs/arg_checked.rds")
result_rds_exists <- file.exists("outputs/assert_date_vector_result.rds")
optional_flag_exists <- file.exists("outputs/optional_flag.csv")

if (!result_rds_exists || !optional_flag_exists || (!arg_checked_exists_csv && !arg_checked_exists_rds)) {
  stop("One or more output files were not written correctly.")
}

# Simple pattern/misalignment checks
result_list_check <- readRDS("outputs/assert_date_vector_result.rds")
if (!is.list(result_list_check) ||
    !all(c("arg_class", "optional_flag", "assert_result_class") %in% names(result_list_check))) {
  stop("assert_date_vector_result.rds structure is not as expected.")
}

if (arg_checked_exists_csv) {
  arg_checked_back <- read.csv("outputs/arg_checked.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (!"arg" %in% names(arg_checked_back)) {
    stop("arg_checked.csv does not contain expected column 'arg'.")
  }
}

if (optional_flag_exists) {
  optional_back <- read.csv("outputs/optional_flag.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (!"optional_flag" %in% names(optional_back)) {
    stop("optional_flag.csv does not contain expected column 'optional_flag'.")
  }
}
```
solution:
library(admiraldev)

# Create outputs directory if it does not exist
if (!dir.exists("outputs")) dir.create("outputs", recursive = TRUE)

#-------------------------------
# Read and preprocess inputs
#-------------------------------

arg_df <- read.delim(
  file = "inputs/arg.tsv",
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  na.strings = c("", "NA")
)

optional_df <- read.delim(
  file = "inputs/optional.tsv",
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  na.strings = c("", "NA")
)

# Basic validation
stopifnot(
  "arg" %in% names(arg_df),
  "optional" %in% names(optional_df)
)

#-------------------------------
# Prepare vectors for checking
#-------------------------------

arg_vec_raw <- arg_df$arg

# Try to parse as Date first, then POSIXct; keep as character if parsing fails
arg_date <- suppressWarnings(as.Date(arg_vec_raw))
arg_posix <- suppressWarnings(as.POSIXct(arg_vec_raw, tz = "UTC"))

# A simple heuristic: if at least one value parses as Date or POSIXct, keep that type
if (any(!is.na(arg_date))) {
  arg_vec <- arg_date
} else if (any(!is.na(arg_posix))) {
  arg_vec <- arg_posix
} else {
  arg_vec <- arg_vec_raw
}

# For `optional`, interpret any non-empty value as TRUE
optional_vec_raw <- optional_df$optional
optional_flag <- any(!is.na(optional_vec_raw) & nzchar(optional_vec_raw))

#-------------------------------
# Run admiraldev::assert_date_vector
#-------------------------------

res <- try(
  admiraldev::assert_date_vector(
    arg = arg_vec,
    optional = optional_flag
  ),
  silent = TRUE
)

# Capture result info
result_list <- list(
  arg_class = class(arg_vec),
  arg_str = utils::capture.output(str(arg_vec)),
  optional_flag = optional_flag,
  assert_result_class = class(res),
  assert_result_is_error = inherits(res, "try-error"),
  assert_result_message = if (inherits(res, "try-error")) as.character(res) else NA_character_
)

#-------------------------------
# Save outputs
#-------------------------------

# Save result_list as RDS
saveRDS(result_list, file = "outputs/assert_date_vector_result.rds")

# If arg_vec is atomic, wrap in data.frame for CSV; otherwise save as RDS
if (is.atomic(arg_vec)) {
  arg_out_df <- data.frame(arg = arg_vec, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  write.csv(arg_out_df, file = "outputs/arg_checked.csv", row.names = FALSE)
} else {
  saveRDS(arg_vec, file = "outputs/arg_checked.rds")
}

# Save optional information
optional_out_df <- data.frame(
  optional_flag = optional_flag,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
write.csv(optional_out_df, file = "outputs/optional_flag.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#-------------------------------
# Post-write checks
#-------------------------------

# Re-read written files to confirm integrity
arg_checked_exists_csv <- file.exists("outputs/arg_checked.csv")
arg_checked_exists_rds <- file.exists("outputs/arg_checked.rds")
result_rds_exists <- file.exists("outputs/assert_date_vector_result.rds")
optional_flag_exists <- file.exists("outputs/optional_flag.csv")

if (!result_rds_exists || !optional_flag_exists || (!arg_checked_exists_csv && !arg_checked_exists_rds)) {
  stop("One or more output files were not written correctly.")
}

# Simple pattern/misalignment checks
result_list_check <- readRDS("outputs/assert_date_vector_result.rds")
if (!is.list(result_list_check) ||
    !all(c("arg_class", "optional_flag", "assert_result_class") %in% names(result_list_check))) {
  stop("assert_date_vector_result.rds structure is not as expected.")
}

if (arg_checked_exists_csv) {
  arg_checked_back <- read.csv("outputs/arg_checked.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (!"arg" %in% names(arg_checked_back)) {
    stop("arg_checked.csv does not contain expected column 'arg'.")
  }
}

if (optional_flag_exists) {
  optional_back <- read.csv("outputs/optional_flag.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (!"optional_flag" %in% names(optional_back)) {
    stop("optional_flag.csv does not contain expected column 'optional_flag'.")
  }
}

```r
# Load required package
library(admiraldev)

# Set paths
input_path  <- "inputs/x.tsv"
output_path <- "outputs/x_dquoted.csv"

# Error handling wrapper
safe_run <- function() {
  if (!file.exists(input_path)) {
    stop("Input file does not exist: ", input_path)
  }

  # Read input; expect a single character column "x"
  x_df <- tryCatch(
    utils::read.table(
      input_path,
      header = TRUE,
      sep = "\t",
      quote = "",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
      check.names = FALSE
    ),
    error = function(e) stop("Failed to read input file: ", conditionMessage(e))
  )

  # Basic validation
  if (!"x" %in% names(x_df)) {
    stop("Input data must contain a column named 'x'.")
  }
  if (!is.character(x_df[["x"]])) {
    x_df[["x"]] <- as.character(x_df[["x"]])
  }

  # Apply admiraldev::dquote to the character vector
  x_vec <- x_df[["x"]]
  dq_vec <- admiraldev::dquote(x_vec)

  # Prepare output data frame
  out_df <- data.frame(
    x_original = x_vec,
    x_dquoted  = dq_vec,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Write output as CSV
  tryCatch(
    utils::write.csv(
      out_df,
      file = output_path,
      row.names = FALSE,
      na = ""
    ),
    error = function(e) stop("Failed to write output CSV: ", conditionMessage(e))
  )

  # Confirm output written correctly
  if (!file.exists(output_path)) {
    stop("Output file was not created: ", output_path)
  }

  # Re-read and basic sanity checks for misalignment/parsing issues
  check_df <- tryCatch(
    utils::read.csv(
      output_path,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
      check.names = FALSE
    ),
    error = function(e) stop("Failed to re-read output CSV: ", conditionMessage(e))
  )

  # Structural checks
  if (!all(c("x_original", "x_dquoted") %in% names(check_df))) {
    stop("Output file structure is not as expected.")
  }
  if (nrow(check_df) != nrow(out_df)) {
    stop("Row count mismatch between in-memory and on-disk output.")
  }

  # Check for unexpected patterns: x_dquoted should all start and end with double quotes
  bad_pattern_idx <- which(
    !is.na(check_df$x_dquoted) &
      !(grepl('^"', check_df$x_dquoted) & grepl('"$', check_df$x_dquoted))
  )
  if (length(bad_pattern_idx) > 0) {
    stop("Unexpected pattern in x_dquoted column at rows: ",
         paste(bad_pattern_idx, collapse = ", "))
  }

  invisible(TRUE)
}

safe_run()
```

[problems()] is a companion helper function to [create_iso8601()]. It retrieves ISO 8601 parsing problems from an object of class iso8601, which is [create_iso8601()]'s return value and that might contain a `problems` attribute in case of parsing failures. [problems()] is a helper function that provides easy access to these parsing problems. Use sdtm.oak's problems function with the following parameters: x (An object of class iso8601, as typically obtained from a call to [create_iso8601()].). Ensure that you carefully read and process the input data according to the parameter requirements. The function returns: s If there are no parsing problems in `x`, then the returned value is `NULL`; otherwise, a [tibble][tibble::tibble-package] of parsing failures is returned. Each row corresponds to a parsing problem. There will be a first column named `..i` indicating the position(s) in the inputs to the [create_iso8601()] call that resulted in failures; remaining columns correspond to the original input values passed on to [create_iso8601()], with columns being automatically named `..var1`, `..var2`, and so on, if the inputs to [create_iso8601()] were unnamed, otherwise, the original variable names are used instead.. After calling the function, save the result to outputs/ directory as appropriate file format (CSV for data frames, RDS for complex objects).
